Exodus21:32 But if the ox gores a slave

Exodus34:34 but whenever he went into the Tabernacle to speak with the Lord
Exodus34:35 and the people would see his face aglow. Afterwards he would put the veil on again until he returned to speak with God.
Exodus35:1 Now Moses called a meeting of all the people and told them
Exodus35:2 “Work six days only; the seventh day is a day of solemn rest
Exodus35:3 Don't even light the fires in your homes that day.“

Exodus35:26 Some other women gladly used their special skill to spin the goats' hair into cloth.

Exodus35:28 and spices
Exodus35:29 So the people of Israel—every man and woman who wanted to assist in the work given to them by the Lord's command to Moses—brought their freewill offerings to him.
Exodus35:30 And Moses told them
Exodus35:31 grandson of Hur of the tribe of Judah) as general superintendent of the project.
Exodus35:32 He will be able to create beautiful workmanship from gold
Exodus35:33 he can cut and set stones like a jeweler and can do beautiful carving; in fact
Exodus35:34 And God has made him and Oholiab gifted teachers of their skills to others. (Oholiab is the son of Ahisamach
Exodus35:35 God has filled them both with unusual skills as jewelers
Exodus36:1 “All the other craftsmen with God-given abilities are to assist Bezalel and Oholiab in constructing and furnishing the Tabernacle.“
Exodus36:2 So Moses told Bezalel and Oholiab and all others who felt called to the work to begin.
Exodus36:3 Moses gave them the materials donated by the people and additional gifts were received each morning.
Exodus36:4 But finally the workmen all left their task
Exodus36:5 to meet with Moses and told him
Exodus36:6 So Moses sent a message throughout the camp announcing that no more donations were needed.
Exodus36:7 Then at last the people were restrained from bringing more!
Exodus36:8 The skilled weavers first made ten sheets from fine linen
Exodus36:9 Each sheet was 42 feet long and 6 feet wide.
Exodus36:10 Five of these sheets were attached end to end
Exodus36:11 Fifty blue ribbons were looped along the edges of these two long
Exodus36:12 sheets
Exodus36:13 Then fifty clasps of gold were made to connect the loops
Exodus36:14 Above the ceiling was a second layer formed by eleven draperies
Exodus36:15 made of goats' hair (uniformly 45 feet long and 6 feet wide).
Exodus36:16 Bezalel coupled five of these draperies together to make one long piece
Exodus36:17 Then he made fifty loops along the end of each
Exodus36:18 and fifty small bronze clasps to couple the loops so that the draperies were firmly attached to each other.
Exodus36:19 The top layer of the roof was made of rams' skins
Exodus36:20 For the sides of the Tabernacle he used frames of acacia wood standing on end.
Exodus36:21 The height of each frame was 15 feet and the width 2 1/4 feet.
Exodus36:22 Each frame had two clasps joining it to the next.
Exodus36:23 There were twenty frames on the south side
Exodus36:24 with the bottoms fitting into forty silver bases. Each frame was connected to its base by two clasps.
Exodus36:25 There were also twenty frames on the north side of
Exodus36:26 the Tabernacle
Exodus36:27 The west side of the Tabernacle
Exodus36:28 plus another at each corner.
Exodus36:29 These frames
Exodus36:30 So
Exodus36:31 Then he made five sets of bars from acacia wood to tie
Exodus36:32 the frames together along the sides
Exodus36:33 The middle bar of the five was halfway up the frames
Exodus36:34 The frames and bars were all overlaid with gold
Exodus36:35 The blue
Exodus36:36 The curtain was then attached to four gold hooks set into four posts of acacia wood
Exodus36:37 Then he made a drapery for the entrance to the Tabernacle; it was woven from finespun linen
Exodus36:38 This drapery was connected by five hooks to five posts. The posts and their capitals and rods were overlaid with gold; their five bases were molded from bronze.
Exodus37:1 Next Bezalel made the Ark. This was constructed of acacia wood and was 3 3/4 feet long
Exodus37:2 It was plated with pure gold inside and out
Exodus37:3 There were four gold rings fastened into its four feet
Exodus37:4 Then he made poles from acacia wood
Exodus37:5 and put the poles into the rings at the sides of the Ark
Exodus37:6 Then
Exodus37:7 He made two statues of Guardian Angels of beaten gold and placed them at the two ends of the gold lid.
Exodus37:8 They were molded so that they were actually a part of the gold lid—it was all one piece.
Exodus37:9 The Guardian Angels faced each other
Exodus37:10 Then he made a table
Exodus37:11 It was overlaid with pure gold
Exodus37:12 A rim 4 inches high was constructed around the edges of the table
Exodus37:13 Then he cast four rings of gold and placed them into the four table legs
Exodus37:14 close to the molding
Exodus37:15 He made the carrying poles of acacia wood covered with gold.
Exodus37:16 Next
Exodus37:17 Then he made the lampstand
Exodus37:18 The lampstand had six branches
Exodus37:19 Each of the branches was decorated with identical carvings of blossoms.
Exodus37:20 The main stem of the lampstand was similarly decorated with almond blossoms
Exodus37:21 a flower on the stem beneath each pair of branches; also a flower below the bottom pair and above the top pair
Exodus37:22 The decorations and branches were all one piece of pure
Exodus37:23 Then he made the seven lamps at the ends of the branches
Exodus37:24 The entire lampstand weighed 107 pounds
Exodus37:25 The incense altar was made of acacia wood. It was 18 inches square and 3 feet high
Exodus37:26 He overlaid it all with pure gold and ran a gold molding around the edge.
Exodus37:27 Two gold rings were placed on each side
Exodus37:28 The carrying poles were gold-plated acacia wood.
Exodus37:29 Then
Exodus38:1 The burnt-offering altar was also constructed of acacia wood; it was 7 1/2 feet square at the top
Exodus38:2 There were four horns at the four corners
Exodus38:3 Then he made bronze utensils to be used with the altar—the pots
Exodus38:4 Next he made a bronze grating that rested upon a ledge about halfway up in the firebox.
Exodus38:5 Four rings were cast for each side of the grating
Exodus38:6 The carrying poles themselves were made of acacia wood
Exodus38:7 The carrying poles were inserted into the rings at the side of the altar. The altar was hollow
Exodus38:8 The bronze washbasin and its bronze pedestal were cast from the solid bronze mirrors donated by the women who assembled at the entrance to the Tabernacle.
Exodus38:9 Then he constructed the courtyard. The south wall was 150 feet long; it consisted of drapes woven from fine-twined linen thread.
Exodus38:10 There were twenty posts to hold drapes
Exodus38:11 The north wall was also 150 feet long
Exodus38:12 The west side was 75 feet wide; the walls were made from drapes supported by ten posts and bases
Exodus38:13 The east side was also 75 feet wide.
Exodus38:14 The drapes at either side of the entrance were 22
Exodus38:15 1/2 feet wide
Exodus38:16 All the drapes making up the walls of the court were woven of fine-twined linen.
Exodus38:17 Each post had a bronze base
Exodus38:18 The drapery covering the entrance to the court was made of fine-twined linen
Exodus38:19 It was supported by four posts
Exodus38:20 All the nails used in constructing the Tabernacle and court were bronze.
Exodus38:21 This summarizes the various steps in building the Tabernacle to house the Ark
Exodus38:22 Bezalel (son of Uri and grandson of Hur
Exodus38:23 assisted by Oholiab (son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan); he too was a skilled craftsman and also an expert at engraving

Exodus39:1 Then
Exodus39:2 The ephod was made from this cloth too
Exodus39:3 Bezalel beat gold into thin plates and cut it into wire threads
Exodus39:4 The ephod was held together by shoulder straps at the top
Exodus39:5 and was tied down by an elaborate one-piece woven sash made of the same gold
Exodus39:6 The two onyx stones
Exodus39:7 These stones were reminders to Jehovah concerning the people of Israel; all this was done in accordance with the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:8 The chestpiece was a beautiful piece of work
Exodus39:9 It was a piece nine inches square
Exodus39:10 there were four rows of stones across it. In the first row were a sardius
Exodus39:11 in the second row were an emerald
Exodus39:12 In the third row were a jacinth
Exodus39:13 In the fourth row
Exodus39:14 The stones were engraved like a seal
Exodus39:15 To attach the chestpiece to the ephod
Exodus39:16 and from these gold rings
Exodus39:17 of twined gold attached to gold clasps
Exodus39:18 on the top corners of the chestpiece.
Exodus39:19 Two gold rings were also set at the lower edge of the chestpiece
Exodus39:20 Two other gold rings were placed low on the shoulder straps of the ephod
Exodus39:21 The chestpiece was held securely above the beautifully woven sash of the ephod by tying the rings of the chestpiece to the rings of the ephod with a blue ribbon. All this was commanded to Moses by the Lord.
Exodus39:22 The main part of the ephod was woven
Exodus39:23 and there was a hole at the center
Exodus39:24 Pomegranates were attached to the bottom edge of the robe; these were made of linen cloth
Exodus39:25 Bells of pure gold were placed between the pomegranates along the bottom edge of the skirt
Exodus39:26 with bells and pomegranates alternating all around the edge. This robe was worn when Aaron ministered to the Lord
Exodus39:27 Robes were now made for Aaron and his sons from fine-twined linen thread.
Exodus39:28 The chestpiece
Exodus39:29 and the linen belt was beautifully embroidered with blue
Exodus39:30 Finally
Exodus39:31 It was tied to the turban with a blue cord
Exodus39:32 And so at last the Tabernacle was finished
Exodus39:33 Then they brought the entire Tabernacle to Moses: Furniture; clasps; frames; bars; Posts; bases; layers of covering for the roof and sides—
Exodus39:34 the rams' skins dyed red
Exodus39:35 the Ark with the Ten Commandments in it; The carrying poles; The place of mercy;
Exodus39:36 The table and all its utensils; The Bread of the Presence;
Exodus39:37 The pure gold lampstand with its lamps
Exodus39:38 The gold altar; The anointing oil; The sweet incense; The curtain-door of the Tabernacle;
Exodus39:39 The bronze altar; The bronze grating; The poles and the utensils; The washbasin and its base;
Exodus39:40 The drapes for the walls of the court and the posts holding them up; The bases and the drapes at the gate of the court; The cords and nails; All the utensils used there in the work of the Tabernacle.
Exodus39:41 They also brought for his inspection the beautifully tailored garments to be worn while ministering in the Holy Place and the holy garments for Aaron the priest
Exodus39:42 So the people of Israel followed all the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:43 And Moses inspected all their work and blessed them because it was all as the Lord had instructed him.
Exodus40:1 The Lord now said to Moses
Exodus40:2 “Put together the Tabernacle on the first day of the first month.
Exodus40:3 In it place the Ark containing the Ten Commandments; and install the veil to enclose the Ark within the Holy of Holies.
Exodus40:4 Then bring in the table and place the utensils on it
Exodus40:5 “Place the gold altar for the incense in front of the Ark. Set up the drapes at the entrance of the Tabernacle
Exodus40:6 and place the altar for burnt offerings in front of the entrance.
Exodus40:7 Set the washbasin between the Tabernacle-tent and the altar
Exodus40:8 Then make the courtyard around the outside of the tent
Exodus40:9 “Take the anointing oil and sprinkle it here and there upon the Tabernacle and everything in it
Exodus40:10 Sprinkle the anointing oil upon the altar of burnt offering and its utensils
Exodus40:11 Then anoint the washbasin and its pedestal
Exodus40:12 “Now bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tabernacle and wash them with water;
Exodus40:13 and clothe Aaron with the holy garments and anoint him
Exodus40:14 Then bring his sons and put their robes upon them
Exodus40:15 and anoint them as you did their father
Exodus40:16 So Moses proceeded to do all as the Lord had commanded him.
Exodus40:17 On the first day of the first month
Exodus40:18 Moses erected it by setting its frames into their bases and attaching the bars.
Exodus40:19 Then he spread the coverings over the framework and put on the top layers
Exodus40:20 Inside the Ark he placed the stones with the Ten Commandments engraved on them
Exodus40:21 Then he brought the Ark into the Tabernacle and set up the curtain to screen it
Exodus40:22 Next he placed the table at the north side of the room outside the curtain
Exodus40:23 and set the Bread of the Presence upon the table before the Lord
Exodus40:24 And he placed the lampstand next to the table
Exodus40:25 Then he lighted the lamps before the Lord
Exodus40:26 and placed the gold altar in the Tabernacle next to the curtain
Exodus40:27 and burned upon it the incense made from sweet spices
Exodus40:28 He attached the curtain at the entrance of the Tabernacle
Exodus40:29 and placed the outside altar for the burnt offerings near the entrance
Exodus40:30 Next he placed the washbasin between the tent and the altar and filled it with water so that the priests could use it for washing.
Exodus40:31 Moses and Aaron and Aaron's sons washed their hands and feet there.
Exodus40:32 Whenever they walked past the altar to enter the Tabernacle
Exodus40:33 Then he erected the enclosure surrounding the tent and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the Tabernacle and the glory of the Lord filled it.
Exodus40:35 Moses was not able to enter because the cloud was standing there
Exodus40:36 Whenever the cloud lifted and moved
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud stayed
Exodus40:38 The cloud rested upon the Tabernacle during the daytime
Leviticus1:1 The Lord now spoke to Moses from the Tabernacle
Leviticus1:2 and commanded him to give the following instructions to the people of Israel: “When you sacrifice to the Lord
Leviticus1:3 “If your sacrifice is to be an ox given as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:4 The person bringing it is to lay his hand upon its head
Leviticus1:5 The man shall then kill the animal there before the Lord
Leviticus1:6 Then the priests will skin the animal and quarter
Leviticus1:7 it
Leviticus1:8 and put the sections of the animal and its head and fat upon the wood.
Leviticus1:9 The internal organs and the legs are to be washed
Leviticus1:10 “If the animal used as a burnt offering is a sheep or a goat
Leviticus1:11 The man who brings it will kill it before the Lord on the north side of the altar
Leviticus1:12 Then the man will quarter it
Leviticus1:13 But the internal organs and the legs shall first be washed with water. Then the priests shall burn it all upon the altar as an offering to the Lord; for burnt offerings give much pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus1:14 “If anyone wishes to use a bird as his burnt offering
Leviticus1:15 A priest will take the bird to the altar and wring off its head
Leviticus1:16 Then the priest will remove the crop and the feathers and throw them on the east side of the altar with the ashes.
Leviticus1:17 Then
Leviticus2:1 “Anyone who wishes to sacrifice a grain offering to the Lord is to bring fine flour and is to pour olive oil and incense upon it.
Leviticus2:2 Then he is to take a handful
Leviticus2:3 The remainder of the flour is to be given to Aaron and his sons as their food; but all of it is counted as a holy burnt offering to the Lord.
Leviticus2:4 “If bread baked in the oven is brought as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus2:5 If the offering is something from the griddle
Leviticus2:6 Break it into pieces and pour oil upon it—it is a form of grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If your offering is cooked in a pan
Leviticus2:8 “However it is prepared—whether baked
Leviticus2:9 “The priests are to burn only a representative portion of the offering
Leviticus2:10 The remainder belongs to the priests for their own use
Leviticus2:11 “Use no yeast with your offerings of flour; for no yeast or honey is permitted in burnt offerings to the Lord.
Leviticus2:12 You may offer yeast bread and honey as thanksgiving offerings at harvest time
Leviticus2:13 “Every offering must be seasoned with salt
Leviticus2:14 “If you are offering from the first of your harvest
Leviticus2:15 Put olive oil and incense on the offering
Leviticus2:16 Then the priests shall burn part of the bruised grain mixed with oil and all of the incense as a representative portion before the Lord.
Leviticus3:1 “When anyone wants to give an offering of thanksgiving to the Lord
Leviticus3:2 The man who brings the animal shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it at the door of the Tabernacle. Then Aaron's sons shall throw the blood against the sides of the altar
Leviticus3:3 and shall burn before the Lord the fat that covers the
Leviticus3:4 inward parts
Leviticus3:5 the gall bladder. And it will give the Lord much pleasure.
Leviticus3:6 “If a goat or sheep is used as a thank-offering to the Lord
Leviticus3:7 “If it is a lamb
Leviticus3:8 shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it at the entrance of the Tabernacle; the priests shall throw the blood against the sides of the altar
Leviticus3:9 and shall offer upon the altar the fat
Leviticus3:10 the two kidneys with the loin-fat on them
Leviticus3:11 the gall bladder
Leviticus3:12 “If anyone brings a goat as his offering to the Lord
Leviticus3:13 he shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it at the entrance of the Tabernacle. The priest shall throw its blood against the sides of the altar
Leviticus3:14 and shall offer upon the altar
Leviticus3:15 the two kidneys and the loin-fat on them
Leviticus3:16 This burnt offering is very pleasing to the Lord. All the fat is Jehovah's.
Leviticus3:17 This is a permanent law throughout your land
Leviticus4:1 Then the Lord gave these further instructions to Moses:
Leviticus4:2 “Tell the people of Israel that these are the laws concerning anyone who unintentionally breaks any of my commandments.
Leviticus4:3 If a priest sins unintentionally and so brings guilt upon the people
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring it to the door of the Tabernacle
Leviticus4:5 Then the priest shall take the animal's blood into the Tabernacle
Leviticus4:6 and shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord in front of the veil that bars the way to the Holy of Holies.
Leviticus4:7 Then the priest shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the incense altar before the Lord in the Tabernacle; the remainder of the blood shall be poured out at the base of the altar for burnt offerings
Leviticus4:8 Then he shall take all the fat on the entrails
Leviticus4:9 the two kidneys and the loin-fat on them
Leviticus4:10 and shall burn them on the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:11 But the remainder of the young bull—the skin
Leviticus4:12 shall be carried to a ceremonially clean place outside the camp—a place where the ashes are brought from the altar—and burned there on a wood fire.
Leviticus4:13 “If the entire nation of Israel sins without realizing it and does something that Jehovah has said not to do
Leviticus4:14 When they realize it
Leviticus4:15 where the leaders of the nation shall lay their hands upon the animal's head and kill it before the Lord.
Leviticus4:16 Then the priest shall bring its blood into the Tabernacle
Leviticus4:17 and shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord
Leviticus4:18 Then he shall put blood upon the horns of the altar there in the Tabernacle before the Lord
Leviticus4:19 All the fat shall be removed and burned upon the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He shall follow the same procedure as for a sin offering; in this way the priest shall make atonement for the nation
Leviticus4:21 The priest shall then cart the young bull outside the camp and burn it there
Leviticus4:22 “If one of the leaders sins without realizing it and is guilty of disobeying one of God's laws
Leviticus4:23 as soon as it is called to his attention he must bring as his sacrifice a male goat without any physical defect.
Leviticus4:24 He shall lay his hand upon its head and kill it at the place where the burnt offerings are killed
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest shall take some of the blood of this sin offering and place it with his finger upon the horns of the altar of burnt offerings
Leviticus4:26 All the fat shall be burned upon the altar
Leviticus4:27 “If any one of the common people sins and doesn't realize it
Leviticus4:28 But as soon as he does realize it
Leviticus4:29 He shall bring it to the place where the animals for burnt offerings are killed
Leviticus4:30 And the priest shall take some of the blood with his finger and smear it upon the horns of the burnt offering altar. Then the priest shall pour out the remainder of the blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:31 All the fat shall be taken off
Leviticus4:32 “However
Leviticus4:33 He shall bring it to the place where the burnt offerings are killed
Leviticus4:34 The priest shall take some of the blood with his finger and smear it upon the horns of the burnt offering altar
Leviticus4:35 The fat shall be used just as in the case of a thank-offering lamb—the priest shall burn the fat on the altar as in any other sacrifice made to Jehovah by fire; and the priest shall make atonement for the man
Leviticus5:1 “Anyone refusing to give testimony concerning what he knows about a crime is guilty.
Leviticus5:2 “Anyone touching anything ceremonially unclean—such as the dead body of an animal forbidden for food
Leviticus5:3 Or if he touches human discharge of any kind
Leviticus5:4 “If anyone makes a rash vow
Leviticus5:5 “In any of these cases
Leviticus5:6 and bring his guilt offering to the Lord
Leviticus5:7 “If he is too poor to bring a lamb to the Lord
Leviticus5:8 The priest shall offer as the sin sacrifice whichever bird is handed to him first
Leviticus5:9 Then he shall sprinkle some of the blood at the side of the altar and the rest shall be drained out at the base of the altar; this is the sin offering.
Leviticus5:10 He shall offer the second bird as a burnt offering
Leviticus5:11 “If he is too poor to bring turtledoves or young pigeons as his sin offering
Leviticus5:12 He shall bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 In this way the priest shall make atonement for him for any sin of this kind
Leviticus5:14 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus5:15 “If anyone sins by unintentionally defiling what is holy
Leviticus5:16 And he shall make restitution for the holy thing he has defiled
Leviticus5:17 “Anyone who disobeys some law of God without realizing it is guilty anyway
Leviticus5:18 and must bring his sacrifice of a value determined by Moses. This sacrifice shall be a ram without blemish taken to the priest as a guilt offering; with it the priest shall make atonement for him
Leviticus5:19 It must be offered as a guilt offering
Leviticus6:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:2 “If anyone sins against me by refusing to return a deposit on something borrowed or rented
Leviticus6:3 or by finding a lost article and lying about it
Leviticus6:4 on the day he is found guilty of any such sin
Leviticus6:5 adding a 20 percent fine
Leviticus6:6 His guilt offering shall be a ram without defect
Leviticus6:7 and the priest shall make atonement for him before the Lord
Leviticus6:8 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:9 “Give Aaron and his sons these regulations concerning the burnt offering: “The burnt offering shall be left upon the hearth of the altar all night
Leviticus6:10 (The next morning) the priest shall put on his linen undergarments and his linen outer garments
Leviticus6:11 Then he shall change his clothes and carry the ashes outside the camp to a place that is ceremonially clean.
Leviticus6:12 Meanwhile
Leviticus6:13 The fire must be kept burning upon the altar continually. It must never go out.
Leviticus6:14 “These are the regulations concerning the grain offering: “Aaron's sons shall stand in front of the altar to offer it before the Lord.
Leviticus6:15 The priest shall then take out a handful of the finely ground flour
Leviticus6:16 After taking out this handful
Leviticus6:17 (Stress this instruction
Leviticus6:18 It may be eaten by any male descendant of Aaron
Leviticus6:19 And Jehovah said to Moses
Leviticus6:20 “On the day Aaron and his sons are anointed and inducted into the priesthood
Leviticus6:21 It shall be cooked on a griddle
Leviticus6:22 As the sons of the priests replace their fathers
Leviticus6:23 These offerings shall be entirely burned up before the Lord; none of it shall be eaten.“
Leviticus6:24 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:25 “Tell Aaron and his sons that these are the instructions concerning the sin offering: “This sacrifice is most holy
Leviticus6:26 The priest who performs the ceremony shall eat it in the courtyard of the Tabernacle.
Leviticus6:27 Only those who are sanctified—the priests—may touch this meat; if any blood sprinkles onto their clothing
Leviticus6:28 Then the clay pot in which the clothing is boiled shall be broken; or if a bronze kettle is used
Leviticus6:29 Every male among the priests may eat this offering
Leviticus6:30 No sin offering may be eaten by the priests if any of its blood is taken into the Tabernacle to make atonement in the Holy Place. That carcass must be entirely burned with fire before the Lord.
Leviticus7:1 “Here are the instructions concerning the most holy offering for guilt:
Leviticus7:2 “The sacrificial animal shall be killed at the place where the burnt offering sacrifices are slain
Leviticus7:3 The priest will offer upon the altar all its fat
Leviticus7:4 the two kidneys and the loin-fat
Leviticus7:5 The priests will burn them upon the altar as a guilt offering to the Lord.
Leviticus7:6 Only males among the priests may then eat the carcass
Leviticus7:7 “The same instructions apply to both the sin offering and the guilt offering—the carcass shall be given to the priest who is in charge of the atonement ceremony
Leviticus7:8 (When the offering is a burnt sacrifice
Leviticus7:9 The priests who present the people's grain offerings to the Lord shall be given whatever remains of the sacrifice after the ceremony is completed. This rule applies whether the sacrifice is baked
Leviticus7:10 All other grain offerings
Leviticus7:11 “Here are the instructions concerning the sacrifices given to the Lord as special peace offerings:
Leviticus7:12 “If it is an offering of thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 This thanksgiving peace offering shall be accompanied with loaves of leavened bread.
Leviticus7:14 Part of this sacrifice shall be presented to the Lord by a gesture of waving it before the altar
Leviticus7:15 After the animal has been sacrificed and presented to the Lord as a peace offering to show special appreciation and thanksgiving to him
Leviticus7:16 “However
Leviticus7:17 But anything left over until the third day shall be burned.
Leviticus7:18 For if any of it is eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 “Any meat that comes into contact with anything that is ceremonially unclean shall not be eaten
Leviticus7:20 Any priest who is ceremonially unclean but eats the thanksgiving offering anyway
Leviticus7:21 Anyone who touches anything that is ceremonially unclean
Leviticus7:22 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:23 “Tell the people of Israel never to eat fat
Leviticus7:24 The fat of an animal that dies of disease

Leviticus7:26 “Never eat blood
Leviticus7:27 Anyone who does shall be excommunicated from his people.“

Leviticus7:32 while the right thigh shall

Leviticus7:35 This is their pay! It is to be set apart from the burnt offerings
Leviticus7:36 For on the day the Lord anointed them

Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and sprinkled it upon the Tabernacle itself and on each item in it

Leviticus8:12 Then he poured the anointing oil upon Aaron's head
Leviticus8:13 Next Moses placed the robes on Aaron's sons
Leviticus8:14 Then he took the young bull for the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 as Moses killed it. He smeared some of the blood with his finger upon the four horns of the altar and upon the altar itself

Leviticus8:17 The carcass of the young bull
Leviticus8:18 Then he presented to the Lord the ram for the burnt offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon its head
Leviticus8:19 and Moses killed it and sprinkled the blood back and forth upon the altar.
Leviticus8:20 Next he quartered the ram and burned the pieces
Leviticus8:21 He then washed the insides and the legs with water
Leviticus8:22 Then Moses presented the other ram
Leviticus8:23 Moses killed it and took some of its blood and smeared it upon the lobe of Aaron's right ear and the thumb of his right hand and upon the big toe of his right foot.
Leviticus8:24 Next he smeared some of the blood upon Aaron's sons—upon the lobes of their right ears
Leviticus8:25 Then he took the fat
Leviticus8:26 and placed on top of these one unleavened wafer
Leviticus8:27 All this was placed in the hands of Aaron and his sons to present to the Lord by a gesture of waving them before the altar.
Leviticus8:28 Moses then took it all back from them and burned it upon the altar
Leviticus8:29 Now Moses took the breast and presented it to the Lord by waving it before the altar; this was Moses' portion of the ram of consecration
Leviticus8:30 Next he took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that had been sprinkled upon the altar
Leviticus8:31 Then Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 Anything left of the meat and bread must be burned.“

Leviticus11:1 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 “Tell the people of Israel that the animals which may be used
Leviticus11:3 for food include any animal with cloven hooves which chews its cud.
Leviticus11:4 This means that the following may not be eaten: The camel (it chews the cud but does not have cloven hooves);
Leviticus11:5 The coney
Leviticus11:6 The hare (because although it chews the cud
Leviticus11:7 The swine (because although it has cloven hooves
Leviticus11:8 You may not eat their meat or even touch their dead bodies; they are forbidden foods for you.
Leviticus11:9 “As to fish
Leviticus11:10 but all other water creatures are strictly forbidden to you.
Leviticus11:11 You mustn't eat their meat or even touch their dead bodies.
Leviticus11:12 I'll repeat it again—any water creature that does not have fins or scales is forbidden to you.
Leviticus11:13 “Among the birds
Leviticus11:14 The falcon (all kinds)
Leviticus11:15 The raven (all kinds)
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the owl
Leviticus11:18 The marsh hen
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 “No insects may be eaten
Leviticus11:21 with the exception of those that jump; locusts of
Leviticus11:22 all varieties—ordinary locusts
Leviticus11:23 All insects that fly and walk or crawl are forbidden to you.
Leviticus11:24 “Anyone touching their dead bodies shall be defiled until the evening
Leviticus11:25 and must wash his clothes immediately. He must also quarantine himself until nightfall
Leviticus11:26 “You are also defiled by touching any animal with only semiparted hoofs
Leviticus11:27 Any animal that walks on paws is forbidden to you as food. Anyone touching the dead body of such an animal shall be defiled until evening.
Leviticus11:28 Anyone carrying away the carcass shall wash his clothes and be ceremonially defiled until evening; for it is forbidden to you.
Leviticus11:29 “These are the forbidden small animals which scurry about your feet or crawl upon the ground: The mole
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 Anyone touching their dead bodies shall be defiled until evening
Leviticus11:32 and anything upon which the carcass falls shall be defiled—any article of wood
Leviticus11:33 If it falls into a pottery bowl
Leviticus11:34 If the water used to cleanse the defiled article touches any food
Leviticus11:35 “If the dead body of such an animal touches any clay oven
Leviticus11:36 If the body falls into a spring or cistern where there is water
Leviticus11:37 And if the carcass touches grain to be sown in the field
Leviticus11:38 but if the seeds are wet and the carcass falls upon it
Leviticus11:39 “If an animal which you are permitted to eat dies of disease
Leviticus11:40 Also
Leviticus11:41 “Animals that crawl shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 This includes all reptiles that slither along upon their bellies as well as those that have legs. No crawling thing with many feet may be eaten
Leviticus11:43 Do not defile yourselves by touching it.
Leviticus11:44 “I am the Lord your God. Keep yourselves pure concerning these things
Leviticus11:45 For I am the Lord who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. You must therefore be holy
Leviticus11:46 These are the laws concerning animals
Leviticus11:47 These are the distinctions between what is ceremonially clean and may be eaten

Leviticus13:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 “If anyone notices a swelling in his skin
Leviticus13:3 for the spot to be examined. If the hair in this spot turns white
Leviticus13:4 “But if the white spot in the skin does not seem to be deeper than the skin and the hair in the spot has not turned white
Leviticus13:5 At the end of that time
Leviticus13:6 Again on the seventh day the priest will examine him
Leviticus13:7 But if the spot spreads in the skin after he has come to the priest to be examined
Leviticus13:8 and the priest shall look again
Leviticus13:9 “When anyone suspected of having leprosy is brought to the priest
Leviticus13:10 the priest is to look to see if there is a white swelling in the skin with white hairs in the spot
Leviticus13:11 If he finds these symptoms
Leviticus13:12 But if the priest sees that the leprosy has erupted and spread all over his body from head to foot wherever he looks
Leviticus13:13 then the priest shall pronounce him cured of leprosy
Leviticus13:14 But if there is raw flesh anywhere
Leviticus13:15 It is proved by the raw flesh.
Leviticus13:16 But if the raw flesh later changes to white
Leviticus13:17 to be examined again. If the spot has indeed turned completely white
Leviticus13:18 “In the case of a man who has a boil in his skin which heals
Leviticus13:19 but which leaves a white swelling or a bright spot
Leviticus13:20 If the priest sees that the trouble seems to be down under the skin
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest sees that there are no white hairs in this spot
Leviticus13:22 If during that time the spot spreads
Leviticus13:23 But if the bright spot grows no larger and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 “If a man is burned in some way
Leviticus13:25 then the priest must examine the spot. If the hair in the bright spot turns white and the problem seems to be more than skin-deep
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest sees that there are no white hairs in the bright spot and the brightness appears to be no deeper than the skin and is fading
Leviticus13:27 and examine him again the seventh day. If the spot spreads in the skin
Leviticus13:28 But if the bright spot does not move or spread in the skin
Leviticus13:29 “If a man or woman has a sore on the head or chin
Leviticus13:30 the priest must examine him; if the infection seems to be below the skin and yellow hair is found in the sore
Leviticus13:31 But if the priest's examination reveals that the spot seems to be only in the skin but there is healthy hair in it
Leviticus13:32 and examined again on the seventh day. If the spot has not spread and no yellow hair has appeared
Leviticus13:33 he shall shave off all the hair around the spot (but not on the spot itself) and the priest shall quarantine him for another seven days.
Leviticus13:34 He shall be examined again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:35 But if
Leviticus13:36 then the priest must examine him again and
Leviticus13:37 But if it appears that the spreading has stopped and black hairs are found in the spot
Leviticus13:38 “If a man or a woman has white
Leviticus13:39 but these spots are growing dimmer
Leviticus13:40 “If a man's hair is gone
Leviticus13:41 If the hair is gone from the front part of his head
Leviticus13:42 However
Leviticus13:43 In that case the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:44 then he is a leper
Leviticus13:45 “Anyone who is discovered to have leprosy must tear his clothes and let his hair grow in wild disarray
Leviticus13:46 As long as the disease lasts
Leviticus13:47 “If leprosy is suspected in a woolen or linen garment
Leviticus13:48 or fabric
Leviticus13:49 and there is a greenish or a reddish spot in it
Leviticus13:50 The priest will put it away for seven days
Leviticus13:51 and look at it again on the seventh day. If the spot has spread
Leviticus13:52 and he must burn the clothing
Leviticus13:53 “But if when he examines it again on the seventh day the spot has not spread
Leviticus13:54 the priest shall order the suspected article to be washed
Leviticus13:55 If after that time the spot has not changed its color
Leviticus13:56 But if the priest sees that the spot has faded after the washing
Leviticus13:57 However
Leviticus13:58 But if after washing it there is no further trouble
Leviticus13:59 These are the regulations concerning leprosy in a garment or anything made of skin or leather

Leviticus14:2 regulations concerning a person whose leprosy disappears:
Leviticus14:3 “The priest shall go out of the camp to examine him. If the priest sees that the leprosy is gone

Leviticus14:5 The priest shall then order one of the birds killed in an earthenware pot held above running water.
Leviticus14:6 The other bird
Leviticus14:7 Then the priest shall sprinkle the blood seven times upon the man cured of his leprosy
Leviticus14:8 “Then the man who is cured shall wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 The seventh day he shall again shave all the hair from his head
Leviticus14:10 “The next day

Leviticus14:13 Then he shall kill the lamb at the place where sin offerings and burnt offerings are killed
Leviticus14:14 The priest shall take the blood from this guilt offering and smear some of it upon the tip of the right ear of the man being cleansed
Leviticus14:15 “Then the priest shall take the olive oil and pour it into the palm of his left hand
Leviticus14:16 and dip his right finger into it
Leviticus14:17 Some of the oil remaining in his left hand shall then be placed by the priest upon the tip of the man's right ear and the thumb of his right hand and the big toe of his right foot—just as he did with the blood of the guilt offering.
Leviticus14:18 The remainder of the oil in his hand shall be used to anoint the man's head. Thus the priest shall make atonement for him before the Lord.
Leviticus14:19 “Then the priest must offer the sin offering and again perform the rite of atonement for the person being cleansed from his leprosy; and afterwards the priest shall kill the burnt offering
Leviticus14:20 and offer it along with the grain offering upon the altar
Leviticus14:21 “If he is so poor that he cannot afford two lambs
Leviticus14:22 “He shall also bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons—whichever he is able to afford—and use one of the pair for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering.
Leviticus14:23 He shall bring them to the priest at the entrance of the Tabernacle on the eighth day
Leviticus14:24 The priest shall take the lamb for the guilt offering
Leviticus14:25 Then he shall kill the lamb for the guilt offering and smear some of its blood upon the tip of the man's right ear—the man on whose behalf the ceremony is being performed—and upon the thumb of his right hand and upon the big toe of his right foot.
Leviticus14:26 “The priest shall then pour the olive oil into the palm of his own left hand
Leviticus14:27 and with his right finger he is to sprinkle some of it seven times before the Lord.
Leviticus14:28 Then he must put some of the olive oil from his hand upon the tip of the man's right ear
Leviticus14:29 The remaining oil in his hand shall be placed upon the head of the man being cleansed
Leviticus14:30 “Then he must offer the two turtledoves or two young pigeons (whichever pair he is able to afford).
Leviticus14:31 One of the pair is for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering

Leviticus18:1 The Lord then told Moses to tell the
Leviticus18:2 people of Israel
Leviticus18:3 so don't act like heathen—like the people of Egypt where you lived so long
Leviticus18:4 You must obey only my laws
Leviticus18:5 If you obey them
Leviticus18:6 “None of you shall marry a near relative
Leviticus18:7 Do not disgrace your father by having intercourse with your mother
Leviticus18:8 nor any other of your father's wives.
Leviticus18:9 Do not have intercourse with your sister or half-sister
Leviticus18:10 “You shall not have intercourse with your granddaughter—the daughter of either your son or your daughter—for she is a close relative.
Leviticus18:11 You may not have intercourse with a half-sister—your father's wife's daughter;
Leviticus18:12 nor your aunt—your father's sister—because she is so closely related to your father;
Leviticus18:13 nor your aunt—your mother's sister—because she is a close relative of your mother;
Leviticus18:14 nor your aunt—the wife of your father's brother.
Leviticus18:15 “You may not marry your daughter-in-law—your son's wife;
Leviticus18:16 nor your brother's wife
Leviticus18:17 You may not marry both a woman and her daughter or granddaughter
Leviticus18:18 You shall not marry two sisters
Leviticus18:19 “There must be no sexual relationship with a woman who is menstruating;
Leviticus18:20 nor with anyone else's wife
Leviticus18:21 “You shall not give any of your children to Molech

Leviticus23:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:2 celebrate several annual festivals of the Lord—times when all Israel will assemble and worship me.
Leviticus23:3 (These are in addition to your Sabbaths—the seventh day of every week—which are always days of rest in every home
Leviticus23:4 These are the holy festivals which are to be observed each year:
Leviticus23:5 “The Passover of the Lord: This is to be celebrated on the first day of April
Leviticus23:6 “The Festival of Unleavened Bread: This is to be celebrated beginning the day following the Passover
Leviticus23:7 On the first day of this festival
Leviticus23:8 You shall do the same on the seventh day of the festival. On each of the intervening days you shall make an offering by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus23:9 “The Festival of First Fruits: When you arrive in the land I will give you
Leviticus23:10 and you reap your first harvest
Leviticus23:11 on the day after the Sabbath. He shall wave it before the Lord in a gesture of offering
Leviticus23:12 That same day you shall sacrifice to the Lord a male yearling lamb without defect as a burnt offering.
Leviticus23:13 A grain offering shall accompany it
Leviticus23:14 Until this is done you must not eat any of the harvest for yourselves—neither fresh kernels nor bread nor parched grain. This is a permanent law throughout your nation.
Leviticus23:15 “The Harvest Festival (Festival of Pentecost): Fifty days later you shall bring to the
Leviticus23:16 Lord an offering of a sample of the new grain of your later crops.
Leviticus23:17 This shall consist of two loaves of bread from your homes to be waved before the Lord in a gesture of offering. Bake this bread from a fifth of a bushel of fine flour containing yeast. It is an offering to the Lord of the first sampling of your later crops.
Leviticus23:18 Along with the bread and the wine
Leviticus23:19 And you shall offer one male goat for a sin offering and two male yearling lambs for a peace offering.
Leviticus23:20 “The priests shall wave these offerings before the Lord along with the loaves representing the first sampling of your later crops. They are holy to the Lord and will be given to the priests as food.
Leviticus23:21 That day shall be announced as a time of sacred convocation of all the people; don't do any work that day. This is a law to be honored from generation to generation.
Leviticus23:22 (When you reap your harvests
Leviticus23:23 “The Festival of Trumpets: Mid-September is a time for all the people to meet together for
Leviticus23:24 worship; it is a time of remembrance
Leviticus23:25 Don't do any hard work on that day
Leviticus23:26 “The Day of Atonement follows nine days later: All the people are to come together before
Leviticus23:27 the Lord
Leviticus23:28 Don't do any work that day
Leviticus23:29 Anyone who does not spend the day in repentance and sorrow for sin shall be excommunicated from his people.
Leviticus23:30 And I will put to death anyone who does any kind of work that day.
Leviticus23:31 This is a law of Israel from generation to generation.
Leviticus23:32 For this is a Sabbath of rest
Leviticus23:33 “The Festival of Shelters: Five days later
Leviticus23:34 is the Festival of Shelters to be celebrated before the Lord for seven days.
Leviticus23:35 On the first day there will be a sacred assembly of all the people; don't do any hard work that day.
Leviticus23:36 On each of the seven days of the festival you are to sacrifice an offering by fire to the Lord. The eighth day requires another sacred convocation of all the people
Leviticus23:37 “(These
Leviticus23:38 These annual festivals are in addition to your regular Sabbaths—the weekly days of holy rest. The sacrifices made during the festivals are to be in addition to your regular giving and normal fulfillment of your vows.)
Leviticus23:39 “This last day of September
Leviticus23:40 On the first day
Leviticus23:41 This seven-day annual feast is a law from generation to generation.
Leviticus23:42 During those seven days
Leviticus23:43 The purpose of this is to remind the people of Israel
Leviticus23:44 So Moses announced these annual festivals of the Lord to the people of Israel.

Leviticus25:7 Cattle and wild animals alike shall be allowed to graze there.

Leviticus25:10 For the fiftieth year shall be holy

Leviticus25:14 Because of this

Leviticus25:23 And remember

Leviticus25:25 If anyone becomes poor and sells some of his land
Leviticus25:26 If there is no one else to redeem it
Leviticus25:27 then he may always buy it back at a price proportionate to the number of harvests until the Jubilee
Leviticus25:28 But if the original owner is not able to redeem it
Leviticus25:29 “If a man sells a house in the city
Leviticus25:30 But if it is not redeemed within the year
Leviticus25:31 But village houses—a village is a settlement without fortifying walls around it—are like farmland
Leviticus25:32 “There is one exception: The homes of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 and must be returned to the original owners in the Year of Jubilee; for the Levites will not be given farmland like the other tribes
Leviticus25:34 The Levites are not permitted to sell the fields of common land surrounding their cities
Leviticus25:35 “If your brother becomes poor
Leviticus25:36 Fear your God and let your brother live with you; and don't charge him interest on the money you lend him.
Leviticus25:37 Remember—no interest; and give him what he needs
Leviticus25:38 For I
Leviticus25:39 “If a fellow Israelite becomes poor and sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 but rather as a hired servant or as a guest; and he shall serve you only until the Year of Jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 At that time he can leave with his children and return to his own family and possessions.
Leviticus25:42 For I brought you from the land of Egypt
Leviticus25:43 or treated harshly; fear your God.
Leviticus25:44 “However
Leviticus25:45 and you may purchase the children of the foreigners living among you
Leviticus25:46 They will be permanent slaves for you to pass on to your children after you; but your brothers
Leviticus25:47 “If a foreigner living among you becomes rich
Leviticus25:48 he may be redeemed by one of his brothers
Leviticus25:49 his uncle
Leviticus25:50 The price of his freedom shall be in proportion to the number of years left before the Year of Jubilee—whatever it would cost to hire a servant for that number of years.
Leviticus25:51 If there are still many years until the Jubilee
Leviticus25:52 if the years have passed and only a few remain until the Jubilee
Leviticus25:53 If he sells himself to a foreigner
Leviticus25:54 If he has not been redeemed by the time the Year of Jubilee arrives
Leviticus25:55 For the people of Israel are my servants; I brought them from the land of Egypt; I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus26:1 “You must have no idols; you must never worship carved images
Leviticus26:2 You must obey my Sabbath laws of rest
Leviticus26:3 “If you obey all of my commandments
Leviticus26:4 I will give you regular rains
Leviticus26:5 And grapes will still be ripening when sowing time comes again. You shall eat your fill
Leviticus26:6 for I will give you peace
Leviticus26:7 You will chase your enemies; they will die beneath your swords.
Leviticus26:8 Five of you will chase a hundred
Leviticus26:9 I will look after you
Leviticus26:10 You will have such a surplus of crops that you won't know what to do with them when the new harvest is ready!
Leviticus26:11 And I will live among you and not despise you.
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you and be your God
Leviticus26:13 For I am the Lord your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt
Leviticus26:14 “But if you will not listen to me or obey me
Leviticus26:15 but reject my laws
Leviticus26:16 this is what I will do to you: I will punish you with sudden terrors and panic
Leviticus26:17 I will set my face against you and you will flee before your attackers; those who hate you will rule you; you will even run when no one is chasing you!
Leviticus26:18 “And if you still disobey me
Leviticus26:19 I will break your proud power and make your heavens as iron and your earth as bronze.
Leviticus26:20 Your strength shall be spent in vain; for your land shall not yield its crops
Leviticus26:21 “And if even then you will not obey me and listen to me
Leviticus26:22 I will send wild animals to kill your children and destroy your cattle and reduce your numbers so that your roads will be deserted.
Leviticus26:23 “And if even this will not reform you
Leviticus26:24 then I will walk against your wishes
Leviticus26:25 I will revenge the breaking of my covenant by bringing war against you. You will flee to your cities
Leviticus26:26 I will destroy your food supply so that one oven will be large enough to bake all the bread available for ten entire families; and you will still be hungry after your pittance has been doled out to you.
Leviticus26:27 “And if you still won't listen to me or obey me
Leviticus26:28 then I will let loose my great anger and send you seven times greater punishment for your sins.
Leviticus26:29 You shall eat your own sons and daughters
Leviticus26:30 and I will destroy the altars on the hills where you worship your idols
Leviticus26:31 I will make your cities desolate
Leviticus26:32 Yes
Leviticus26:33 “I will scatter you out among the nations
Leviticus26:34 Then at last the land will rest and make up for the many years you refused to let it lie idle; for it will lie desolate all the years that you are captives in enemy lands.
Leviticus26:35 Yes
Leviticus26:36 “And for those who are left alive
Leviticus26:37 Yes
Leviticus26:38 You shall perish among the nations and be destroyed among your enemies.
Leviticus26:39 Those left shall pine away in enemy lands because of their sins
Leviticus26:40 “But at last they shall confess their sins and their fathers' sins of treachery against me.
Leviticus26:41 (Because they were against me
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember again my promises to Abraham
Leviticus26:43 For the land shall enjoy its Sabbaths as it lies desolate. But then at last they shall accept their punishment for rejecting my laws and for despising my rule.
Leviticus26:44 But despite all they have done
Leviticus26:45 For their sakes I will remember my promises to their ancestors to be their God. For I brought their forefathers out of Egypt as all the nations watched in wonder. I am Jehovah.“
Leviticus26:46 These were the laws

Leviticus27:2 a special vow to give himself to the Lord
Leviticus27:3 A man from the age of twenty to sixty shall pay twenty-five dollars;
Leviticus27:4 a woman from the age of twenty to sixty shall pay fifteen dollars;
Leviticus27:5 a boy from five to twenty shall pay ten dollars; a girl
Leviticus27:6 A boy one month to five years old shall have paid for him two and a half dollars; a girl
Leviticus27:7 A man over sixty shall pay seven and a half dollars; a woman
Leviticus27:8 But if the person is too poor to pay this amount
Leviticus27:9 “But if it is an animal that is vowed to be given to the Lord as a sacrifice
Leviticus27:10 The vow may not be changed; the donor may neither change his mind about giving it to the Lord
Leviticus27:11 But if the animal given to the Lord is not a kind that is permitted as a sacrifice
Leviticus27:12 owner shall bring it to the priest to value it
Leviticus27:13 If the animal is a kind that may be offered as a sacrifice
Leviticus27:14 “If someone donates his home to the Lord and then wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:15 and the man shall pay that amount plus 20 percent
Leviticus27:16 “If a man dedicates any part of his field to the Lord
Leviticus27:17 If a man dedicates his field in the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 but if it is after the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 If the man decides to redeem the field
Leviticus27:20 But if he decides not to redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 When it is freed in the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:22 “If a man dedicates to the Lord a field he has bought
Leviticus27:23 the priest shall estimate the value until the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:24 and in the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to the original owner from whom it was bought.
Leviticus27:25 All the valuations shall be stated in standard money.
Leviticus27:26 “You may not dedicate to the Lord the firstborn of any ox or sheep
Leviticus27:27 But if it is the firstborn of an animal that cannot be sacrificed because it is not on the list of those acceptable to the Lord
Leviticus27:28 However
Leviticus27:29 No one sentenced by the courts to die may pay a fine instead; he shall surely be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 “A tenth of the produce of the land
Leviticus27:31 If anyone wants to buy back this fruit or grain
Leviticus27:32 And the Lord owns every tenth animal of your herds and flocks and other domestic animals
Leviticus27:33 The tenth given to the Lord shall not be selected on the basis of whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments the Lord gave to Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.

Numbers6:1 The Lord gave Moses these further instructions for the people of Israel:
Numbers6:2 “When either a man or a woman takes the special vow of a Nazirite
Numbers6:3 he must not thereafter
Numbers6:4 He may eat nothing that comes from grape vines
Numbers6:5 “Throughout that time he must never cut his hair
Numbers6:6 “And he may not go near any dead body during the entire period of his vow
Numbers6:7 even if it is the body of his father
Numbers6:8 and he is consecrated to the Lord throughout the entire period.
Numbers6:9 If he is defiled by having someone fall dead beside him
Numbers6:10 The next day
Numbers6:11 The priest shall offer one of the birds for a sin offering
Numbers6:12 The days of his vow that were fulfilled before his defilement no longer count. He must begin all over again with a new vow
Numbers6:13 “At the conclusion of the period of his vow of separation to the Lord
Numbers6:14 and offer a burnt sacrifice to the Lord
Numbers6:15 a basket of bread made without yeast; pancakes made of fine flour mixed with olive oil; unleavened wafers spread with oil; and the accompanying grain offering and drink offerings.
Numbers6:16 The priest shall present these offerings before the Lord: first the sin offering and the burnt offering;
Numbers6:17 then the ram for a peace offering
Numbers6:18 “Then the Nazirite shall shave his long hair—the sign of his vow of separation. This shall be done at the entrance of the Tabernacle
Numbers6:19 After the man's head has been shaved
Numbers6:20 The priest shall then wave it all back and forth before the Lord in a gesture of offering; all of it is a holy portion for the priest
Numbers6:21 “These are the regulations concerning a Nazirite and his sacrifices at the conclusion of his period of special dedication. In addition to these sacrifices he must bring any further offering he promised at the time he took his vow to become a Nazirite.“
Numbers6:22 Now the Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:23 they are to give this special blessing to the people of Israel:
Numbers6:24 'May the Lord bless and protect you; may the Lord's
Numbers6:25 face radiate with joy because of you; may he be gracious
Numbers6:26 to you
Numbers6:27 This is how Aaron and his sons shall call down my blessings upon the people of Israel; and I myself will personally bless them.“

Numbers13:15 Geuel

Numbers13:19 and whether the land is fertile or not; and what cities there are
Numbers13:20 whether the land is rich or poor
Numbers13:21 So they spied out the land all the way from the wilderness of Zin to Rehob near Hamath.
Numbers13:22 Going northward
Numbers13:23 Then they came to what is now known as the Valley of Eshcol where they cut down a single cluster of grapes so large that it took two of them to carry it on a pole between them! They also took some samples of the pomegranates and figs.
Numbers13:24 The Israelis named the valley “Eshcol“ at that time (meaning “Cluster“) because of the cluster of grapes they found!
Numbers13:25 After forty days of exploration they returned from their tour.
Numbers13:26 They made their report to Moses
Numbers13:27 This was their report: “We arrived in the land you sent us to see
Numbers13:28 But the people living there are powerful
Numbers13:29 The Amalekites live in the south
Numbers13:30 But Caleb reassured the people as they stood before Moses. “Let us go up at once and possess it
Numbers13:31 “Not against people as strong as they are!“ the other spies said. “They would crush us!“
Numbers13:32 So the majority report of the spies was negative: “The land is full of warriors
Numbers13:33 and we saw some of the Anakim there
Numbers14:1 Then all the people began weeping aloud
Numbers14:2 Their voices rose in a great chorus of complaint against Moses and Aaron. “We wish we had died in Egypt
Numbers14:3 rather than be taken into this country ahead of us. Jehovah will kill us there
Numbers14:4 The idea swept the camp. “Let's elect a leader to take us back to Egypt!“ they shouted.
Numbers14:5 Then Moses and Aaron fell face downward on the ground before the people of Israel.
Numbers14:6 Two of the spies
Numbers14:7 and said to all the people
Numbers14:8 and the Lord loves us. He will bring us safely into the land and give it to us. It is very fertile
Numbers14:9 Oh
Numbers14:10 But the only response of the people was to talk of stoning them. Then the glory of the Lord appeared
Numbers14:11 and the Lord said to Moses
Numbers14:12 I will disinherit them and destroy them with a plague
Numbers14:13 “But what will the Egyptians think when they hear about it?“ Moses pleaded with the Lord. “They know full well the power you displayed in rescuing your people.
Numbers14:14 They have told this to the inhabitants of this land
Numbers14:15 Now if you kill all your people

Numbers19:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 “Here is another of my laws: “Tell the people of Israel to bring you a red heifer without defect
Numbers19:3 Give her to Eleazar the priest and he shall take her outside the camp and someone shall kill her as he watches.
Numbers19:4 Eleazar shall take some of her blood upon his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the Tabernacle.
Numbers19:5 Then someone shall burn the heifer as he watches—her hide
Numbers19:6 Eleazar shall take cedar wood and hyssop branches and scarlet thread
Numbers19:7 “Then he must wash his clothes
Numbers19:8 And the one who burns the animal must wash his clothes and bathe
Numbers19:9 Then someone who is not ceremonially defiled shall gather up the ashes of the heifer and place them in some purified place outside the camp
Numbers19:10 And the one who gathers up the ashes of the heifer must wash his clothes and be defiled until evening; this is a permanent law for the benefit of the people of Israel and any foreigners living among them.
Numbers19:11 “Anyone who touches a dead human body shall be defiled for seven days
Numbers19:12 and must purify himself the third and seventh days with water run through the ashes of the red heifer; then he will be purified; but if he does not do this on the third day
Numbers19:13 Anyone who touches a dead person and does not purify himself in the manner specified has defiled the Tabernacle of the Lord
Numbers19:14 “When a man dies in a tent
Numbers19:15 Any container in the tent without a lid over it is defiled.
Numbers19:16 “If someone out in a field touches the corpse of someone who has been killed in battle or who has died in any other way
Numbers19:17 To become purified again
Numbers19:18 Then a person who is not defiled shall take hyssop branches and dip them into the water and sprinkle the water upon the tent and upon all the pots and pans in the tent
Numbers19:19 This shall take place on the third and seventh days; then the defiled person must wash his clothes and bathe himself
Numbers19:20 “But anyone who is defiled and doesn't purify himself shall be excommunicated
Numbers19:21 This is a permanent law. The man who sprinkles the water must afterwards wash his clothes; and anyone touching the water shall be defiled until evening.
Numbers19:22 And anything a defiled person touches shall be defiled until evening.“

Numbers28:1 The Lord gave Moses these instructions to give to the people of Israel:
Numbers28:2 “The offerings which you burn on the altar for me are my food
Numbers28:3 “When you make offerings by fire
Numbers28:4 One lamb shall be sacrificed in the morning
Numbers28:5 With them shall be offered a grain offering of three quarts of finely ground flour mixed with three pints of oil.
Numbers28:6 This is the burnt offering ordained at Mount Sinai
Numbers28:7 Along with it shall be the drink offering
Numbers28:8 Offer the second lamb in the evening with the same grain offering and drink offering. It too is a fragrant odor to the Lord
Numbers28:9 “On the Sabbath day
Numbers28:10 They are to be accompanied by a grain offering of six quarts of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers28:11 “Also
Numbers28:12 Accompany them with nine quarts of finely ground flour mixed with oil as a grain offering with each bull; and six quarts of finely ground flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for the ram;
Numbers28:13 and for each lamb
Numbers28:14 Along with each sacrifice shall be a drink offering—six pints of wine with each bull
Numbers28:15 “Also on the first day of each month you shall offer one male goat for a sin offering to the Lord. This is in addition to the regular daily burnt offering and its drink offering.
Numbers28:16 “On April first you shall celebrate the Passover—when the death angel passed over the oldest sons of the Israelites in Egypt
Numbers28:17 On the following day a great
Numbers28:18 On the first day of the festival all the people shall be called together before the Lord. No hard work shall be done on that day.
Numbers28:19 You shall offer as burnt sacrifices to the Lord two young bulls
Numbers28:20 With each bull there shall be a grain offering of nine quarts of fine flour mixed with oil; with the ram there shall be six quarts;
Numbers28:21 and with each of the seven lambs there shall be three quarts of fine flour.
Numbers28:22 You must also offer a male goat as a sin offering
Numbers28:23 These offerings shall be in addition to the usual daily sacrifices.
Numbers28:24 This same sacrifice shall be offered on each of the seven days of the feast; they will be very pleasant to the Lord.
Numbers28:25 On the seventh day there shall again be a holy and solemn assembly of all the people
Numbers28:26 “On the first day of the Harvest Festival all the people must come before the Lord for a special
Numbers28:27 A special burnt offering
Numbers28:28 These shall be accompanied by your grain offering of nine quarts of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers28:29 with each bull
Numbers28:30 Also offer one male goat to make atonement for yourselves.
Numbers28:31 These special offerings are in addition to the regular daily burnt offerings and grain offerings and drink offerings. Make sure that the animals you sacrifice are without defect.
Numbers29:1 “The Festival of Trumpets shall be celebrated on the fifteenth day of September each year; there shall be a solemn assembly of all the people on that day
Numbers29:2 On that day you shall offer a burnt sacrifice consisting of one young bull
Numbers29:3 A grain offering of nine quarts of fine flour mingled with oil shall be offered with
Numbers29:4 the bull
Numbers29:5 In addition
Numbers29:6 These special sacrifices are in addition to the regular monthly burnt offering for that day

Numbers29:9 and their accompanying grain offerings.
Numbers29:10 Nine quarts of fine flour mixed with oil are to be offered with the bull
Numbers29:11 “You are also to sacrifice one male goat for a sin offering. This is in addition to the sin offering of the Day of Atonement offered annually on that day
Numbers29:12 “Five days later there shall be yet another assembly of all the people

Numbers29:14 accompanied by the usual grain offerings—nine quarts of fine flour mingled with oil for each of the thirteen young bulls; six quarts for each of the two rams;
Numbers29:15 and three quarts for each of the fourteen lambs.
Numbers29:16 There must also be a male goat sacrificed for a sin offering

Numbers29:18 accompanied by the usual grain offerings and drink offerings.
Numbers29:19 Also
Numbers29:20 “On the third day of the festival

Numbers29:22 And in addition to the regular daily burnt sacrifices
Numbers29:23 “On the fourth day of the festival
Numbers29:24 each with its accompanying grain offering and drink offering;
Numbers29:25 also a male goat as a sin offering (along with the usual grain and drink offerings) in addition to the regular daily sacrifices.
Numbers29:26 “On the fifth day of the festival
Numbers29:27 accompanied by the usual grain offerings and drink offerings;
Numbers29:28 also sacrifice a male goat with the usual grain and drink offerings
Numbers29:29 “On the sixth day of the festival
Numbers29:30 along with their usual grain and drink offerings.
Numbers29:31 In addition to the usual daily sacrifices
Numbers29:32 “On the seventh day of the festival
Numbers29:33 each with its customary grain and drink offerings;
Numbers29:34 also sacrifice an extra sin offering of one male goat
Numbers29:35 “On the eighth day summon the people to another solemn assembly; you must do no hard work that day.
Numbers29:36 Sacrifice a burnt offering—they are very pleasant to the Lord—of one young bull
Numbers29:37 and the customary grain and drink offerings.
Numbers29:38 Sacrifice also one male goat with the usual grain and drink offerings for a sin offering
Numbers29:39 These offerings are compulsory at the times of your annual feasts

Numbers30:2 that when anyone makes a promise to the Lord
Numbers30:3 “If a woman promises the Lord to do or not do something
Numbers30:4 and her father hears that she has made a vow with penalties
Numbers30:5 But if her father refuses to let her make the vow
Numbers30:6 “If she takes a vow or makes a foolish pledge
Numbers30:7 and her husband learns of her vow and says nothing on the day he hears of it
Numbers30:8 But if her husband refuses to accept her vow or foolish pledge
Numbers30:9 “But if the woman is a widow or is divorced
Numbers30:10 “If she is married and living in her husband's home when she makes the vow
Numbers30:11 and her husband hears of it and does nothing
Numbers30:12 but if he refuses to allow it on the first day he hears of it
Numbers30:13 So her husband may either confirm or nullify her vow
Numbers30:14 but if he says nothing for a day
Numbers30:15 If he waits more than a day and then refuses to permit the vow

Numbers35:1 While Israel was camped beside the Jordan on the plains of Moab
Numbers35:2 “Instruct the people of Israel to give to the Levites as their inheritance certain cities and surrounding pasture lands.
Numbers35:3 These cities are for their homes
Numbers35:4 Their gardens and vineyards shall extend
Numbers35:5 walls in each direction
Numbers35:6 “You shall give the Levites the six Cities of Refuge
Numbers35:7 In all
Numbers35:8 These cities shall be in various parts of the nation; the larger tribes with many cities will give several to the Levites
Numbers35:9 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers35:10 people that when they arrive in the land
Numbers35:11 Cities of Refuge shall be designated for anyone to flee into if he has killed someone accidentally.
Numbers35:12 These cities will be places of protection from the dead man's relatives who want to avenge his death; for the slayer must not be killed unless a fair trial establishes his guilt.
Numbers35:13 Three of these six Cities of Refuge are to be located in the
Numbers35:14 land of Canaan
Numbers35:15 These are not only for the protection of Israelites
Numbers35:16 “But if someone is struck and killed by a piece of iron
Numbers35:17 Or if the slain man was struck down with a large stone
Numbers35:18 The same is true if he is killed with a wooden weapon.
Numbers35:19 The avenger of his death shall personally kill the murderer when he meets him.
Numbers35:20 So if anyone kills another out of hatred by throwing something at him
Numbers35:21 or angrily striking him with his fist so that he dies
Numbers35:22 “But if it is an accident—a case in which something is thrown unintentionally
Numbers35:23 or in which a stone is thrown without anger
Numbers35:24 then the people shall judge whether or not it was an accident
Numbers35:25 If it is decided that it was accidental
Numbers35:26 “If the slayer leaves the city
Numbers35:27 and the avenger finds him outside and kills him
Numbers35:28 for the man should have stayed inside the city until the death of the High Priest. But after the death of the High Priest
Numbers35:29 These are permanent laws for all Israel from generation to generation.
Numbers35:30 “All murderers must be executed
Numbers35:31 Whenever anyone is judged guilty of murder
Numbers35:32 Nor may a payment be accepted from a refugee in a City of Refuge
Numbers35:33 In this way the land will not be polluted
Numbers35:34 You shall not defile the land where you are going to live

Deuteronomy1:1 This book records Moses' address to the people of Israel when they were camped in the valley of the Arabah in the wilderness of Moab
Deuteronomy1:2 The speech was given on February 15
Deuteronomy1:3 though it takes only eleven days to travel by foot from Mount Horeb to Kadesh-barnea
Deuteronomy1:4 At the time of this address
Deuteronomy1:5 Here
Deuteronomy1:6 “It was forty years ago
Deuteronomy1:7 Now go and occupy the hill country of the Amorites
Deuteronomy1:8 I am giving all of it to you! Go in and possess it
Deuteronomy1:9 “At that time I told the people
Deuteronomy1:10 for the Lord has multiplied you to become as many as the stars!
Deuteronomy1:11 And may he multiply you a thousand times more and bless you as he promised
Deuteronomy1:12 but what can one man do to settle all your quarrels and problems?
Deuteronomy1:13 So choose some men from each tribe who are wise
Deuteronomy1:14 “They agreed to this;
Deuteronomy1:15 I took the men they selected
Deuteronomy1:16 I instructed them to be perfectly fair at all times
Deuteronomy1:17 'When giving your decisions
Deuteronomy1:18 And I gave them other instructions at that time also.
Deuteronomy1:19 “Then we left Mount Horeb and traveled through the great and terrible desert
Deuteronomy1:20 and I said to the people
Deuteronomy1:21 Go and possess it as he told us to. Don't be afraid! Don't even doubt!'
Deuteronomy1:22 “But they replied
Deuteronomy1:23 “This seemed like a good idea
Deuteronomy1:24 They crossed into the hills and came to the Valley of Eshcol
Deuteronomy1:25 and returned with samples of the local fruit. One look was enough to convince us that it was indeed a good land the Lord our God had given us.
Deuteronomy1:26 But the people refused to go in and rebelled against the Lord's command.
Deuteronomy1:27 “They murmured and complained in their tents and said
Deuteronomy1:28 What are we getting into? Our brothers who spied out the land have frightened us with their report. They say that the people of the land are tall and powerful
Deuteronomy1:29 “But I said to them
Deuteronomy1:30 The Lord God is your leader
Deuteronomy1:31 And you know how he has cared for you again and again here in the wilderness
Deuteronomy1:32 But nothing I said did any good. “They refused to believe the Lord our God
Deuteronomy1:33 who had led them all the way
Deuteronomy1:34 “Well
Deuteronomy1:35 He vowed that not one person in that entire generation would live to see the good land he had promised their fathers
Deuteronomy1:36 except Caleb (the son of Jephunneh)
Deuteronomy1:37 “And the Lord was even angry with me because of them and said to me
Deuteronomy1:38 Instead
Deuteronomy1:39 I will give the land to the children they said would die in the wilderness.
Deuteronomy1:40 But as for you of the older generation
Deuteronomy1:41 “Then they confessed
Deuteronomy1:42 “But the Lord said to me
Deuteronomy1:43 “I told them
Deuteronomy1:44 But the Amorites who lived there came out against them and chased them like bees and killed them from Seir to Hormah.
Deuteronomy1:45 Then they returned and wept before the Lord
Deuteronomy1:46 So they stayed there at Kadesh for a long time.

Deuteronomy2:5 Don't start a fight! For I have given them all the Mount Seir hill country as their permanent possession

Deuteronomy16:1 “Always remember to celebrate the Passover during the month of April
Deuteronomy16:2 Your Passover sacrifice shall be either a lamb or an ox
Deuteronomy16:3 Eat the sacrifice with unleavened bread. Eat unleavened bread for seven days as a reminder of the bread you ate as you escaped from Egypt. This is to remind you that you left Egypt in such a hurry that there was no time for the bread to rise. Remember that day all the rest of your lives!
Deuteronomy16:4 For seven days no trace of yeast shall be in your homes
Deuteronomy16:5 “The Passover is not to be eaten in your homes.
Deuteronomy16:6 It must be eaten at the place the Lord shall choose as his sanctuary. Sacrifice it there on the anniversary evening just as the sun goes down.
Deuteronomy16:7 Roast the lamb and eat it
Deuteronomy16:8 For the following six days you shall eat no bread made with yeast. On the seventh day there shall be a quiet gathering of the people of each city before the Lord your God. Don't do any work that day.
Deuteronomy16:9 “Seven weeks after the harvest begins
Deuteronomy16:10 there shall be another festival before the Lord your God called the Festival of Weeks. At that time bring to him a free-will offering proportionate in size to his blessing upon you as judged by the amount of your harvest.
Deuteronomy16:11 It is a time to rejoice before the Lord with your family and household. And don't forget to include the local Levites
Deuteronomy16:12 Remember! You were a slave in Egypt
Deuteronomy16:13 “Another celebration
Deuteronomy16:14 This will be a happy time of rejoicing together with your family and servants. And don't forget to include the Levites
Deuteronomy16:15 “This feast will be held at the sanctuary
Deuteronomy16:16 “Every man in Israel shall appear before the Lord your God three times a year at the sanctuary for these festivals: The Festival of Unleavened Bread
Deuteronomy16:17 Give as you are able
Deuteronomy16:18 “Appoint judges and administrative officials for all the cities the Lord your God is giving you. They will administer justice in every part of the land.
Deuteronomy16:19 Never twist justice to benefit a rich man
Deuteronomy16:20 Justice must prevail. “That is the only way you will be successful in the land that the Lord your God is giving you.

Deuteronomy28:8 The Lord will bless you with good crops and healthy cattle
Deuteronomy28:9 He will change you into a holy people dedicated to himself; this he has promised to do if you will only obey him and walk in his ways.
Deuteronomy28:10 All the nations in the world shall see that you belong to the Lord
Deuteronomy28:11 “The Lord will give you an abundance of good things in the land
Deuteronomy28:12 He will open to you his wonderful treasury of rain in the heavens
Deuteronomy28:13 If you will only listen and obey the commandments of the Lord your God that I am giving you today
Deuteronomy28:14 But each of these blessings depends on your not turning aside in any way from the laws I have given you; and you must never worship other gods.
