Deuteronomy28:21 He will send disease among you until you are destroyed from the face of the land you are about to enter and possess.
Deuteronomy28:22 He will send tuberculosis
Deuteronomy28:23 “The heavens above you will be as unyielding as bronze
Deuteronomy28:24 The land will become as dry as dust for lack of rain
Deuteronomy28:25 “The Lord will cause you to be defeated by your enemies. You will march out to battle gloriously
Deuteronomy28:26 Your dead bodies will be food to the birds and wild animals
Deuteronomy28:27 “He will send upon you Egyptian boils
Deuteronomy28:28 He will send madness
Deuteronomy28:29 You shall grope in the bright sunlight just as the blind man gropes in darkness. You shall not prosper in anything you do; you will be oppressed and robbed continually
Deuteronomy28:30 “Someone else will marry your fiancee; someone else will live in the house you build; someone else will eat the fruit of the vineyard you plant.
Deuteronomy28:31 Your oxen shall be butchered before your eyes
Deuteronomy28:32 You will watch as your sons and daughters are taken away as slaves. Your heart will break with longing for them
Deuteronomy28:33 A foreign nation you have not even heard of will eat the crops you will have worked so hard to grow. You will always be oppressed and crushed.
Deuteronomy28:34 You will go mad because of all the tragedy you see around you.
Deuteronomy28:35 The Lord will cover you with boils from head to foot.
Deuteronomy28:36 “He will exile you and the king you will choose to a nation to whom neither you nor your ancestors gave a second thought; and while in exile you shall worship gods of wood and stone!
Deuteronomy28:37 You will become an object of horror
Deuteronomy28:38 “You will sow much but reap little
Deuteronomy28:39 You will plant vineyards and care for them
Deuteronomy28:40 Olive trees will be growing everywhere
Deuteronomy28:41 Your sons and daughters will be snatched away from you as slaves.
Deuteronomy28:42 The locusts shall destroy your trees and vines.
Deuteronomy28:43 Foreigners living among you shall become richer and richer while you become poorer and poorer.
Deuteronomy28:44 They shall lend to you
Deuteronomy28:45 “All these curses shall pursue and overtake you until you are destroyed—all because you refuse to listen to the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy28:46 These horrors shall befall you and your descendants as a warning:
Deuteronomy28:47 You will become slaves to your enemies because of your failure to praise God for all that he has given you.
Deuteronomy28:48 The Lord will send your enemies against you
Deuteronomy28:49 “The Lord will bring a distant nation against you
Deuteronomy28:50 a nation of fierce and angry men who will have no mercy upon young or old.
Deuteronomy28:51 They will eat you out of house and home until your cattle and crops are gone. Your grain
Deuteronomy28:52 That nation will lay siege to your cities and knock down your highest walls—the walls you will trust to protect you.
Deuteronomy28:53 You will even eat the flesh of your own sons and daughters in the terrible days of siege that lie ahead.
Deuteronomy28:54 The most tenderhearted man among you will be utterly callous toward his own brother and his beloved wife and his children who are still alive.
Deuteronomy28:55 He will refuse to give them a share of the flesh he is devouring—the flesh of his own children—because he is starving in the midst of the siege of your cities.
Deuteronomy28:56 The most tender and delicate woman among you—the one who would not so much as touch her feet to the ground—will refuse to share with her beloved husband
Deuteronomy28:57 She will hide from them the afterbirth and the new baby she has borne
Deuteronomy28:58 “If you refuse to obey all the laws written in this book
Deuteronomy28:59 then Jehovah will send perpetual plagues upon you and upon your children.

Joshua9:14 Joshua and the other leaders finally believed them. They did not bother to ask the Lord
Joshua9:15 but went ahead and signed a peace treaty. And the leaders of Israel ratified the agreement with a binding oath.
Joshua9:16 Three days later the facts came out—these men were close neighbors.
Joshua9:17 The Israeli army set out at once to investigate and reached their cities in three days. (The names of the cities were Gibeon
Joshua9:18 But the cities were not harmed because of the vow which the leaders of Israel had made before the Lord God. The people of Israel were angry with their leaders because of the peace treaty.
Joshua9:19 But the leaders replied
Joshua9:20 We must let them live
Joshua9:21 So they became servants of the Israelis
Joshua9:22 Joshua summoned their leaders and demanded
Joshua9:23 Now a curse shall be upon you! From this moment you must always furnish us with servants to chop wood and carry water for the service of our God.“
Joshua9:24 They replied
Joshua9:25 But now we are in your hands; you may do with us as you wish.“
Joshua9:26 So Joshua would not allow the people of Israel to kill them
Joshua9:27 but they became woodchoppers and water-carriers for the people of Israel and for the altar of the Lord—wherever it would be built (for the Lord hadn't yet told them where to build it). This arrangement is still in force at the time of this writing.
Joshua10:1 When Adoni-zedek
Joshua10:2 he was very frightened. For Gibeon was a great city—as great as the royal cities and much larger than Ai—and its men were known as hard fighters.
Joshua10:3 So King Adoni-zedek of Jerusalem sent messengers to several other kings: King Hoham of Hebron
Joshua10:4 “Come and help me destroy Gibeon
Joshua10:5 So these five Amorite kings combined their armies for a united attack on Gibeon.
Joshua10:6 The men of Gibeon hurriedly sent messengers to Joshua at Gilgal. “Come and help your servants!“ they demanded. “Come quickly and save us! For all the kings of the Amorites who live in the hills are here with their armies.“
Joshua10:7 So Joshua and the Israeli army left Gilgal and went to rescue Gibeon.
Joshua10:8 “Don't be afraid of them
Joshua10:9 Joshua traveled all night from Gilgal and took the enemy armies by surprise.
Joshua10:10 Then the Lord threw them into a panic so that the army of Israel slaughtered great numbers of them at Gibeon and chased the others all the way to Beth-horon and Azekah and Makkedah
Joshua10:11 And as the enemy was racing down the hill to Beth-horon
Joshua10:12 As the men of Israel were pursuing and harassing the foe
Joshua10:13 And the sun and the moon didn't move until the Israeli army had finished the destruction of its enemies! This is described in greater detail in The Book of Jashar. So the sun stopped in the heavens and stayed there for almost twenty-four hours!
Joshua10:14 There had never been such a day before
Joshua10:15 (Afterwards Joshua and the Israeli army returned to Gilgal.)
Joshua10:16 During the battle the five kings escaped and hid in a cave at Makkedah.
Joshua10:17 When the news was brought to Joshua that they had been found
Joshua10:18 he issued a command that a great stone be rolled against the mouth of the cave and that guards be placed there to keep the kings inside.
Joshua10:19 Then Joshua commanded the rest of the army
Joshua10:20 So Joshua and the Israeli army continued the slaughter and wiped out the five armies except for a tiny remnant that managed to reach their fortified cities.
Joshua10:21 Then the Israelis returned to their camp at Makkedah without having lost a single man! And after that no one dared to attack Israel.
Joshua10:22 Joshua now instructed his men to remove the stone from the mouth of the
Joshua10:23 cave and to bring out the five kings—of Jerusalem
Joshua10:24 Joshua told the captains of his army to put their feet on the kings' necks.
Joshua10:25 “Don't ever be afraid or discouraged
Joshua10:26 With that
Joshua10:27 As the sun was going down
Joshua10:28 On that same day Joshua destroyed the city of Makkedah and killed its king and everyone in it. Not one person in the entire city was left alive.
Joshua10:29 Then the Israelis went to Libnah.
Joshua10:30 There
Joshua10:31 From Libnah they went to Lachish and attacked it.
Joshua10:32 And the Lord gave it to them on the second day; here
Joshua10:33 During the attack on Lachish
Joshua10:34 The Israeli army then captured Eglon on the first day
Joshua10:35 and
Joshua10:36 After leaving Eglon they went to Hebron
Joshua10:37 and captured it and all of its surrounding villages
Joshua10:38 Then they turned back to Debir
Joshua10:39 which they quickly captured with all of its outlying villages. And they killed everyone just as they had at Libnah.
Joshua10:40 So Joshua and his army conquered the whole country—the nations and kings of the hill country
Joshua10:41 slaughtering them from Kadesh-barnea to Gaza
Joshua10:42 This was all accomplished in one campaign
Joshua10:43 Then Joshua and his army returned to their camp at Gilgal.

1Samuel1:1 This is the story of Elkanah
1Samuel1:2 He had two wives
1Samuel1:3 Each year Elkanah and his families journeyed to the Tabernacle at Shiloh to worship the Lord of the heavens and to sacrifice to him. (The priests on duty at that time were the two sons of Eli—Hophni and Phinehas.)
1Samuel1:4 On the day he presented his sacrifice
1Samuel1:5 but although he loved Hannah very much
1Samuel1:6 Peninnah made matters worse by taunting Hannah because of her barrenness.

1Samuel1:8 “What's the matter

1Samuel1:10 She was in deep anguish and was crying bitterly as she prayed to the Lord.

1Samuel2:1 This was Hannah's prayer: “How I rejoice in the Lord! How he has blessed me! Now I have an answer for my enemies
1Samuel2:2 No one is as holy as the Lord! There is no other God
1Samuel2:3 Quit acting so proud and arrogant! The Lord knows what you have done
1Samuel2:4 Those who were mighty are mighty no more! Those who were weak are now strong.
1Samuel2:5 Those who were well are now starving; Those who were starving are fed. The barren woman now has seven children; She with many children has no more!
1Samuel2:6 The Lord kills
1Samuel2:7 Some he causes to be poor And others to be rich. He cuts one down And lifts another up.
1Samuel2:8 He lifts the poor from the dust—Yes
1Samuel2:9 He will protect his godly ones
1Samuel2:10 Those who fight against the Lord shall be broken; He thunders against them from heaven. He judges throughout the earth. He gives mighty strength to his King
1Samuel2:11 So they returned home to Ramah without Samuel; and the child became the Lord's helper
1Samuel2:12 Now the sons of Eli were evil men who didn't love the Lord.
1Samuel2:13 It was their regular practice to send out a servant whenever anyone was offering a sacrifice
1Samuel2:14 the servant would put a three-pronged fleshhook into the pot and demand that whatever it brought up be given to Eli's sons. They treated all of the Israelites in this way when they came to Shiloh to worship.
1Samuel2:15 Sometimes the servant would come even before the rite of burning the fat on the altar had been performed
1Samuel2:16 If the man offering the sacrifice replied
1Samuel2:17 So the sin of these young men was very great in the eyes of the Lord; for they treated the people's offerings to the Lord with contempt.
1Samuel2:18 Samuel
1Samuel2:19 Each year his mother made a little coat for him and brought it to him when she came with her husband for the sacrifice.
1Samuel2:20 Before they returned home Eli would bless Elkanah and Hannah and ask God to give them other children to take the place of this one they had given to the Lord.
1Samuel2:21 And the Lord gave Hannah three sons and two daughters. Meanwhile Samuel grew up in the service of the Lord.
1Samuel2:22 Eli was now very old
1Samuel2:23 “I have been hearing terrible reports from the Lord's people about what you are doing
1Samuel2:24 “It is an awful thing to make the Lord's people sin.
1Samuel2:25 Ordinary sin receives heavy punishment

1Samuel2:27 One day a prophet came to Eli and gave him this message from the Lord: “Didn't I demonstrate my power when the people of Israel were slaves in Egypt?
1Samuel2:28 Didn't I choose your ancestor Levi from among all his brothers to be my priest
1Samuel2:29 Then why are you so greedy for all the other offerings which are brought to me? Why have you honored your sons more than me—for you and they have become fat from the best of the offerings of my people!

1Samuel2:31 I will put an end to your family

1Samuel5:2 Ebenezer to the temple of their idol Dagon in the city of Ashdod.
1Samuel5:3 But when the local citizens went to see it the next morning
1Samuel5:4 but the next morning the same thing had happened—the idol had fallen face down before the Ark of the Lord again. This time his head and hands had been cut off and were lying in the doorway; only the trunk of his body was left intact.
1Samuel5:5 (That is why to this day neither the priests of Dagon nor his worshipers will walk on the threshold of the temple of Dagon in Ashdod.)

1Samuel6:12 And sure enough
1Samuel6:13 The people of Beth-shemesh were reaping wheat in the valley
1Samuel6:14 The cart came into the field of a man named Joshua and stopped beside a large rock. So the people broke up the wood of the cart for a fire and killed the cows and sacrificed them to the Lord as a burnt offering.
1Samuel6:15 Several men of the tribe of Levi lifted the Ark and the chest containing the gold rats and tumors from the cart and laid them on the rock. And many burnt offerings and sacrifices were offered to the Lord that day by the men of Beth-shemesh.
1Samuel6:16 After the five Philistine mayors had watched for awhile

1Samuel6:18 The gold rats were to placate God for the other Philistine cities
1Samuel6:19 But the Lord killed seventy of the men of Beth-shemesh because they looked into the Ark. And the people mourned because of the many people whom the Lord had killed.

1Kings4:1 Here is a list of King Solomon's cabinet members:
1Kings4:2 Azariah (son of Zadok) was the High Priest;
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah (sons of Shisha) were secretaries; Jehoshaphat (son of Ahilud) was the official historian and in charge of the archives;
1Kings4:4 Benaiah (son of Jehoiada) was commander-in-chief of the army; Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
1Kings4:5 Azariah (son of Nathan) was secretary of state; Zabud (son of Nathan) was the king's personal priest and special friend;
1Kings4:6 Ahishar was manager of palace affairs; Adoniram (son of Abda) was superintendent of public works.
1Kings4:7 There were also twelve officials of Solomon's court—one man from each tribe—responsible for requisitioning food from the people for the king's household. Each of them arranged provisions for one month of the year.
1Kings4:8 The names of these twelve officers were: Ben-hur
1Kings4:9 Ben-deker
1Kings4:10 Ben-hesed
1Kings4:11 Ben-abinadab (who married Solomon's daughter
1Kings4:12 Baana (son of Ahilud)
1Kings4:13 Ben-geber
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab (the son of Iddo)
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz (who married Princess Basemath
1Kings4:16 Baana (son of Hushai)
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat (son of Paruah)
1Kings4:18 Shimei (son of Ela)
1Kings4:19 Geber (son of Uri)
1Kings4:20 Israel and Judah were a wealthy
1Kings4:21 King Solomon ruled the whole area from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines and down to the borders of Egypt. The conquered peoples of those lands sent taxes to Solomon and continued to serve him throughout his lifetime.
1Kings4:22 The daily food requirements for the palace were 195 bushels of fine flour
1Kings4:23 10 oxen from the fattening pens
1Kings4:24 His dominion extended over all the kingdoms west of the Euphrates River
1Kings4:25 Throughout the lifetime of Solomon
1Kings4:26 Solomon owned forty thousand chariot horses and employed twelve thousand charioteers.
1Kings4:27 Each month the tax officials provided food for King Solomon and his court
1Kings4:28 also the barley and straw for the royal horses in the stables.

1Kings4:30 In fact

1Kings4:32 He was the author of 3
1Kings4:33 He was a great naturalist

1Kings5:1 King Hiram of Tyre had always been a great admirer of David
1Kings5:2 Solomon replied with a proposal about the Temple of the Lord he wanted to build.
1Kings5:3 His father David
1Kings5:4 “But now
1Kings5:5 So I am planning to build a Temple for the Lord my God
1Kings5:6 Now please assist me with this project. Send your woodsmen to the mountains of Lebanon to cut cedar timber for me
1Kings5:7 Hiram was very pleased with the message from Solomon. “Praise God for giving David a wise son to be king of the great nation of Israel
1Kings5:8 Then he sent this reply to Solomon: “I have received your message and I will do as you have asked concerning the timber. I can supply both cedar and cypress.
1Kings5:9 My men will bring the logs from the Lebanon mountains to the Mediterranean Sea and build them into rafts. We will float them along the coast to wherever you need them; then we will break the rafts apart and deliver the timber to you. You can pay me with food for my household.“
1Kings5:10 So Hiram produced for Solomon as much cedar and cypress timber as he desired
1Kings5:11 and in return Solomon sent him an annual payment of 125
1Kings5:12 So the Lord gave great wisdom to Solomon just as he had promised. And Hiram and Solomon made a formal alliance of peace.
1Kings5:13 Then Solomon drafted thirty thousand laborers from all over Israel
1Kings5:14 and rotated them to Lebanon
1Kings5:15 Solomon also had seventy thousand additional laborers
1Kings5:16 and thirty-three hundred foremen.
1Kings5:17 The stonecutters quarried and shaped huge blocks of stone—a very expensive job—for the foundation of the Temple.
1Kings5:18 Men from Gebal helped Solomon's and Hiram's builders in cutting the timber and making the boards
1Kings6:1 It was in the spring of the fourth year of Solomon's reign that he began the actual construction of the Temple. (This was 480 years after the people of Israel left their slavery in Egypt.)
1Kings6:2 The Temple was ninety feet long
1Kings6:3 All along the front of the Temple was a porch thirty feet long and fifteen feet deep.
1Kings6:4 Narrow windows were used throughout.
1Kings6:5 An annex of rooms was built along the full length of both sides of the Temple against the outer walls.
1Kings6:6 These rooms were three stories high
1Kings6:7 The stones used in the construction of the Temple were prefinished at the quarry
1Kings6:8 The bottom floor of the side rooms was entered from the right side of the Temple
1Kings6:9 After completing the Temple
1Kings6:10 As already stated
1Kings6:11 Then the Lord sent this message to Solomon concerning the Temple he was building:
1Kings6:12 “If you do as I tell you to and follow all of my commandments and instructions
1Kings6:13 I will live among the people of Israel and never forsake them.“
1Kings6:14 At last the Temple was finished.
1Kings6:15 The entire inside
1Kings6:16 The thirty-foot inner room at the far end of the Temple—the Most Holy Place—was also paneled from the floor to the ceiling with cedar boards.
1Kings6:17 The remainder of the Temple—other than the Most Holy Place—was sixty feet long.
1Kings6:18 Throughout the Temple the cedar paneling laid over the stone walls was carved with designs of rosebuds and open flowers.
1Kings6:19 The inner room was where the Ark of the Covenant of the Lord was placed.
1Kings6:20 This inner sanctuary was thirty feet long
1Kings6:21 Then he overlaid the interior of the remainder of the Temple—
1Kings6:22 including the cedar altar—with pure gold; and he made gold chains to protect the entrance to the Most Holy Place.
1Kings6:23 Within the inner sanctuary Solomon placed two statues of Guardian Angels made from olive wood
1Kings6:24 They were placed so that their outspread wings reached from wall to wall
1Kings6:25 while their inner wings touched each other at the center of the room;
1Kings6:26 each wing was 7 1/2 feet long
1Kings6:27 from wing tip to wing tip. The two angels were identical in all dimensions
1Kings6:28 and each was overlaid with gold.
1Kings6:29 Figures of angels
1Kings6:30 and the floor of both rooms was overlaid with gold.
1Kings6:31 The doorway to the inner sanctuary was a five-sided opening
1Kings6:32 and its two olive-wood doors were carved with Guardian Angels
1Kings6:33 Then he made square doorposts of olive wood for the entrance to the Temple.
1Kings6:34 There were two folding doors of cypress wood
1Kings6:35 Angels
1Kings6:36 The wall of the inner court had three layers of hewn stone and one layer of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 The foundation of the Temple was laid in the month of May in the fourth year of Solomon's reign
1Kings6:38 and the entire building was completed in every detail in November of the eleventh year of his reign. So it took seven years to build.
1Kings7:1 Then Solomon built his own palace
1Kings7:2 One of the rooms in the palace was called the Hall of the Forest of Lebanon. It was huge—measuring 150 feet long
1Kings7:3 There were forty-five windows in the hall
1Kings7:4 above the other
1Kings7:5 Each of the doorways and windows had a square frame.
1Kings7:6 Another room was called the Hall of Pillars. It was seventy-five feet long and forty-five feet wide
1Kings7:7 There was also the Throne Room or Judgment Hall
1Kings7:8 His cedar-paneled living quarters surrounded a courtyard behind this hall. (He designed similar living quarters
1Kings7:9 These buildings were constructed entirely from huge
1Kings7:10 The foundation stones were twelve to fifteen feet across.
1Kings7:11 The huge stones in the walls were also cut to measure and were topped with cedar beams.
1Kings7:12 The Great Court had three courses of hewn stone in its walls
1Kings7:13 King Solomon then asked for a man named Hiram to come from Tyre
1Kings7:14 He was half Jewish
1Kings7:15 He cast two hollow bronze pillars
1Kings7:16 At the tops of the pillars
1Kings7:17 he made two lily-shaped capitals from molten
1Kings7:18 bronze
1Kings7:19 The upper part of each capital was
1Kings7:20 shaped like a lily
1Kings7:21 Each capital was decorated with seven sets of bronze
1Kings7:22 Hiram set these pillars at the entrance of the Temple. The one on the south was named the Jachin Pillar
1Kings7:23 Then Hiram cast a round bronze tank
1Kings7:24 On the underside of the rim were two rows of ornaments an inch or two apart
1Kings7:25 It rested on twelve bronze oxen standing tail to tail
1Kings7:26 The sides of the tank were four inches thick; its brim was shaped like a goblet
1Kings7:27 Then he made ten four-wheeled movable stands
1Kings7:28 They were constructed with undercarriages braced with square crosspieces.
1Kings7:29 These crosspieces were decorated with carved lions
1Kings7:30 Each of these movable stands had four bronze wheels and bronze axles
1Kings7:31 The top of each stand was a round piece 1 1/2 feet high. Its center was concave
1Kings7:32 The stands rode on four wheels which were connected to axles that had been cast as part of the stands. The wheels were twenty-seven inches high
1Kings7:33 and were similar to chariot wheels. All the parts of the stands were cast from molten bronze
1Kings7:34 There were supports at each of the four corners of the stands
1Kings7:35 A nine-inch rim surrounded the tip of each stand
1Kings7:36 Guardian Angels
1Kings7:37 All ten stands were the same size and were made alike
1Kings7:38 Then he made ten brass vats
1Kings7:39 Five of these vats were arranged on the left and five on the right-hand side of the room. The tank was in the southeast corner
1Kings7:40 Hiram also made the necessary pots
1Kings7:41 Here is a list of the items he made: Two pillars; A capital at the top of each pillar; Latticework covering the bases of the capitals of each pillar;
1Kings7:42 Four hundred pomegranates in two rows on the latticework
1Kings7:43 Ten movable stands holding ten vats;
1Kings7:44 One large tank and twelve oxen supporting it;
1Kings7:45 Pots; Shovels; Basins. All these items were made of burnished bronze
1Kings7:46 and were cast at the plains of the Jordan River between Succoth and Zarethan.
1Kings7:47 The total weight of these pieces was not known because they were too heavy to weigh!
1Kings7:48 All the utensils and furniture used in the Temple were made of solid gold. This included the altar
1Kings7:49 the lampstands (five on the right-hand side and five on the left
1Kings7:50 cups
1Kings7:51 When the Temple was finally finished
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon called a convocation at Jerusalem of all the leaders of Israel—the heads of the tribes and clans—to observe the transferring of the Ark of the Covenant of the Lord from the Tabernacle in Zion
1Kings8:2 This celebration occurred at the time of the Tabernacle Festival in the month of October.
1Kings8:3 During the festivities the priests carried the Ark to the Temple
1Kings8:4 with all the sacred vessels that had previously been in the Tabernacle.
1Kings8:5 King Solomon and all the people gathered before the Ark
1Kings8:6 Then the priests took the Ark into the inner sanctuary of the Temple—the Most Holy Place—and placed it under the wings of the statues of the mighty angels.
1Kings8:7 The angels had been constructed in such a manner that their wings spread out over the spot where the Ark would be placed; so now their wings overshadowed the Ark and its carrying poles.
1Kings8:8 The poles were so long that they stuck out past the angels and could be seen from the next room
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the Ark at that time except the two stone tablets that Moses had placed there at Mount Horeb at the time the Lord made his covenant with the people of Israel after they left Egypt.
1Kings8:10 Look! As the priests are returning from the inner sanctuary
1Kings8:11 The priests have to go outside because the glory of the Lord is filling the entire building!

1Kings8:28 And yet
1Kings8:29 Please watch over this Temple night and day—this place you have promised to live in—and as I face toward the Temple and pray
1Kings8:30 Listen to every plea of the people of Israel whenever they face this place to pray; yes
1Kings8:31 “If a man is accused of doing something wrong and then
1Kings8:32 hear him in heaven and do what is right; judge whether or not he did it.
1Kings8:33 “And when your people sin and their enemies defeat them
1Kings8:34 hear them from heaven and forgive them if they turn to you again and confess that you are their God. Bring them back again to this land which you have given to their fathers.
1Kings8:35 “And when the skies are shut up and there is no rain because of their sin
1Kings8:36 hear them from heaven and forgive them when they pray toward this place and confess your name. And after you have punished them
1Kings8:37 “If there is a famine in the land caused by plant disease or locusts or caterpillars
1Kings8:38 then when the people realize their sin and pray toward this Temple
1Kings8:39 hear them from heaven and forgive and answer all who have made an honest confession; for you know each heart.
1Kings8:40 In this way they will always learn to reverence you as they continue to live in this land that you have given their fathers.
1Kings8:41 “And when foreigners hear of your great name and come from distant lands to worship you
1Kings8:42 (for they shall hear of your great name and mighty miracles) and pray toward this Temple
1Kings8:43 hear them from heaven and answer their prayers. And all the nations of the earth will know and fear your name just as your own people Israel do; and all the earth will know that this is your Temple.
1Kings8:44 “When you send your people out to battle against their enemies and they pray to you
1Kings8:45 hear their prayer and help them.
1Kings8:46 “If they sin against you (and who doesn't?) and you become angry with them and let their enemies lead them away as captives to some foreign land
1Kings8:47 and they come to their senses and turn to you and cry to you saying
1Kings8:48 if they honestly return to you and pray toward this land that you have given their fathers
1Kings8:49 hear their prayers and pleadings from heaven where you live
1Kings8:50 “Forgive your people for all of their evil deeds
1Kings8:51 for they are your people—your inheritance that you brought out from the Egyptian furnace.
1Kings8:52 May your eyes be open and your ears listening to their pleas. O Lord
1Kings8:53 for when you brought our fathers out of the land of Egypt
1Kings8:54 Solomon had been kneeling with his hands outstretched toward heaven. As he finished this prayer
1Kings8:55 he rose from before the altar of Jehovah and cried out this blessing upon all the people of Israel:
1Kings8:56 “Blessed be the Lord who has fulfilled his promise and given rest to his people Israel; not one word has failed of all the wonderful promises proclaimed by his servant Moses.
1Kings8:57 May the Lord our God be with us as he was with our fathers; may he never forsake us.
1Kings8:58 May he give us the desire to do his will in everything

2Kings17:12 Yes
2Kings17:13 Again and again the Lord had sent prophets to warn both Israel and Judah to turn from their evil ways; he had warned them to obey his commandments which he had given to their ancestors through these prophets

2Kings17:19 But even Judah refused to obey the commandments of the Lord their God; they too walked in the same evil paths as Israel had.

2Kings17:21 For Israel split off from the kingdom of David and chose Jeroboam I (the son of Nebat) as its king. Then Jeroboam drew Israel away from following the Lord. He made them sin a great sin
2Kings17:22 and the people of Israel never quit doing the evil things that Jeroboam led them into
2Kings17:23 until the Lord finally swept them away

2Kings17:28 So one of them returned to Bethel and taught the colonists from Babylon how to worship the Lord.
2Kings17:29 But these foreigners also worshiped their own gods. They placed them in the shrines on the hills near their cities.
2Kings17:30 Those from Babylon worshiped idols of their god Succoth-benoth; those from Cuth worshiped their god Nergal; and the men of Hamath worshiped Ashima.
2Kings17:31 The gods Nibhaz and Tartak were worshiped by the Avvites
2Kings17:32 They also worshiped the Lord
2Kings17:33 But they continued to follow the religious customs of the nations from which they came.
2Kings17:34 And this is still going on among them today—they follow their former practices instead of truly worshiping the Lord or obeying the laws he gave to the descendants of Jacob (whose name was later changed to Israel).
2Kings17:35 For the Lord had made a contract with them—that they were never to worship or make sacrifices to any heathen gods.
2Kings17:36 They were to worship only the Lord who had brought them out of the land of Egypt with such tremendous miracles and power.
2Kings17:37 The descendants of Jacob were to obey all of God's laws and never worship other gods.
2Kings17:38 For God had said
2Kings17:39 You must worship only the Lord; he will save you from all your enemies.“
2Kings17:40 But Israel didn't listen
2Kings17:41 These colonists from Babylon worshiped the Lord

2Kings20:2 Hezekiah turned his face to the wall.

2Kings20:4 So before Isaiah had left the courtyard
2Kings20:5 “Go back to Hezekiah
2Kings20:6 I will add fifteen years to his life and save him and this city from the king of Assyria. And it will all be done for the glory of my own name and for the sake of my servant David.“
2Kings20:7 Isaiah then instructed Hezekiah to boil some dried figs and to make a paste of them and spread it on the boil. And he recovered!
2Kings20:8 Meanwhile
2Kings20:9 “All right
2Kings20:10 “The shadow always moves forward
2Kings20:11 So Isaiah asked the Lord to do this
2Kings20:12 At that time Merodach-baladan (the son of King Baladan of Babylon) sent ambassadors with greetings and a present to Hezekiah
2Kings20:13 Hezekiah welcomed them and showed them all his treasures—the silver
2Kings20:14 Then Isaiah went to King Hezekiah and asked him
2Kings20:15 “What have they seen in your palace?“ Isaiah asked. And Hezekiah replied
2Kings20:16 Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah
2Kings20:17 The time will come when everything in this palace shall be carried to Babylon. All the treasures of your ancestors will be taken—nothing shall be left.
2Kings20:18 Some of your own sons will be taken away and made into eunuchs who will serve in the palace of the king of Babylon.“
2Kings20:19 “All right
2Kings20:20 The rest of the history of Hezekiah and his great deeds—including the pool and conduit he made and how he brought water into the city—are recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah.
2Kings20:21 When he died
2Kings21:1 New king of Judah: Manasseh His age at the beginning of his reign: 12 years old Length of reign: 55 years
2Kings21:2 Character of his reign: evil; he did the same things the nations had done that were thrown out of the land to make room for the people of Israel
2Kings21:3 He rebuilt the hilltop shrines that his father Hezekiah had destroyed.
2Kings21:4 He built altars for Baal and made a shameful Asherah idol
2Kings21:5 Heathen altars to the sun god
2Kings21:6 And he sacrificed one of his sons as a burnt offering on a heathen altar. He practiced black magic and used fortune-telling
2Kings21:7 Manasseh even set up a shameful Asherah-idol in the Temple—the very place that the Lord had spoken to David and Solomon about when he said
2Kings21:8 If the people of Israel will only follow the instructions I gave them through Moses
2Kings21:9 But the people did not listen to the Lord
2Kings21:10 Then the Lord declared through the prophets
2Kings21:11 “Because King Manasseh has done these evil things and is even more wicked than the Amorites who were in this land long ago
2Kings21:12 I will bring such evil upon Jerusalem and Judah that the ears of those who hear about it will tingle with horror.
2Kings21:13 I will punish Jerusalem as I did Samaria
2Kings21:14 Then I will reject even those few of my people who are left
2Kings21:15 For they have done great evil and have angered me ever since I brought their ancestors from Egypt.“
2Kings21:16 In addition to the idolatry which God hated and into which Manasseh led the people of Judah
2Kings21:17 The rest of the history of Manasseh's sinful reign is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah.
2Kings21:18 When he died he was buried in the garden of his palace at Uzza
2Kings21:19 New king of Judah: Amon His age at the beginning of his reign: 22 years old Length of reign: 2 years
2Kings21:20 Character of his reign: evil
2Kings21:21 He did all the evil things his father had done: he worshiped the same idols
2Kings21:22 and turned his back on the Lord God of his ancestors. He refused to listen to God's instructions.
2Kings21:23 But his aides conspired against him and killed him in the palace.
2Kings21:24 Then a posse of civilians killed all the assassins and placed Amon's son Josiah upon the throne.
2Kings21:25 The rest of Amon's biography is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah.
2Kings21:26 He was buried in a crypt in the garden of Uzza
2Kings22:1 New king of Judah: Josiah His age at the beginning of his reign: 8 years old Length of reign: 31 years
2Kings22:2 Character of his reign: good; he followed in the steps of his ancestor King David
2Kings22:3 In the eighteenth year of his reign
2Kings22:4 “Collect the money given to the priests at the door of the Temple when the people come to worship.
2Kings22:5 Give this money to the building superintendents so that they can hire
2Kings22:6 carpenters and masons to repair the Temple
2Kings22:7 (The building superintendents were not required to keep account of their expenditures
2Kings22:8 One day Hilkiah the High Priest went to Shaphan the secretary and exclaimed
2Kings22:9 When Shaphan reported to the king about the progress of the repairs at the Temple
2Kings22:10 Then Shaphan read it to the king.
2Kings22:11 When the king heard what was written in it
2Kings22:12 He commanded Hilkiah the priest
2Kings22:13 ask the Lord
2Kings22:14 So Hilkiah the priest
2Kings22:15 She gave them this message from the Lord God of Israel: “Tell the man who sent you to me
2Kings22:16 that I am going to destroy this city and its people
2Kings22:17 For the people of Judah have thrown me aside and have worshiped other gods and have made me very angry; and my anger can't be stopped.
2Kings22:18 But because you were sorry and concerned
2Kings22:19 and humbled yourself before the Lord when you read the book and its warnings that this land would be cursed and become desolate
2Kings22:20 The death of this nation will not occur until after you die—you will not see the evil that I will bring upon this place.“ So they took the message to the king.
2Kings23:1 Then the king sent for the elders and other leaders of Judah and Jerusalem to go to the Temple with him.
2Kings23:2 So all the priests and prophets and the people
2Kings23:3 He stood beside the pillar in front of the people
2Kings23:4 Then the king instructed Hilkiah the High Priest and the rest of the priests and the guards of the Temple to destroy all the equipment used in the worship of Baal
2Kings23:5 He killed the heathen priests who had been appointed by the previous kings of Judah
2Kings23:6 He removed the shameful idol of Asherah from the Temple and took it outside Jerusalem to Kidron Brook; there he burned it and beat it to dust and threw the dust on the graves of the common people.
2Kings23:7 He also tore down the houses of male prostitution around the Temple
2Kings23:8 He brought back to Jerusalem the priests of the Lord
2Kings23:9 However
2Kings23:10 Then the king destroyed the altar of Topheth in the Valley of the Sons of Hinnom
2Kings23:11 He tore down the statues of horses and chariots located near the entrance of the Temple
2Kings23:12 Then he tore down the altars that the kings of Judah had built on the palace roof above the Ahaz Room. He also destroyed the altars that Manasseh had built in the two courts of the Temple; he smashed them to bits and scattered the pieces in Kidron Valley.
2Kings23:13 Next he removed the shrines on the hills east of Jerusalem and south of Destruction Mountain. (Solomon had built these shrines for Ashtoreth
2Kings23:14 He smashed the obelisks and cut down the shameful idols of Asherah; then he defiled these places by scattering human bones over them.
2Kings23:15 He also tore down the altar and shrine at Bethel that Jeroboam I had made when he led Israel into sin. He crushed the stones to dust and burned the shameful idol of Asherah.
2Kings23:16 As Josiah was looking around
2Kings23:17 “What is that monument over there?“ he asked. And the men of the city told him
2Kings23:18 So King Josiah replied
2Kings23:19 Josiah demolished the shrines on the hills in all of Samaria. They had been built by the various kings of Israel and had made the Lord very angry. But now he crushed them into dust
2Kings23:20 He executed the priests of the heathen shrines upon their own altars
2Kings23:21 The king then issued orders for his people to observe the Passover ceremonies as recorded by the Lord their God in The Book of the Covenant.
2Kings23:22 There had not been a Passover celebration like that since the days of the judges of Israel
2Kings23:23 This Passover was in the eighteenth year of the reign of King Josiah
2Kings23:24 Josiah also exterminated the mediums and wizards
2Kings23:25 There was no other king who so completely turned to the Lord and followed all the laws of Moses; and no king since the time of Josiah has approached his record of obedience.
2Kings23:26 But the Lord still did not hold back his great anger against Judah
2Kings23:27 For the Lord had said
2Kings23:28 The rest of the biography of Josiah is written in The Annals of the Kings of Judah.
2Kings23:29 In those days King Neco of Egypt went out to help the king of Assyria at the Euphrates River. Then King Josiah went out with his troops to fight King Neco; but King Neco withstood him at Megiddo and killed him.
2Kings23:30 His officers took his body back in a chariot from Megiddo to Jerusalem and buried him in the grave he had selected. And his son Jehoahaz was chosen by the nation as its new king.
2Kings23:31 New king of Judah: Jehoahaz His age at the beginning of his reign: 23 years old Length of reign: 3 months
2Kings23:32 Character of his reign: evil
2Kings23:33 Pharaoh-Neco jailed him at Riblah in Hamath to prevent his reigning in Jerusalem
2Kings23:34 The Egyptian king then chose Eliakim
2Kings23:35 Jehoiakim taxed the people to get the money that the Pharaoh had demanded.
2Kings23:36 New king of Judah: Jehoiakim His age at the beginning of his reign: 25 years old Length of reign: 11 years
2Kings23:37 Character of his reign: evil
2Kings24:1 During the reign of King Jehoiakim
2Kings24:2 And the Lord sent bands of Chaldeans
2Kings24:3 It is clear that these disasters befell Judah at the direct command of the Lord. He had decided to wipe Judah out of his sight because of the many sins of Manasseh
2Kings24:4 for he had filled Jerusalem with blood
2Kings24:5 The rest of the history of the life of Jehoiakim is recorded in The Annals of the Kings of Judah.
2Kings24:6 When he died
2Kings24:7 (The Egyptian Pharaoh never returned after that
2Kings24:8 New king of Judah: Jehoiachin His age at the beginning of his reign: 18 years old
2Kings24:9 Length of reign: 3 months
2Kings24:10 During his reign the armies of King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon besieged the city of Jerusalem.
2Kings24:11 Nebuchadnezzar himself arrived during the siege
2Kings24:12 and King Jehoiachin
2Kings24:13 The Babylonians carried home all the treasures from the Temple and the royal palace; and they cut apart all the gold bowls which King Solomon of Israel had placed in the Temple at the Lord's directions.
2Kings24:14 King Nebuchadnezzar took ten thousand captives from Jerusalem
2Kings24:15 Nebuchadnezzar took King Jehoiachin
2Kings24:16 He also took seven thousand of the best troops and one thousand craftsmen and smiths
2Kings24:17 Then the king of Babylon appointed King Jehoiachin's great-uncle
2Kings24:18 New king of Judah: Zedekiah His age at the beginning of his reign: 21 years old Length of reign: 11 years
2Kings24:19 Character of his reign: evil
2Kings24:20 So the Lord finally
2Kings25:1 Then King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon mobilized his entire army and laid siege to Jerusalem
2Kings25:2 The siege continued into the eleventh year of his reign.
2Kings25:3 The last food in the city was eaten on July 24
2Kings25:4 and that night the king and his troops made a hole in the inner wall and fled out toward the Arabah through a gate that lay between the double walls near the king's garden.
2Kings25:5 The Babylonian troops surrounding the city took out after him and captured him in the plains of Jericho
2Kings25:6 He was taken to Riblah
2Kings25:7 He was forced to watch as his sons were killed before his eyes; then his eyes were put out
2Kings25:8 General Nebuzaradan
2Kings25:9 He burned down the Temple
2Kings25:10 He then supervised the Babylonian army in tearing down the walls of Jerusalem.
2Kings25:11 The remainder of the people in the city and the Jewish deserters who had declared their allegiance to the king of Babylon were all taken as exiles to Babylon.
2Kings25:12 But the poorest of the people were left to farm the land.
2Kings25:13 The Babylonians broke up the bronze pillars of the Temple and the bronze tank and its bases and carried all the bronze to Babylon.
2Kings25:14 They also took all the pots
2Kings25:15 The gold and silver bowls
2Kings25:16 It was impossible to estimate the weight of the two pillars and the great tank and its bases—all made for the Temple by King Solomon—because they were so heavy.
2Kings25:17 Each pillar was 27 feet high
2Kings25:18 The general took Seraiah
2Kings25:19 A commander of the army of Judah
2Kings25:20 were taken by General Nebuzaradan to the king of Babylon at Riblah
2Kings25:21 where they were put to the sword and died. So Judah was exiled from its land.
2Kings25:22 Then King Nebuchadnezzar appointed Gedaliah (the son of Ahikam and grandson of Shaphan) as governor over the people left in Judah.
2Kings25:23 When the Israeli guerrilla forces learned that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah as governor
2Kings25:24 Gedaliah vowed that if they would give themselves up and submit to the Babylonians
2Kings25:25 But seven months later
2Kings25:26 Then all the men of Judah and the guerrilla leaders fled in panic to Egypt
2Kings25:27 King Jehoiachin was released from prison on the twenty-seventh day of the last month of the thirty-seventh year of his captivity. This occurred during the first year of the reign of King Evil-merodach of Babylon.
2Kings25:28 He treated Jehoiachin kindly and gave him preferential treatment over all the other kings who were being held as prisoners in Babylon.
2Kings25:29 Jehoiachin was given civilian clothing to replace his prison garb
2Kings25:30 The king also gave him a daily cash allowance for the rest of his life.

1Chronicles10:1 The Philistines attacked and defeated the Israeli troops
1Chronicles10:2 They caught up with Saul and his three sons
1Chronicles10:3 Saul had been hard pressed with heavy fighting all around him
1Chronicles10:4 He cried out to his bodyguard
1Chronicles10:5 Then his bodyguard
1Chronicles10:6 So Saul and his three sons died together; the entire family was wiped out in one day.
1Chronicles10:7 When the Israelis in the valley below the mountain heard that their troops had been routed and that Saul and his sons were dead
1Chronicles10:8 When the Philistines went back the next day to strip the bodies of the men killed in action and to gather the booty from the battlefield
1Chronicles10:9 So they stripped off Saul's armor and cut off his head; then they displayed them throughout the nation and celebrated the wonderful news before their idols.
1Chronicles10:10 They fastened his armor to the walls of the Temple of the Gods and nailed his head to the wall of Dagon's temple.
1Chronicles10:11 But when the people of Jabesh-gilead heard what the Philistines had done to Saul
1Chronicles10:12 their heroic warriors went out to the battlefield and brought back his body and the bodies of his three sons. Then they buried them beneath the oak tree at Jabesh and mourned and fasted for seven days.
1Chronicles10:13 Saul died for his disobedience to the Lord and because he had consulted a medium
1Chronicles10:14 and did not ask the Lord for guidance. So the Lord killed him and gave the kingdom to David

2Chronicles3:1 So Solomon broke ground
2Chronicles3:2 He broke ground on the second day in the second month of the fourth year of his rule.
2Chronicles3:3 These are the dimensions that Solomon set for the construction of the house of God: ninety feet long and thirty feet wide.
2Chronicles3:4 The porch in front stretched the width of the building
2Chronicles3:5 He paneled the main hall with cypress and veneered it with fine gold engraved with palm tree and chain designs.
2Chronicles3:6 He decorated the building with precious stones and gold from Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 Everything was coated with gold veneer: rafters
2Chronicles3:8 He made the Holy of Holies a cube
2Chronicles3:9 The gold nails weighed fifty shekels (a little over a pound). The upper rooms were also veneered in gold.
2Chronicles3:10 He made two sculptures of cherubim
2Chronicles3:11 The combined wingspread of the side-by-side cherubim (each wing measuring seven
2Chronicles3:12 and a half feet) stretched from wall to wall
2Chronicles3:13 They stood erect facing the main hall.

2Chronicles4:1 He made the Bronze Altar thirty feet long
2Chronicles4:2 He made a Sea—an immense round basin of cast metal fifteen feet in diameter
2Chronicles4:3 Just under the rim
2Chronicles4:4 The Sea was set on twelve bulls
2Chronicles4:5 The Sea was three inches thick and flared at the rim like a cup
2Chronicles4:6 He made ten Washbasins
2Chronicles4:7 He made ten gold Lampstands
2Chronicles4:8 He made ten tables and set five on the right and five on the left. He also made a hundred gold bowls.
2Chronicles4:9 He built a Courtyard especially for the priests and then the great court and doors for the court. The doors were covered with bronze.
2Chronicles4:10 He placed the Sea on the right side of The Temple at the southeast corner.
2Chronicles4:11 He also made ash buckets
2Chronicles4:12 two pillars; two bowl-shaped capitals for the tops of the pillars; two decorative filigrees for the capitals;
2Chronicles4:13 four hundred pomegranates for the filigrees (a double row of pomegranates for each filigree);
2Chronicles4:14 ten washstands with their basins;
2Chronicles4:15 one Sea and the twelve bulls under it;
2Chronicles4:16 miscellaneous buckets
2Chronicles4:17 The king had them cast in clay in a foundry on the Jordan plain between Succoth and Zarethan.
2Chronicles4:18 These artifacts were never weighed—there were far too many! Nobody has any idea how much bronze was used.

2Chronicles33:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king. He ruled for fifty-five years in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles33:2 In God's opinion he was a bad king—an evil king. He reintroduced all the moral rot and spiritual corruption that had been scoured from the country when God dispossessed the pagan nations in favor of the children of Israel.
2Chronicles33:3 He rebuilt the sex-and-religion shrines that his father Hezekiah had torn down
2Chronicles33:4 He built shrines to the cosmic powers and placed them in both courtyards of The Temple of God
2Chronicles33:5 the very Jerusalem Temple dedicated exclusively by God's decree to God's Name (“in Jerusalem I place my Name“).
2Chronicles33:6 He burned his own sons in a sacrificial rite in the Valley of Ben Hinnom. He practiced witchcraft and fortunetelling. He held s-ances and consulted spirits from the underworld. Much evil—in God's view a career in evil. And God was angry.
2Chronicles33:7 As a last straw he placed a carved image of the sex goddess Asherah that he had commissioned in The Temple of God
2Chronicles33:8 He had promised
2Chronicles33:9 But Manasseh led Judah and the citizens of Jerusalem off the beaten path into practices of evil exceeding even the evil of the pagan nations that God had earlier destroyed.
2Chronicles33:10 When God spoke to Manasseh and his people about this
2Chronicles33:11 Then God directed the leaders of the troops of the king of Assyria to come after Manasseh. They put a hook in his nose
2Chronicles33:12 Now that he was in trouble
2Chronicles33:13 As he prayed
2Chronicles33:14 After that Manasseh rebuilt the outside defensive wall of the City of David to the west of the Gihon spring in the valley. It went from the Fish Gate and around the hill of Ophel. He also increased its height. He tightened up the defense system by posting army captains in all the fortress cities of Judah.
2Chronicles33:15 He also did a good spring cleaning on The Temple
2Chronicles33:16 He put the Altar of God back in working order and restored worship
2Chronicles33:17 But the people didn't take him seriously—they used the name “God“ but kept on going to the old pagan neighborhood shrines and doing the same old things.

2Chronicles33:22 In God's opinion he lived an evil life
2Chronicles33:23 but he never did repent to God as Manasseh repented. He just kept at it
2Chronicles33:24 In the end Amon's servants revolted and assassinated him—killed the king right in his own palace.
2Chronicles33:25 The citizens in their turn then killed the king's assassins. The citizens then crowned Josiah

God's anger must be burning furiously against us—our ancestors haven't obeyed a thing written in this book of God

Ezra7:1 Here is the genealogy of Ezra

Ezra8:21 Then I declared a fast while we were at the Ahava River so that we would humble ourselves before our God; and we prayed that he would give us a good journey and protect us

Ezra8:23 So we fasted and begged God to take care of us. And he did.

Ezra8:32 So at last we arrived safely at Jerusalem.

Ezra9:1 But then the Jewish leaders came to tell me that many of the Jewish people and even some of the priests and Levites had taken up the horrible customs of the heathen people who lived in the land—the Canaanites
Ezra9:2 The men of Israel had married girls from these heathen nations and had taken them as wives for their sons. So the holy people of God were being polluted by these mixed marriages
Ezra9:3 When I heard this
Ezra9:4 Then many who feared the God of Israel because of this sin of his people came and sat with me until the time of the evening burnt offering.
Ezra9:5 Finally I stood before the Lord in great embarrassment; then I fell to my knees and lifted my hands to the Lord
Ezra9:6 and cried out
Ezra9:7 Our whole history has been one of sin; that is why we and our kings and our priests were slain by the heathen kings—we were captured
Ezra9:8 But now we have been given a moment of peace
Ezra9:9 For we were slaves
Ezra9:10 “And now
Ezra9:11 The prophets warned us that the land we would possess was totally defiled by the horrible practices of the people living there. From one end to the other it is filled with corruption.
Ezra9:12 You told us not to let our daughters marry their sons
Ezra9:13 And now
Ezra9:14 we have broken your commandments again and intermarried with people who do these awful things. Surely your anger will destroy us now until not even this little remnant escapes.
Ezra9:15 O Lord God of Israel
Ezra10:1 As I lay on the ground in front of the Temple

Nehemiah5:1 About this time there was a great outcry of protest from parents against some of the rich Jews who were profiteering on them.
Nehemiah5:2 What was happening was that families who ran out of money for food had to sell their children
Nehemiah5:3 or mortgage their fields
Nehemiah5:4 and some couldn't even do that
Nehemiah5:5 “We are their brothers
Nehemiah5:6 I was very angry when I heard this;
Nehemiah5:7 so after thinking about it I spoke out against these rich government officials. “What is this you are doing?“ I demanded. “How dare you demand a mortgage as a condition for helping another Israelite!“ Then I called a public trial to deal with them.
Nehemiah5:8 At the trial I shouted at them
Nehemiah5:9 Then I pressed further. “What you are doing is very evil
Nehemiah5:10 The rest of us are lending money and grain to our fellow-Jews without any interest. I beg you
Nehemiah5:11 Restore their fields
Nehemiah5:12 So they agreed to do it and said that they would assist their brothers without requiring them to mortgage their lands and sell them their children. Then I summoned the priests and made these men formally vow to carry out their promises.
Nehemiah5:13 And I invoked the curse of God upon any of them who refused. “May God destroy your homes and livelihood if you fail to keep this promise
Nehemiah5:14 I would like to mention that for the entire twelve years that I was governor of Judah—from the twentieth until the thirty-second year of the reign of King Artaxerxes—my aides and I accepted no salaries or other assistance from the people of Israel.
Nehemiah5:15 This was quite a contrast to the former governors who had demanded food and wine and “100 a day in cash
Nehemiah5:16 I stayed at work on the wall and refused to speculate in land; I also required my officials to spend time on the wall.
Nehemiah5:17 All this despite the fact that I regularly fed 150 Jewish officials at my table
Nehemiah5:18 The provisions required for each day were one ox

Nehemiah9:7 “You are the Lord God who chose Abram and brought him from Ur of the Chaldeans and renamed him Abraham.
Nehemiah9:8 When he was faithful to you
Nehemiah9:9 “You saw the troubles and sorrows of our ancestors in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 You displayed great miracles against Pharaoh and his people
Nehemiah9:11 You divided the sea for your people so they could go through on dry land! And then you destroyed their enemies in the depths of the sea; they sank like stones beneath the mighty waters.
Nehemiah9:12 You led our ancestors by a pillar of cloud during the day and a pillar of fire at night so that they could find their way.
Nehemiah9:13 “You came down upon Mount Sinai and spoke with them from heaven and gave them good laws and true commandments
Nehemiah9:14 including the laws about the holy Sabbath; and you commanded them
Nehemiah9:15 “You gave them bread from heaven when they were hungry and water from the rock when they were thirsty. You commanded them to go in and conquer the land you had sworn to give them;
Nehemiah9:16 but our ancestors were a proud and stubborn lot
Nehemiah9:17 “They refused to obey and didn't pay any attention to the miracles you did for them; instead
Nehemiah9:18 even though they made a calf-idol and proclaimed
Nehemiah9:19 but in your great mercy you didn't abandon them to die in the wilderness! The pillar of cloud led them forward day by day
Nehemiah9:20 You sent your good Spirit to instruct them
Nehemiah9:21 For forty years you sustained them in the wilderness; they lacked nothing in all that time. Their clothes didn't wear out
Nehemiah9:22 “Then you helped them conquer great kingdoms and many nations
Nehemiah9:23 You caused a population explosion among the Israelis and brought them into the land you had promised to their ancestors.
Nehemiah9:24 You subdued whole nations before them—even the kings and the people of the Canaanites were powerless!
Nehemiah9:25 Your people captured fortified cities and fertile land; they took over houses full of good things
Nehemiah9:26 “But despite all this
Nehemiah9:27 So you gave them to their enemies. But in their time of trouble they cried to you
Nehemiah9:28 But when all was going well
Nehemiah9:29 You punished them in order to turn them toward your laws; but even though they should have obeyed them
Nehemiah9:30 You were patient with them for many years. You sent your prophets to warn them about their sins
Nehemiah9:31 But in your great mercy you did not destroy them completely or abandon them forever. What a gracious and merciful God you are!
Nehemiah9:32 “And now
Nehemiah9:33 Every time you punished us you were being perfectly fair; we have sinned so greatly that you gave us only what we deserved.
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 They did not worship you despite the wonderful things you did for them and the great goodness you showered upon them. You gave them a large
Nehemiah9:36 “So now we are slaves here in the land of plenty that you gave to our ancestors! Slaves among all this abundance!

Nehemiah9:38 Because of all this

Job16:1 Job's reply:
Job16:2 “I have heard all this before. What miserable comforters all of you are.
Job16:3 Won't you ever stop your flow of foolish words? What have I said that makes you speak so endlessly?
Job16:4 But perhaps I'd sermonize the same as you—if you were I and I were you. I would spout off my criticisms against you and shake my head at you.
Job16:5 But no! I would speak in such a way that it would help you. I would try to take away your grief.
Job16:6 “But now my grief remains no matter how I defend myself; nor does it help if I refuse to speak.
Job16:7 For God has ground me down and taken away my family.
Job16:8 O God
Job16:9 God hates me and angrily tears at my flesh; he has gnashed upon me with his teeth and watched to snuff out any sign of life.

Job16:21 pleading that he will listen as a man would listen to his neighbor.
Job16:22 For all too soon I must go down that road from which I shall never return.

Job20:1 The speech of Zophar the Naamathite:
Job20:2 “I hasten to reply
Job20:3 You have tried to make me feel ashamed of myself for calling you a sinner
Job20:4 “Don't you realize that ever since man was first placed upon the earth
Job20:5 the triumph of the wicked has been short-lived
Job20:6 Though the godless be proud as the heavens and walk with his nose in the air
Job20:7 yet he shall perish forever
Job20:8 He will fade like a dream.
Job20:9 Neither his friends nor his family will ever see him again.
Job20:10 “His children shall beg from the poor
Job20:11 Though still a young man
Job20:12 “He enjoyed the taste of his wickedness
Job20:13 sipping it slowly
Job20:14 “But suddenly the food he has eaten turns sour within him.
Job20:15 He will vomit the plunder he gorged. God won't let him keep it down.
Job20:16 It is like poison and death to him.

Job38:4 “Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell me
Job38:5 Do you know how its dimensions were determined
Job38:6 What supports its foundations
Job38:7 morning stars sang together and all the angels shouted for joy?
Job38:8 “Who decreed the boundaries of the seas when they gushed from the depths?
Job38:9 Who clothed them with clouds and thick darkness
Job38:10 and barred them by limiting their shores
Job38:11 and said
Job38:12 “Have you ever once commanded the morning to appear and caused the dawn to rise in the east?
Job38:13 Have you ever told the daylight to spread to the ends of the earth
Job38:14 Have you ever robed the dawn in red
Job38:15 and disturbed the haunts of wicked men
Job38:16 “Have you explored the springs from which the seas come
Job38:17 Has the location of the gates of Death been revealed to you? Do you realize the extent of the earth?
Job38:18 Tell me about it if you know!
Job38:19 Where does the light come from
Job38:20 Can you find its boundaries
Job38:21 But of course you know all this! For you were born before it was all created
Job38:22 “Have you visited the treasuries of the snow
Job38:23 For I have reserved it for the time when I will need it in war.
Job38:24 Where is the path to the distribution point of light? Where is the home of the east wind?
Job38:25 Who dug the valleys for the torrents of rain? Who laid out the path for the lightning
Job38:26 causing the rain to fall upon the barren deserts
Job38:27 so that the parched and barren ground is satisfied with water and tender grass springs up?
Job38:28 “Has the rain a father? Where does dew come from?
Job38:29 Who is the mother of the ice and frost?
Job38:30 For the water changes and turns to ice as hard as rock.
Job38:31 “Can you hold back the stars? Can you restrain Orion or Pleiades?
Job38:32 Can you ensure the proper sequence of the seasons
Job38:33 Do you know the laws of the universe and how the heavens influence the earth?
Job38:34 Can you shout to the clouds and make it rain?
Job38:35 Can you make lightning appear and cause it to strike as you direct it?
Job38:36 “Who gives intuition and instinct?
Job38:37 Who is wise enough to number all the clouds?
Job38:38 Who can tilt the water jars of heaven
Job38:39 Can you stalk prey like a lioness
Job38:40 as they lie in their dens or lie in wait in the jungle?
Job38:41 Who provides for the ravens when their young cry out to God as they try to struggle up from their nest in hunger?
Job39:1 “Do you know how mountain goats give birth? Have you ever seen them giving birth to their young?
Job39:2 Do you know how many months of pregnancy they have before they bow
Job39:3 themselves to give birth to their young and carry their burden no longer?
Job39:4 Their young grow up in the open field
Job39:5 “Who makes the wild donkeys wild?
Job39:6 I have placed them in the wilderness and given them salt plains to live in.
Job39:7 For they hate the noise of the city and want no drivers shouting at them!
Job39:8 The mountain ranges are their pastureland; there they search for every blade of grass.
Job39:9 “Will the wild ox be your happy servant? Will he stay beside your feeding crib?
Job39:10 Can you use a wild ox to plow with? Will he pull the harrow for you?
Job39:11 Because he is so strong
Job39:12 Can you send him out to bring in the grain from the threshing-floor?
Job39:13 “The ostrich flaps her wings grandly but has no true motherly love.
Job39:14 She lays her eggs on top of the earth
Job39:15 She forgets that someone may step on them and crush them
Job39:16 She ignores her young as though they weren't her own and is unconcerned though they die
Job39:17 for God has deprived her of wisdom.
Job39:18 But whenever she jumps up to run
Job39:19 “Have you given the horse strength or clothed his neck with a quivering mane?
Job39:20 Have you made him able to leap forward like a locust? His majestic snorting is something to hear!
Job39:21 He paws the earth and rejoices in his strength
Job39:22 he is unafraid and does not run away
Job39:23 though the arrows rattle against him
Job39:24 Fiercely he paws the ground and rushes forward into battle when the trumpet blows.
Job39:25 At the sound of the bugle he shouts
Job39:26 “Do you know how a hawk soars and spreads her wings to the south?
Job39:27 Is it at your command that the eagle rises high upon the cliffs to make her nest?
Job39:28 She lives upon the cliffs
Job39:29 From there she spies her prey
Job39:30 Her nestlings gulp down blood
Job40:1 The Lord went on:
Job40:2 “Do you still want to argue with the Almighty? Or will you yield? Do you—God's critic—have the answers?“
Job40:3 Then Job replied to God:
Job40:4 “I am nothing—how could I ever find the answers? I lay my hand upon my mouth in silence.
Job40:5 I have said too much already.“
Job40:6 Then the Lord spoke to Job again from the whirlwind:
Job40:7 “Stand up like a man and brace yourself for battle. Let me ask you a question
Job40:8 Are you going to discredit my justice and condemn me so that you can say you are right?
Job40:9 Are you as strong as God
Job40:10 All right then
Job40:11 Give vent to your anger. Let it overflow against the proud.
Job40:12 Humiliate the haughty with a glance; tread down the wicked where they stand.
Job40:13 Knock them into the dust
Job40:14 If you can do that
Job40:15 “Take a look at the hippopotamus! I made him
Job40:16 See his powerful loins and the muscles of his belly.
Job40:17 His tail is as straight as a cedar. The sinews of his thighs are tightly knit together.
Job40:18 His vertebrae lie straight as a tube of brass. His ribs are like iron bars.
Job40:19 How ferocious he is among all of God's creation
Job40:20 The mountains offer their best food to him—the other wild animals on which he preys.
Job40:21 He lies down under the lotus plants
Job40:22 covered by their shade among the willows there beside the stream.
Job40:23 He is not disturbed by raging rivers
Job40:24 No one can catch him off guard or put a ring in his nose and lead him away.

Job41:7 Will his hide be hurt by darts

Job41:25 When he stands up
Job41:26 No sword can stop him
Job41:27 Iron is nothing but straw to him

Job41:29 Clubs do no good
Job41:30 His belly is covered with scales as sharp as shards; they tear up the ground as he drags through the mud.

Job41:32 He leaves a shining wake of froth behind him. One would think the sea was made of frost!
Job41:33 There is nothing else so fearless anywhere on earth.
Job41:34 Of all the beasts

Job42:2 “I know that you can do anything and that no one can stop you.
Job42:3 You ask who it is who has so foolishly denied your providence. It is I. I was talking about things I knew nothing about and did not understand
Job42:4 “ You said
Job42:5 “ But now I say
Job42:6 and I loathe myself and repent in dust and ashes.' “
Job42:7 After the Lord had finished speaking with Job

Job42:10 Then
Job42:11 Then all of his brothers
Job42:12 So the Lord blessed Job at the end of his life more than at the beginning. For now he had 14
Job42:13 God also gave him seven more sons and three more daughters.
Job42:14 These were the names of his daughters: Jemima
Job42:15 And in all the land there were no other girls as lovely as the daughters of Job; and their father put them into his will along with their brothers.
Job42:16 Job lived 140 years after that
Job42:17 Then at last he died

Psalms18:33 He gives me the surefootedness of a mountain goat upon the crags. He leads me safely along the top of the cliffs.

Psalms72:1 O God
Psalms72:2 Help him to give justice to your people
Psalms72:3 May the mountains and hills flourish in prosperity because of his good reign.
Psalms72:4 Help him to defend the poor and needy and to crush their oppressors.
Psalms72:5 May the poor and needy revere you constantly
Psalms72:6 May the reign of this son of mine be as gentle and fruitful as the springtime rains upon the grass—like showers that water the earth!
Psalms72:7 May all good men flourish in his reign with abundance of peace to the end of time.
Psalms72:8 Let him reign from sea to sea and from the Euphrates River to the ends of the earth.
Psalms72:9 The desert nomads shall bow before him; his enemies shall fall face downward in the dust.
Psalms72:10 Kings along the Mediterranean coast—the kings of Tarshish and the islands—and those from Sheba and from Seba—all will bring their gifts.
Psalms72:11 Yes
Psalms72:12 He will take care of the helpless and poor when they cry to him; for they have no one else to defend them.
Psalms72:13 He feels pity for the weak and needy and will rescue them.
Psalms72:14 He will save them from oppression and from violence
Psalms72:15 And he shall live; and to him will be given the gold of Sheba
Psalms72:16 Bless us with abundant crops throughout the land
Psalms72:17 His name will be honored forever; it will continue as the sun; and all will be blessed in him; all nations will praise him.
Psalms72:18 Blessed be Jehovah God
Psalms72:19 Blessed be his glorious name forever! Let the whole earth be filled with his glory. Amen and amen!

Psalms73:2 But as for me

Psalms73:4 Yes

Psalms73:6 so their pride sparkles like a jeweled necklace
Psalms73:7 These fat cats have everything their hearts could ever wish for!
Psalms73:8 They scoff at God and threaten his people. How proudly they speak!
Psalms73:9 They boast against the very heavens
Psalms73:10 And so God's people are dismayed and confused and drink it all in.
Psalms73:11 “Does God realize what is going on?“ they ask.
Psalms73:12 “Look at these men of arrogance; they never have to lift a finger—theirs is a life of ease; and all the time their riches multiply.“

Psalms73:14 All I get out of it is trouble and woe—every day and all day long!

Psalms74:2 Remember that we are your people—the ones you chose in ancient times from slavery and made the choicest of your possessions. You chose Jerusalem as your home on earth!

Psalms74:4 There they shouted their battle cry and erected their idols to flaunt their victory.
Psalms74:5 Everything lies in shambles like a forest chopped to the ground.

Psalms74:7 they set the sanctuary on fire

Psalms74:10 How long

Psalms74:12 God is my King from ages past; you have been actively helping me everywhere throughout the land.
Psalms74:13 You divided the Red Sea with your strength; you crushed the sea-god's heads!
Psalms74:14 You gave him to the desert tribes to eat!
Psalms74:15 At your command the springs burst forth to give your people water; and then you dried a path for them across the ever-flowing Jordan.
Psalms74:16 Day and night alike belong to you; you made the starlight and the sun.
Psalms74:17 All nature is within your hands; you make the summer and the winter too.

Psalms77:1 I cry to the Lord; I call and call to him. Oh
Psalms77:2 I am in deep trouble and I need his help so much. All night long I pray
Psalms77:3 I think of God and moan
Psalms77:4 I cannot sleep until you act. I am too distressed even to pray!
Psalms77:5 I keep thinking of the good old days of the past
Psalms77:6 Then my nights were filled with joyous songs. I search my soul and meditate upon the difference now.
Psalms77:7 Has the Lord rejected me forever? Will he never again be favorable?
Psalms77:8 Is his loving-kindness gone forever? Has his promise failed?

Psalms77:10 And I said: This is my fate
Psalms77:11 I recall the many miracles he did for me so long ago.
Psalms77:12 Those wonderful deeds are constantly in my thoughts. I cannot stop thinking about them.

Psalms78:1 O my people
Psalms78:2 For I will show you lessons from our
Psalms78:3 history
Psalms78:4 I will reveal these truths to you so that you can describe these glorious deeds of Jehovah to your children and tell them about the mighty miracles he did.
Psalms78:5 For he gave his laws to Israel and commanded our fathers to teach them to their children
Psalms78:6 so that they in turn could teach their children too. Thus his laws pass down from generation to generation.
Psalms78:7 In this way each generation has been able to obey his laws and to set its hope anew on God and not forget his glorious miracles.
Psalms78:8 Thus they did not need to be as their fathers were—stubborn
Psalms78:9 The people of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 because they didn't obey his laws. They refused to follow his ways.
Psalms78:11 And they forgot about the wonderful miracles God had
Psalms78:12 done for them and for their fathers in Egypt.
Psalms78:13 For he divided the sea before them and led them through! The water stood banked up along both sides of them!
Psalms78:14 In the daytime he led them by a cloud
Psalms78:15 He split open the rocks in the wilderness to give them plenty of water
Psalms78:16 Streams poured from the rock
Psalms78:17 Yet they kept on with their rebellion
Psalms78:18 They murmured and complained
Psalms78:19 They even spoke against God himself.
Psalms78:20 “Why can't he give us decent food as well as water?“ they grumbled.
Psalms78:21 Jehovah heard them and was angry; the fire of his wrath burned against Israel
Psalms78:22 because they didn't believe in God or trust in him to care for them
Psalms78:23 even though he commanded the skies to open—he opened the windows of heaven—
Psalms78:24 and rained down manna for their food. He gave them bread from heaven!
Psalms78:25 They ate angels' food! He gave them all they could hold.
Psalms78:26 And he led forth the east wind and guided the south wind by his mighty power.
Psalms78:27 He rained down birds as thick as dust
Psalms78:28 He caused the birds to fall to the ground among the tents.
Psalms78:29 The people ate their fill. He gave them what they asked for.
Psalms78:30 But they had hardly finished eating
Psalms78:31 when the anger of the Lord rose against them and killed the finest of Israel's young men.
Psalms78:32 Yet even so the people kept on sinning and refused to believe in miracles.
Psalms78:33 So he cut their lives short and gave them years of terror and disaster.
Psalms78:34 Then at last
Psalms78:35 Then they remembered that God was their Rock—that their Savior was the God above all gods.
Psalms78:36 But it was only with their words they followed him
Psalms78:37 their hearts were far away. They did not keep their promises.
Psalms78:38 Yet he was merciful and forgave their sins and didn't destroy them all. Many and many a time he held back his anger.
Psalms78:39 For he remembered that they were merely mortal men
Psalms78:40 Oh
Psalms78:41 Again and again they turned away and tempted God to kill them
Psalms78:42 They forgot his power and love and how he had rescued them from their enemies;
Psalms78:43 they forgot the plagues he sent upon the Egyptians in Tanis—
Psalms78:44 how he turned their rivers into blood so that no one could drink
Psalms78:45 how he sent vast swarms of flies to fill the land
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to caterpillars. Their harvest was consumed by locusts.
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their grapevines and their sycamores with hail.
Psalms78:48 Their cattle died in the fields
Psalms78:49 He loosed on them the fierceness of his anger
Psalms78:50 He gave free course to his anger and did not spare the Egyptians' lives
Psalms78:51 Then he killed the eldest son in each Egyptian family—he who was the beginning of its strength and joy.
Psalms78:52 But he led forth his own people like a flock
Psalms78:53 He kept them safe
Psalms78:54 He brought them to the border of his land of blessing
Psalms78:55 He drove out the nations occupying the land and gave each tribe of Israel its apportioned place as its home.
Psalms78:56 Yet though he did all this for them
Psalms78:57 They turned back from entering the Promised Land and disobeyed as their fathers had. Like a crooked arrow
Psalms78:58 They made him angry by erecting idols and altars to other gods.
Psalms78:59 When God saw their deeds
Psalms78:60 Then he abandoned his Tabernacle at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 and allowed his Ark to be captured; he surrendered his glory into enemy hands.
Psalms78:62 He caused his people to be butchered because his anger was intense.
Psalms78:63 Their young men were killed by fire
Psalms78:64 The priests were slaughtered
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord rose up as though awakening from sleep
Psalms78:66 he routed his enemies; he drove them back and sent them to eternal shame.
Psalms78:67 But he rejected Joseph's family
Psalms78:68 and chose the tribe of Judah—and Mount Zion
Psalms78:69 There he built his towering temple
Psalms78:70 He chose his servant David
Psalms78:71 and from following the ewes with lambs; God presented David to his people as their shepherd
Psalms78:72 and he cared for them with a true heart and skillful hands.

Psalms90:1 A prayer of Moses
Psalms90:2 Before the mountains were created
Psalms90:3 You speak
Psalms90:4 A thousand years are but as yesterday to you! They are like a single hour!
Psalms90:5 We glide along the tides of time as swiftly as a racing river and vanish as quickly as a dream.
Psalms90:6 We are like grass that is green in the morning but mowed down and withered before the evening shadows fall.
Psalms90:7 We die beneath your anger; we are overwhelmed by your wrath.
Psalms90:8 You spread out our sins before you—our secret sins—and see them all.
Psalms90:9 No wonder the years are long and heavy here beneath your wrath. All our days are filled with sighing.
Psalms90:10 Seventy years are given us! And some may even live to eighty. But even the best of these years are often emptiness and pain; soon they disappear
Psalms90:11 Who can realize the terrors of your anger? Which of us can fear you as he should?
Psalms90:12 Teach us to number our days and recognize how few they are; help us to spend them as we should.
Psalms90:13 O Jehovah
Psalms90:14 Satisfy us in our earliest youth with your loving-kindness
Psalms90:15 Give us gladness in proportion to our former misery! Replace the evil years with good.
Psalms90:16 Let us see your miracles again; let our children see glorious things
Psalms90:17 and let the Lord our God favor us and give us success. May he give permanence to all we do.

Psalms91:6 nor dread the plagues of darkness

Psalms109:24 My knees are weak from fasting
Psalms109:25 I am a symbol of failure to all mankind; when they see me they shake their heads.

Psalms109:31 For he stands beside the poor and hungry to save them from their enemies.

Psalms111:2 All who are thankful should ponder them with me.
Psalms111:3 For his miracles demonstrate his honor
Psalms111:4 Who can forget the wonders he performs—deeds of mercy and of grace?
Psalms111:5 He gives food to those who trust him; he never forgets his promises.

Proverbs1:1 These are the proverbs of King Solomon of Israel
Proverbs1:2 He wrote them to teach his people how to live—how to act in every circumstance
Proverbs1:3 for he wanted them to be understanding
Proverbs1:4 “I want to make the simpleminded wise!“ he said. “I want to warn young men about some problems they will face.
Proverbs1:5 I want those already wise to become the wiser and become
Proverbs1:6 leaders by exploring the depths of meaning in these nuggets of truth.“
Proverbs1:7 How does a man become wise? The first step is to trust and reverence the Lord! Only fools refuse to be taught.
Proverbs1:8 Listen to your father and mother. What you learn from them
Proverbs1:9 will stand you in good stead; it will gain you many honors.

Proverbs1:21 She calls out to the crowds along Main Street
Proverbs1:22 “You simpletons!“ she cries. “How long will you go on being fools? How long will you scoff at wisdom and fight the facts?

Proverbs1:25 For you have spurned my counsel and reproof.

Proverbs1:27 When a storm of terror surrounds you
