Proverbs1:32 For you turned away from me—to death; your own complacency will kill you. Fools!
Proverbs1:33 But all who listen to me shall live in peace and safety

Ecclesiastes2:16 For the wise and fool both die

Ecclesiastes7:9 Don't be quick-tempered—that is being a fool.
Ecclesiastes7:10 Don't long for “the good old days
Ecclesiastes7:11 To be wise is as good as being rich; in fact
Ecclesiastes7:12 You can get anything by either wisdom or money
Ecclesiastes7:13 See the way God does things and fall into line. Don't fight the facts of nature. Who can straighten what he has made crooked?“
Ecclesiastes7:14 Enjoy prosperity whenever you can
Ecclesiastes7:15 In this silly life I have seen everything
Ecclesiastes7:16 So don't be too good or too wise! Why destroy yourself?
Ecclesiastes7:17 On the other hand
Ecclesiastes7:18 Tackle every task that comes along
Ecclesiastes7:19 A wise man is stronger than the mayors of ten big cities!
Ecclesiastes7:20 And there is not a single man in all the earth who is always good and never sins.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Don't eavesdrop! You may hear your servant cursing you!
Ecclesiastes7:22 For you know how often you yourself curse others!
Ecclesiastes7:23 I have tried my best to be wise. I declared
Ecclesiastes7:24 Wisdom is far away and very difficult to find.
Ecclesiastes7:25 I searched everywhere
Ecclesiastes7:26 A prostitute is more bitter than death. May it please God that you escape from her
Ecclesiastes7:27 This is my conclusion
Ecclesiastes7:28 Step by step I came to this result after researching in every direction: One tenth of one percent of the men I interviewed could be said to be wise
Ecclesiastes7:29 And I found that though God has made men upright
Ecclesiastes8:1 How wonderful to be wise
Ecclesiastes8:2 Obey the king as you have vowed to do. Don't always be trying to get out of doing your duty
Ecclesiastes8:3 For the king punishes those who disobey.
Ecclesiastes8:4 The king's command is backed by great power
Ecclesiastes8:5 Those who obey him will not be punished. The wise man will find a time and a way to do what he says.
Ecclesiastes8:6 Yes
Ecclesiastes8:7 upon him; for how can he avoid what he doesn't know is going to happen?
Ecclesiastes8:8 No one can hold back his spirit from departing; no one has the power to prevent his day of death
Ecclesiastes8:9 I have thought deeply about all that goes on here in the world
Ecclesiastes8:10 I have seen wicked men buried
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because God does not punish sinners instantly
Ecclesiastes8:12 But though a man sins a hundred times and still lives
Ecclesiastes8:13 unlike the wicked
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is a strange thing happening here upon the earth: Providence seems to treat some good men as though they were wicked
Ecclesiastes8:15 Then I decided to spend my time having fun because I felt that there was nothing better in all the earth than that a man should eat
Ecclesiastes8:16 In my search for wisdom I observed all that was going on everywhere across the earth—ceaseless activity
Ecclesiastes8:17 (Of course
Ecclesiastes9:1 This
Ecclesiastes9:2 The same providence confronts everyone
Ecclesiastes9:3 That is why men are not more careful to be good but instead choose their own mad course
Ecclesiastes9:4 There is hope only for the living. “It is better to be a live dog than a dead lion!“
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living at least know that they will die! But the dead know nothing; they don't even have their memories.
Ecclesiastes9:6 Whatever they did in their lifetimes—loving
Ecclesiastes9:7 So go ahead
Ecclesiastes9:8 Wear fine clothes—with a dash of cologne!
Ecclesiastes9:9 Live happily with the woman you love through the fleeting days of life
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever you do
Ecclesiastes9:11 Again I looked throughout the earth and saw that the swiftest person does not always win the race
Ecclesiastes9:12 A man never knows when he is going to run into bad luck. He is like a fish caught in a net
Ecclesiastes9:13 Here is another thing that has made a deep impression on me as I have watched human affairs:
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small city with only a few people living in it
Ecclesiastes9:15 There was in the city a wise man
Ecclesiastes9:16 Then I realized that though wisdom is better than strength
Ecclesiastes9:17 But even so
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies will cause even a bottle of perfume to stink! Yes
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise man's heart leads him to do right
Ecclesiastes10:3 You can identify a fool just by the way he walks down the street!
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the boss is angry with you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is another evil I have seen as I have watched the world go by
Ecclesiastes10:6 For I have seen foolish men given great authority and rich men not given their rightful place of dignity!
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have even seen servants riding
Ecclesiastes10:8 Dig a well—and fall into it! Demolish an old wall—and be bitten by a snake!
Ecclesiastes10:9 When working in a quarry
Ecclesiastes10:10 A dull axe requires great strength; be wise and sharpen the blade.
Ecclesiastes10:11 When the horse is stolen
Ecclesiastes10:12 It is pleasant to listen to wise words
Ecclesiastes10:13 Since he begins with a foolish premise
Ecclesiastes10:14 A fool knows all about the future and tells everyone in detail! But who can really know what is going to happen?
Ecclesiastes10:15 A fool is so upset by a little work that he has no strength for the simplest matter.
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to the land whose king is a child and whose leaders are already drunk in the morning.
Ecclesiastes10:17 Happy the land whose king is a nobleman and whose leaders work hard before they feast and drink
Ecclesiastes10:18 Laziness lets the roof leak
Ecclesiastes10:19 A party gives laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Never curse the king
Ecclesiastes11:1 Give generously
Ecclesiastes11:2 Divide your gifts among many
Ecclesiastes11:3 When the clouds are heavy
Ecclesiastes11:4 If you wait for perfect conditions
Ecclesiastes11:5 God's ways are as mysterious as the pathway of the wind and as the manner in which a human spirit is infused into the little body of a baby while it is yet in its mother's womb.
Ecclesiastes11:6 Keep on sowing your seed
Ecclesiastes11:7 It is a wonderful thing to be alive!
Ecclesiastes11:8 If a person lives to be very old
Ecclesiastes11:9 Young man
Ecclesiastes11:10 So banish grief and pain
Ecclesiastes12:1 Don't let the excitement of being young cause you to forget about your Creator. Honor him in your youth before the evil years come—when you'll no longer enjoy living.
Ecclesiastes12:2 It will be too late then to try to remember him when the sun and light and moon and stars are dim to your old eyes
Ecclesiastes12:3 For there will come a time when your limbs will tremble with age
Ecclesiastes12:4 Then let your lips be tightly closed while eating when your teeth are gone! And you will waken at dawn with the first note of the birds; but you yourself will be deaf and tuneless
Ecclesiastes12:5 You will be afraid of heights and of falling—a white-haired
Ecclesiastes12:6 Yes
Ecclesiastes12:7 then the dust returns to the earth as it was
Ecclesiastes12:8 All is futile
Ecclesiastes12:9 But then
Ecclesiastes12:10 For the Preacher was not only a wise man but a good teacher; he not only taught what he knew to the people
Ecclesiastes12:11 The wise man's words are like goads that spur to action. They nail down important truths. Students are wise who master what their teachers tell them.
Ecclesiastes12:12 But
Ecclesiastes12:13 Here is my final conclusion: fear God and obey his commandments
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God will judge us for everything we do

Isaiah1:5 Oh

Isaiah1:7 Your country lies in ruins; your cities are burned; while you watch
Isaiah1:8 You stand there helpless and abandoned like a watchman's shanty in the field when the harvesttime is over—or when the crop is stripped and robbed.

Isaiah1:10 An apt comparison! Listen
Isaiah1:11 “I am sick of your sacrifices. Don't bring me any more of them. I don't want your fat rams; I don't want to see the blood from your offerings.
Isaiah1:12 Who wants your sacrifices when you have no sorrow for your sins?
Isaiah1:13 The incense you bring me is a stench in my nostrils. Your holy celebrations of the new moon and the Sabbath
Isaiah1:14 I hate them all; I can't stand the sight of them.
Isaiah1:15 From now on
Isaiah1:16 “Oh

Isaiah2:2 In the last days Jerusalem and the Temple of the Lord will become the world's greatest attraction
Isaiah2:3 “Come
Isaiah2:4 The Lord will settle international disputes; all the nations will convert their weapons of war into implements of peace. Then at the last all wars will stop and all military training will end.
Isaiah2:5 O Israel
Isaiah2:6 The Lord has rejected you because you welcome foreigners from the East who practice magic and communicate with evil spirits
Isaiah2:7 Israel has vast treasures of silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 and idols—the land is full of them! They are man-made
Isaiah2:9 Small and great
Isaiah2:10 Crawl into the caves in the rocks and hide in terror from his glorious majesty
Isaiah2:11 for the day is coming when your proud looks will be brought low; the Lord alone will be exalted.
Isaiah2:12 On that day the Lord Almighty will move against the proud and haughty and bring them to the dust.
Isaiah2:13 All the tall cedars of Lebanon and all the mighty oaks of Bashan shall bend low
Isaiah2:14 and all the high mountains and hills
Isaiah2:15 and every high tower and wall
Isaiah2:16 and all the proud ocean ships and trim harbor craft—all shall be crushed before the Lord that day.
Isaiah2:17 All the glory of mankind will bow low; the pride of men will lie in the dust
Isaiah2:18 And all idols will be utterly abolished and destroyed.
Isaiah2:19 When the Lord stands up from his throne to shake up the earth
Isaiah2:20 Then at last they will abandon their gold and silver idols to the moles and bats
Isaiah2:21 and crawl into the caverns to hide among the jagged rocks at the tops of the cliffs
Isaiah2:22 Puny man! Frail as his breath! Don't ever put your trust in him!

Isaiah5:3 Now
Isaiah5:4 What more could I have done? Why did my vineyard give me wild grapes instead of sweet?

Isaiah5:6 I won't prune it or hoe it

Isaiah5:9 But the Lord Almighty has sworn your awful fate—with my own ears I heard him say

Isaiah5:12 You furnish lovely music at your grand parties; the orchestras are superb! But for the Lord you have no thought or care.

Isaiah5:19 They even mock the Holy One of Israel and dare the Lord to punish them. “Hurry up and punish us
Isaiah5:20 They say that what is right is wrong and what is wrong is right; that black is white and white is black; bitter is sweet and sweet is bitter.
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who are wise and shrewd in their own eyes!
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are “heroes“ when it comes to drinking and boast about the liquor they can hold.
Isaiah5:23 They take bribes to pervert justice
Isaiah5:24 Therefore God will deal with them and burn them. They will disappear like straw on fire. Their roots will rot and their flowers wither
Isaiah5:25 That is why the anger of the Lord is hot against his people; that is why he has reached out his hand to smash them. The hills will tremble

Isaiah5:30 They growl over their victims like the roaring of the sea. Over all Israel lies a pall of darkness and sorrow

Jeremiah1:1 These are God's messages to Jeremiah the priest (the son of Hilkiah) who lived in the town of Anathoth in the land of Benjamin.
Jeremiah1:2 The first of these messages came to him in the thirteenth year of the reign of Amon's son Josiah
Jeremiah1:3 Others came during the reign of Josiah's son Jehoiakim
Jeremiah1:4 The Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:5 “I knew you before you were formed within your mother's womb; before you were born I sanctified you and appointed you as my spokesman to the world.“

Jeremiah1:7 “Don't say that

Jeremiah1:10 Today your work begins

Jeremiah1:17 Get up and dress and go out and tell them whatever I tell you to say. Don't be afraid of them
Jeremiah1:18 For see
Jeremiah1:19 They will try
Jeremiah2:1 Again the Lord spoke to me and said:
Jeremiah2:2 “Go and shout this in Jerusalem's streets: The Lord says
Jeremiah2:3 In those days Israel was a holy people
Jeremiah2:4 “O Israel
Jeremiah2:5 What sin did they find in me that turned them away and changed them into fools who worship idols?
Jeremiah2:6 They ignore the fact that it was I
Jeremiah2:7 And I brought them into a fruitful land
Jeremiah2:8 Even their priests cared nothing for the Lord
Jeremiah2:9 “But I will not give you up—I will plead for you to return to me and will keep on pleading; yes
Jeremiah2:10 “Look around you and see if you can find another nation anywhere that has traded in its old gods for new ones—even though their gods are nothing.
Jeremiah2:11 Send to the west to the island of Cyprus; send to the east to the deserts of Kedar. See if anyone there has ever heard so strange a thing as this. And yet my people have given up their glorious God for silly idols!
Jeremiah2:12 The heavens are shocked at such a thing and shrink back in horror and dismay.
Jeremiah2:13 For my people have done two evil things: They have forsaken me
Jeremiah2:14 “Why has Israel become a nation of slaves? Why is she captured and led far away?
Jeremiah2:15 “I see great armies marching on Jerusalem with mighty shouts to destroy her and leave her cities in ruins
Jeremiah2:16 I see the armies of Egypt rising against her
Jeremiah2:17 And you have brought this on yourselves by rebelling against the Lord your God when he wanted to lead you and show you the way!
Jeremiah2:18 “What have you gained by your alliances with Egypt and with Assyria?
Jeremiah2:19 Your own wickedness will punish you. You will see what an evil
Jeremiah2:20 “Long ago you shook off my yoke and broke away from my ties. Defiant
Jeremiah2:21 “How could this happen? How could this be? For when I planted you
Jeremiah2:22 No amount of soap or lye can make you clean. You are stained with guilt that cannot ever be washed away. I see it always before me
Jeremiah2:23 You say it isn't so
Jeremiah2:24 You are a wild donkey
Jeremiah2:25 Why don't you turn from all this weary running after other gods? But you say
Jeremiah2:26 “Like a thief
Jeremiah2:27 They call a carved-up wooden post their father
Jeremiah2:28 Why don't you call on these gods you have made? When danger comes
Jeremiah2:29 Don't come to me—you are all rebels
Jeremiah2:30 I have punished your children
Jeremiah2:31 “O my people
Jeremiah2:32 How can you disown your God like that? Can a girl forget her jewels? What bride will seek to hide her wedding dress? Yet for years on end my people have forgotten me—the most precious of their treasures.
Jeremiah2:33 “How you plot and scheme to win your lovers. The most experienced harlot could learn a lot from you!

Jeremiah3:3 That is why even the springtime rains have failed. For you are a prostitute and completely unashamed.

Jeremiah3:6 This message from the Lord came to me during the reign of King Josiah: “Have you seen what Israel does? Like a wanton wife who gives herself to other men at every chance
Jeremiah3:7 I thought that someday she would return to me and once again be mine; but she didn't come back. And her faithless sister Judah saw the continued rebellion of Israel.
Jeremiah3:8 Yet she paid no attention
Jeremiah3:9 She treated it all so lightly—to her it was nothing at all that she should worship idols made of wood and stone. And so the land was greatly polluted and defiled.

Jeremiah3:19 And I thought how wonderful it would be for you to be here among my children. I planned to give you part of this beautiful land
Jeremiah3:20 But you have betrayed me; you have gone off and given yourself to a host of foreign gods; you have been like a faithless wife who leaves her husband.“

Jeremiah13:7 And I did; I dug it out of the hole where I had hidden it. But now it was mildewed and falling apart. It was utterly useless!

Jeremiah13:21 How will you feel when he sets your allies over you as your rulers? You will writhe in pain like a woman having a child.
Jeremiah13:22 And if you ask yourself

Jeremiah22:18 Therefore this is God's decree of punishment against King Jehoiakim

Jeremiah23:26 How long will this continue? If they are 'prophets

Jeremiah23:28 Let these false prophets tell their dreams and let my true messengers faithfully proclaim my every word. There is a difference between chaff and wheat!
Jeremiah23:29 Does not my word burn like fire?“ asks the Lord. “Is it not like a mighty hammer that smashed the rock to pieces?

Jeremiah32:1 The following message came to Jeremiah from the Lord in the tenth year of the reign of Zedekiah
Jeremiah32:2 At this time Jeremiah was imprisoned in the dungeon beneath the palace

Jeremiah32:9 So I bought the field
Jeremiah32:10 I signed and sealed the deed of purchase before witnesses

Jeremiah32:18 You are loving and kind to thousands
Jeremiah32:19 You have all wisdom and do great and mighty miracles; for your eyes are open to all the ways of men
Jeremiah32:20 You have done incredible things in the land of Egypt—things still remembered to this day. And you have continued to do great miracles in Israel and all around the world. You have made your name very great
Jeremiah32:21 “You brought Israel out of Egypt with mighty miracles and great power and terror.
Jeremiah32:22 You gave Israel this land that you promised their fathers long ago—a wonderful land that 'flows with milk and honey.'
Jeremiah32:23 Our fathers came and conquered it and lived in it
Jeremiah32:24 See how the siege mounds have been built against the city walls
Jeremiah32:25 And yet you say to buy the field—paying good money for it before these witnesses—even though the city will belong to our enemies.“
Jeremiah32:26 Then this message came to Jeremiah:
Jeremiah32:27 “I am the Lord
Jeremiah32:28 Yes
Jeremiah32:29 And the Babylonians outside the walls shall come in and set fire to the city and burn down all these houses
Jeremiah32:30 For Israel and Judah have done nothing but wrong since their earliest days; they have infuriated me with all their evil deeds.
Jeremiah32:31 From the time this city was built until now it has done nothing but anger me; so I am determined to be rid of it.
Jeremiah32:32 “The sins of Israel and Judah—the sins of the people
Jeremiah32:33 They have turned their backs upon me and refused to return; day after day
Jeremiah32:34 They have even defiled my own Temple by worshiping their abominable idols there.
Jeremiah32:35 And they have built high altars to Baal in the Valley of Hinnom. There they have burnt their children as sacrifices to Molech—something I never commanded and cannot imagine suggesting. What an incredible evil
Jeremiah32:36 “Now therefore the Lord God of Israel says concerning this city that it will fall to the king of Babylon through warfare
Jeremiah32:37 but I will bring my people back again from all the countries where in my fury I will scatter them. I will bring them back to this very city and make them live in peace and safety.
Jeremiah32:38 And they shall be my people
Jeremiah32:39 And I will give them one heart and mind to worship me forever
Jeremiah32:40 “And I will make an everlasting covenant with them
Jeremiah32:41 I will rejoice to do them good and will replant them in this land with great joy.
Jeremiah32:42 Just as I have sent all these terrors and evils upon them
Jeremiah32:43 “Fields will again be bought and sold in this land now ravaged by the Babylonians
Jeremiah32:44 Yes

Jeremiah52:1 (Events told about in chapter 39.) Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
Jeremiah52:2 But he was a wicked king
Jeremiah52:3 Things became so bad at last that the Lord
Jeremiah52:4 In the ninth year of Zedekiah's reign
Jeremiah52:5 and laid siege to the city for two years.
Jeremiah52:6 Then finally
Jeremiah52:7 the people in the city tore a hole in the city wall and all the soldiers fled from the city during the night
Jeremiah52:8 But the Chaldean soldiers chased them and caught King Zedekiah in some fields near Jericho—for all his army was scattered from him.
Jeremiah52:9 They brought him to the king of Babylon who was staying in the city of Riblah in the kingdom of Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 He made Zedekiah watch while his sons and all the princes of Judah were killed before his eyes.
Jeremiah52:11 Then his eyes were gouged out
Jeremiah52:12 On the tenth day of the fifth month during the nineteenth year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:13 and burned the Temple and the palace and all the larger homes
Jeremiah52:14 and set the Chaldean army to work tearing down the walls of the city.
Jeremiah52:15 Then he took to Babylon
Jeremiah52:16 But he left some of the poorest people to care for the crops as vinedressers and plowmen.
Jeremiah52:17 The Babylonians dismantled the two large bronze pillars that stood at the entrance of the Temple
Jeremiah52:18 And he took along all the bronze pots and kettles
Jeremiah52:19 He also took the firepans
Jeremiah52:20 The weight of the two enormous pillars
Jeremiah52:21 For the pillars were each 27 feet high and 18 feet in circumference
Jeremiah52:22 The top 7 1/2 feet of each column had bronze carvings
Jeremiah52:23 There were 96 pomegranates on the sides
Jeremiah52:24 The captain of the guard took along with him as his prisoners: Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 one of the commanding officers of the army
Jeremiah52:26 He took them to the king of Babylon at Riblah
Jeremiah52:27 where the king killed them all. So it was that Judah's exile was accomplished.
Jeremiah52:28 The number of captives taken to Babylon in the seventh year of Nebuchadnezzar's reign was 3
Jeremiah52:29 Then
Jeremiah52:30 five years after that he sent Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:31 On February 25 of the thirty-seventh year of the imprisonment in Babylon of Jehoiachin
Jeremiah52:32 He spoke pleasantly to him and gave him preference over all the other kings in Babylon;
Jeremiah52:33 he gave him new clothes and fed him from the king's kitchen as long as he lived.
Jeremiah52:34 And he was given a regular allowance to cover his daily needs until the day of his death.
Lamentations1:1 Jerusalem's streets
Lamentations1:2 She sobs through the night; tears run down her cheeks. Among all her lovers
Lamentations1:3 Why is Judah led away
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her enemies prosper
Lamentations1:6 All her beauty and her majesty are gone; her princes are like starving deer that search for pasture—helpless game too weak to keep on running from their foes.
Lamentations1:7 And now in the midst of all Jerusalem's sadness she remembers happy bygone days. She thinks of all the precious joys she had before her mocking enemy struck her down—and there was no one to give her aid.
Lamentations1:8 For Jerusalem sinned so horribly; therefore
Lamentations1:9 She indulged herself in immorality and refused to face the fact that punishment was sure to come. Now she lies in the gutter with no one left to lift her out. “O Lord
Lamentations1:10 Her enemies have plundered her completely
Lamentations1:11 Her people groan and cry for bread; they have sold all they have for food to give a little strength. “Look
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 He has sent fire from heaven that burns within my bones; he has placed a pitfall in my path and turned me back. He has left me sick and desolate the whole day through.
Lamentations1:14 He wove my sins into ropes to hitch me to a yoke of slavery. He sapped my strength and gave me to my enemies; I am helpless in their hands.
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has trampled all my mighty men. A great army has come at his command to crush the noblest youth. The Lord has trampled his beloved city as grapes in a winepress.
Lamentations1:16 For all these things I weep; tears flow down my cheeks. My Comforter is far away—he who alone could help me. My children have no future; we are a conquered land.
Lamentations1:17 Jerusalem pleads for help
Lamentations1:18 And the Lord is right
Lamentations1:19 I begged my allies for their help. False hope—they could not help at all. Nor could my priests and elders—they were starving in the streets while searching through the garbage dumps for bread.
Lamentations1:20 See
Lamentations1:21 Hear my groans! And there is no one anywhere to help. All my enemies have heard my troubles
Lamentations1:22 Look also on their sins
Lamentations2:1 A cloud of anger from the Lord has overcast Jerusalem; the fairest city of Israel lies in the dust of the earth
Lamentations2:2 The Lord without mercy has destroyed every home in Israel. In his wrath he has broken every fortress
Lamentations2:3 All the strength of Israel vanishes beneath his wrath. He has withdrawn his protection as the enemy attacks. God burns across the land of Israel like a raging fire.
Lamentations2:4 He bends his bow against his people as though he were an enemy. His strength is used against them to kill their finest youth. His fury is poured out like fire upon them.
Lamentations2:5 Yes
Lamentations2:6 He has violently broken down his Temple as though it were a booth of leaves and branches in a garden! No longer can the people celebrate their holy feasts and Sabbaths. Kings and priests together fall before his wrath.
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected his own altar
Lamentations2:8 The Lord determined to destroy Jerusalem. He laid out an unalterable line of destruction. Therefore the ramparts and walls fell down before him.
Lamentations2:9 Jerusalem's gates are useless. All their locks and bars are broken
Lamentations2:10 The elders of Jerusalem sit upon the ground in silence
Lamentations2:11 I have cried until the tears no longer come; my heart is broken
Lamentations2:12 “Mama
Lamentations2:13 In all the world has there ever been such sorrow? O Jerusalem
Lamentations2:14 Your “prophets“ have said so many foolish things
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by scoff and shake their heads and say
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies deride you. They hiss and grind their teeth and say
Lamentations2:17 But it is the Lord who did it
Lamentations2:18 Then the people wept before the Lord. O walls of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:19 Rise in the night and cry to your God. Pour out your hearts like water to the Lord; lift up your hands to him; plead for your children as they faint with hunger in the streets.
Lamentations2:20 O Lord
Lamentations2:21 See them lying in the streets—old and young
Lamentations2:22 You have deliberately called for this destruction; in the day of your anger none escaped or remained. All my little children lie dead upon the streets before the enemy.
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen the afflictions that come from the rod of God's wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has brought me into deepest darkness
Lamentations3:3 He has turned against me. Day and night his hand is heavy on me.
Lamentations3:4 He has made me old and has broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has built forts against me and surrounded me with anguish and distress.
Lamentations3:6 He buried me in dark places
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in; I cannot escape; he has fastened me with heavy chains.
Lamentations3:8 And though I cry and shout
Lamentations3:9 He has shut me into a place of high
Lamentations3:10 He lurks like a bear
Lamentations3:11 He has dragged me into the underbrush and torn me with his claws
Lamentations3:12 He has bent his bow and aimed it squarely at me
Lamentations3:13 and sent his arrows deep within my heart.
Lamentations3:14 My own people laugh at me; all day long they sing their ribald songs.
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness and given me a cup of deepest sorrows to drink.
Lamentations3:16 He has made me eat gravel and broken my teeth; he has rolled me in ashes and dirt.
Lamentations3:17 O Lord
Lamentations3:18 All hope is gone; my strength has turned to water
Lamentations3:19 Oh
Lamentations3:20 For I can never forget these awful years; always my soul will live in utter shame.
Lamentations3:21 Yet there is one ray of hope:
Lamentations3:22 his compassion never ends. It is only the Lord's mercies that have kept us from complete destruction.
Lamentations3:23 Great is his faithfulness; his loving-kindness begins afresh each day.
Lamentations3:24 My soul claims the Lord as my inheritance; therefore I will hope in him.
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is wonderfully good to those who wait for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good both to hope and wait quietly for the salvation of the Lord.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a young man to be under discipline
Lamentations3:28 for it causes him to sit apart in silence beneath the Lord's demands
Lamentations3:29 to lie face downward in the dust; then at last there is hope for him.
Lamentations3:30 Let him turn the other cheek to those who strike him and accept their awful insults
Lamentations3:31 for the Lord will not abandon him forever.
Lamentations3:32 Although God gives him grief
Lamentations3:33 For he does not enjoy afflicting men and causing sorrow.
Lamentations3:34 But you have trampled and crushed beneath your feet the lowly of the world
Lamentations3:35 and deprived men of their God-given rights
Lamentations3:36 and refused them justice. No wonder the Lord has had to deal with you!
Lamentations3:37 For who can act against you without the Lord's permission?
Lamentations3:38 It is the Lord who helps one and harms another.
Lamentations3:39 Why then should we
Lamentations3:40 Let us examine ourselves instead
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift our hearts and hands to him in heaven
Lamentations3:42 for we have sinned; we have rebelled against the Lord
Lamentations3:43 You have engulfed us by your anger
Lamentations3:44 You have veiled yourself as with a cloud so that our prayers do not reach through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us as refuse and garbage among the nations.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have spoken out against us.
Lamentations3:47 We are filled with fear
Lamentations3:48 My eyes flow day and night with never-ending streams
Lamentations3:49 of tears because of the destruction of my people.
Lamentations3:50 Oh
Lamentations3:51 My heart is breaking over what is happening to the young girls of Jerusalem.
Lamentations3:52 My enemies
Lamentations3:53 They threw me in a well and capped it with a rock.
Lamentations3:54 The water flowed above my head. I thought
Lamentations3:55 But I called upon your name
Lamentations3:56 and you heard me! You listened to my pleading; you heard my weeping!
Lamentations3:57 Yes
Lamentations3:58 O Lord
Lamentations3:59 You have seen the wrong they did to me; be my Judge
Lamentations3:60 You have seen the plots my foes have laid against me.
Lamentations3:61 You have heard the vile names they have called me
Lamentations3:62 and all they say about me and their whispered plans.
Lamentations3:63 See how they laugh and sing with glee
Lamentations3:64 O Lord
Lamentations3:65 Harden their hearts and curse them
Lamentations3:66 Go after them in fierce pursuit and wipe them off the earth
Lamentations4:1 How the finest gold has lost its luster! For the inlaid Temple walls are scattered in the streets!
Lamentations4:2 The cream of our youth—the finest of the gold—are treated as earthenware pots.
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals feed their young
Lamentations4:4 The children's tongues stick to the roofs of their mouths for thirst
Lamentations4:5 Those who used to eat fastidiously are begging in the streets for anything at all. Those brought up in palaces now scratch in garbage pits for food.
Lamentations4:6 For the sin of my people is greater than that of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Our princes were lean and tanned
Lamentations4:8 but now their faces are as black as soot. No one can recognize them. Their skin sticks to their bones; it is dry and hard and withered.
Lamentations4:9 Those killed by the sword are far better off than those who die of slow starvation.
Lamentations4:10 Tenderhearted women have cooked and eaten their own children; thus they survived the siege.
Lamentations4:11 But now at last the anger of the Lord is satisfied; his fiercest anger has been poured out. He started a fire in Jerusalem that burned it down to its foundations.
Lamentations4:12 Not a king in all the earth—no one in all the world—would have believed an enemy could enter through Jerusalem's gates!
Lamentations4:13 Yet God permitted it because of the sins of her prophets and priests
Lamentations4:14 Now these same men are blindly staggering through the streets
Lamentations4:15 “Get away!“ the people shout at them. “You are defiled!“ They flee to distant lands and wander there among the foreigners; but none will let them stay.
Lamentations4:16 The Lord himself has dealt with them; he no longer helps them
Lamentations4:17 We look for our allies to come and save us
Lamentations4:18 We can't go into the streets without danger to our lives. Our end is near—our days are numbered. We are doomed.
Lamentations4:19 Our enemies are swifter than the eagles; if we flee to the mountains they find us. If we hide in the wilderness
Lamentations4:20 Our king—the life of our life
Lamentations4:21 Do you rejoice
Lamentations4:22 Israel's exile for her sins will end at last
Lamentations5:1 O Lord
Lamentations5:2 Our homes
Lamentations5:3 We are orphans—our fathers dead
Lamentations5:4 We must even pay for water to drink; our fuel is sold to us at the highest of prices.
Lamentations5:5 We bow our necks beneath the victors' feet; unending work is now our lot.
Lamentations5:6 We beg for bread from Egypt
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned but died before the hand of judgment fell. We have borne the blow that they deserved!
Lamentations5:8 Our former servants have become our masters; there is no one left to save us.
Lamentations5:9 We went into the wilderness to hunt for food
Lamentations5:10 Our skin was black from famine.
Lamentations5:11 They rape the women of Jerusalem and the girls in Judah's cities.
Lamentations5:12 Our princes are hanged by their thumbs. Even aged men are treated with contempt.
Lamentations5:13 They take away the young men to grind their grain
Lamentations5:14 The old men sit no longer in the city gates; the young no longer dance and sing.
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our hearts has ended; our dance has turned to death.
Lamentations5:16 Our glory is gone. The crown is fallen from our head. Woe upon us for our sins.
Lamentations5:17 Our hearts are faint and weary; our eyes grow dim.
Lamentations5:18 Jerusalem and the Temple of the Lord are desolate
Lamentations5:19 O Lord
Lamentations5:20 Why do you forget us forever? Why do you forsake us for so long?
Lamentations5:21 Turn us around and bring us back to you again! That is our only hope! Give us back the joys we used to have!
Lamentations5:22 Or have you utterly rejected us? Are you angry with us still?
Ezekiel1:1 Ezekiel was a priest (the son of Buzi) who lived with the Jewish exiles beside the Chebar Canal in Babylon.
Ezekiel1:2 One day late in June
Ezekiel1:3 heavens were suddenly opened to me and I saw visions from God.
Ezekiel1:4 I saw
Ezekiel1:5 Then from the center of the cloud
Ezekiel1:6 except that each had four faces and two pairs of wings!
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were like those of men
Ezekiel1:8 And beneath each of their wings I could see human hands.
Ezekiel1:9 The four living beings were joined wing to wing
Ezekiel1:10 Each had the face of a man in front
Ezekiel1:11 Each had two pairs of wings spreading out from the middle of his back. One pair stretched out to attach to the wings of the living beings on each side
Ezekiel1:12 Wherever their spirit went they went
Ezekiel1:13 Going up and down among them were other forms that glowed like bright coals of fire or brilliant torches
Ezekiel1:14 The living beings darted to and fro
Ezekiel1:15 As I stared at all of this
Ezekiel1:16 The wheels looked as if they were made of polished amber
Ezekiel1:17 They could go in any of the four directions without having to face around.
Ezekiel1:18 The four wheels had rims and spokes
Ezekiel1:19 When the four living beings flew forward
Ezekiel1:20 When the living beings stopped
Ezekiel1:21 For the spirit of the four living beings was in the wheels; so wherever their spirit went
Ezekiel1:22 The sky spreading out above them looked as though it were made of crystal; it was inexpressibly beautiful.
Ezekiel1:23 The wings of each stretched straight out to touch the others' wings
Ezekiel1:24 And as they flew
Ezekiel1:25 And every time they stopped
Ezekiel1:26 For high in the sky above them was what looked like a throne made of beautiful blue sapphire stones
Ezekiel1:27 From his waist up
Ezekiel1:28 like a rainbow all around him. That was the way the glory of the Lord appeared to me. And when I saw it

Ezekiel4:2 Draw a picture of siege mounds being built against the city
Ezekiel4:3 And put an iron plate between you and the city
Ezekiel4:4 “Now lie on your left side for 390 days
Ezekiel4:5 Each day you lie there represents a year of punishment ahead for Israel.
Ezekiel4:6 Afterwards
Ezekiel4:7 “Meanwhile continue your demonstration of the siege of Jerusalem; lie there with your arm bared to signify great strength and power in the attack against her. This will prophesy her doom.
Ezekiel4:8 And I will paralyze you so that you can't turn over from one side to the other until you have completed all the days of your siege.
Ezekiel4:9 “During the first 390 days eat bread made of flour mixed from wheat
Ezekiel4:10 You are to ration this out to yourself at the rate of eight ounces at a time
Ezekiel4:11 And use one quart of water a day; don't use more than that.
Ezekiel4:12 Each day take flour from the barrel and prepare it as you would barley cakes. While all the people are watching

Ezekiel4:15 Then the Lord said

Ezekiel4:17 I will cause the people to lack both bread and water; they will look at one another in frantic terror and waste away beneath their punishment.

Ezekiel6:13 When your slain lie scattered among your idols and altars on every hill and mountain and under every green tree and great oak where they offered incense to their gods—you will realize that I alone am God.

Ezekiel12:1 Again a message came to me from the Lord:
Ezekiel12:2 “Son of dust
Ezekiel12:3 for they are rebels. So now put on a demonstration to show them what being exiled will be like. Pack whatever you can carry on your back and leave your home—go somewhere else. Go in the daylight so they can see
Ezekiel12:4 Bring your baggage outside your house during the daylight so they can watch. Then leave the house at night
Ezekiel12:5 Dig a tunnel through the city wall while they are observing and carry your possessions out through the hole.
Ezekiel12:6 As they watch
Ezekiel12:7 So I did as I was told. I brought my pack outside in the daylight—all I could take into exile—and in the evening I dug through the wall with my hands. I went out into the darkness with my pack on my shoulder while the people looked on.
Ezekiel12:8 The next morning this message came to me from the Lord:
Ezekiel12:9 “Son of dust
Ezekiel12:10 Tell them the Lord God says it is a message to King Zedekiah in Jerusalem and to all the people of Israel.
Ezekiel12:11 Explain that what you did was a demonstration of what is going to happen to them

Ezekiel16:2 “Son of dust
Ezekiel16:3 Tell her

Ezekiel16:6 'But I came by and saw you there
Ezekiel16:7 Thrive like a plant in the field!' And you did! You grew up and became tall
Ezekiel16:8 'Later
Ezekiel16:9 Then
Ezekiel16:10 clothes of linens and silk

Ezekiel16:12 a ring for your nose and two more for your ears
Ezekiel16:13 And so you were made beautiful with gold and silver
Ezekiel16:14 Your reputation was great among the nations for your beauty; it was perfect because of all the gifts I gave you
Ezekiel16:15 'But you thought you could get along without me—you trusted in your beauty instead; and you gave yourself as a prostitute to every man who came along. Your beauty was his for the asking.
Ezekiel16:16 You used the lovely things I gave you for making idol shrines and to decorate your bed of prostitution. Unbelievable! There has never been anything like it before!
Ezekiel16:17 You took the very jewels and gold and silver ornaments I gave to you and made statues of men and worshiped them
Ezekiel16:18 You used the beautifully embroidered clothes I gave you—to cover your idols! And used my oil and incense to worship them!
Ezekiel16:19 You set before them as a lovely sacrifice—imagine it—the fine flour and oil and honey I gave you!
Ezekiel16:20 And you took my sons and daughters you had borne to me and sacrificed them to your gods; and they are gone. Wasn't it enough that you should be a prostitute?
Ezekiel16:21 Must you also slay my children by sacrificing them to idols?
Ezekiel16:22 'And in all these years of adultery and sin you have not thought of those days long ago when you were naked and covered with blood.
Ezekiel16:23 'And then
Ezekiel16:24 you built a spacious brothel for your lovers and idol altars on every street
Ezekiel16:25 and there you offered your beauty to every man who came by
Ezekiel16:26 And you added lustful Egypt to your prostitutions by your alliance with her. My anger is great.
Ezekiel16:27 'Therefore I have crushed you with my fist; I have reduced your boundaries and delivered you into the hands of those who hate you—the Philistines—and even they are ashamed of you.
Ezekiel16:28 'You have committed adultery with the Assyrians too by making them your allies and worshiping their gods; it seems that you can never find enough new gods. After your adultery there
Ezekiel16:29 so you worshiped the gods of that great merchant land of Babylon—and you still weren't satisfied.
Ezekiel16:30 What a filthy heart you have
Ezekiel16:31 building your idol altars
Ezekiel16:32 Yes
Ezekiel16:33 Prostitutes charge for their services—men pay with many gifts. But not you
Ezekiel16:34 So you are different from other prostitutes. But you had to pay them
Ezekiel16:35 'O prostitute
Ezekiel16:36 The Lord God says: Because I see your filthy sins
Ezekiel16:37 this is what I am going to do: I will gather together all your allies—these lovers of yours you have sinned with
Ezekiel16:38 I will punish you as a murderess is punished and as a woman breaking wedlock living with other men.
Ezekiel16:39 I will give you to your lovers—these many nations—to destroy
Ezekiel16:40 They will burn your homes
Ezekiel16:41 And I will see to it that you stop your adulteries with other gods and end your payments to your allies for their love.
Ezekiel16:42 'Then at last my fury against you will die away; my jealousy against you will end
Ezekiel16:43 But first

Ezekiel16:45 For your mother loathed her husband and her children
Ezekiel16:46 'Your older sister is Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 You have not merely sinned as they do—no
Ezekiel16:48 'As I live
Ezekiel16:49 Your sister Sodom's sins were pride
Ezekiel16:50 She insolently worshiped many idols as I watched. Therefore I crushed her.
Ezekiel16:51 'Even Samaria has not committed half your sins. You have worshiped idols far more than your sisters have; they seem almost righteous in comparison with you!

Ezekiel16:55 'Yes

Ezekiel16:57 But now your greater wickedness has been exposed to all the world

Ezekiel16:62 I will reaffirm my covenant with you

Ezekiel18:1 Then the Lord's message came to me again.
Ezekiel18:2 “Why do people use this proverb about the land of Israel: The children are punished for their fathers' sins?
Ezekiel18:3 As I live
Ezekiel18:4 for all souls are mine to judge—fathers and sons alike—and my rule is this: It is for a man's own sins that he will die.
Ezekiel18:5 “But if a man is just and does what is lawful and right
Ezekiel18:6 and has not gone out to the mountains to feast before the idols of Israel and worship them
Ezekiel18:7 if he is a merciful creditor
Ezekiel18:8 and if he grants loans without interest
Ezekiel18:9 and obeys my laws—that man is just
Ezekiel18:10 “But if that man has a son who is a robber or murderer and who fulfills none of his responsibilities
Ezekiel18:11 who refuses to obey the laws of God but worships idols on the mountains and commits adultery
Ezekiel18:12 oppresses the poor and helpless
Ezekiel18:13 and loans out his money at interest—shall that man live? No! He shall surely die
Ezekiel18:14 “But if this sinful man has
Ezekiel18:15 he doesn't go up on the mountains to feast before the idols and worship them and does not commit adultery;
Ezekiel18:16 he is fair to those who borrow from him and doesn't rob them
Ezekiel18:17 helps the poor
Ezekiel18:18 But his father shall die for his own sins because he is cruel and robs and does wrong.
Ezekiel18:19 'What?' you ask. 'Doesn't the son pay for his father's sins?' No! For if the son does what is right and keeps my laws
Ezekiel18:20 The one who sins is the one who dies. The son shall not be punished for his father's sins
Ezekiel18:21 But if a wicked person turns away from all his sins and begins to obey my laws and do what is just and right
Ezekiel18:22 All his past sins will be forgotten
Ezekiel18:23 “Do you think I like to see the wicked die?“ asks the Lord. “Of course not! I only want him to turn from his wicked ways and live.
Ezekiel18:24 However
Ezekiel18:25 “Yet you say: 'The Lord isn't being fair!' Listen to me
Ezekiel18:26 When a good man turns away from being good
Ezekiel18:27 And if a wicked person turns away from his wickedness and obeys the law and does right
Ezekiel18:28 for he has thought it over and decided to turn from his sins and live a good life. He shall surely live—he shall not die.
Ezekiel18:29 “And yet the people of Israel keep saying: 'The Lord is unfair!' O people of Israel
Ezekiel18:30 I will judge each of you
Ezekiel18:31 Put them behind you and receive a new heart and a new spirit. For why will you die
Ezekiel18:32 I do not enjoy seeing you die

Ezekiel19:4 Then the nations called out their hunters; they trapped him in a pit and brought him in chains to Egypt.
Ezekiel19:5 “When Israel
Ezekiel19:6 He became a leader among the lions and learned to catch prey
Ezekiel19:7 He demolished the palaces of the surrounding nations and ruined their cities; their farms were desolated
Ezekiel19:8 Then the armies of the nations surrounded him
Ezekiel19:9 They prodded him into a cage and brought him before the king of Babylon. He was held in captivity so that his voice could never again be heard upon the mountains of Israel.
Ezekiel19:10 “Your mother was like a vine beside an irrigation ditch
Ezekiel19:11 Its strongest branch became a ruler's scepter
Ezekiel19:12 But the vine was uprooted in fury and thrown down to the ground. Its branches were broken and withered by a strong wind from the east; the fruit was destroyed by fire.
Ezekiel19:13 Now the vine is planted in the wilderness where the ground is hard and dry.
Ezekiel19:14 It is decaying from within; no strong branch remains. The fulfillment of this sad prophecy has already begun

Ezekiel23:31 You have followed in your sister's footsteps
Ezekiel23:32 Yes
Ezekiel23:33 You will reel like a drunkard beneath the awful blows of sorrow and distress
Ezekiel23:34 In deep anguish you will drain that cup of terror to the very bottom and will lick the inside to get every drop. For I have spoken
Ezekiel23:35 “Because you have forgotten me and turned your backs on me
Ezekiel23:36 “Son of dust
Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed both adultery and murder; they have worshiped idols and murdered my children whom they bore to me
Ezekiel23:38 On the same day they defiled my Temple and ignored my Sabbaths
Ezekiel23:39 for when they had murdered their children in front of their idols
Ezekiel23:40 “You even sent away to distant lands for priests to come with other gods for you to serve
Ezekiel23:41 You sat together on a beautifully embroidered bed and put my incense and my oil upon a table spread before you.
Ezekiel23:42 From your apartment came the sound of many men carousing—lewd men and drunkards from the wilderness
Ezekiel23:43 Will they commit adultery with these who have become old harlot hags?
Ezekiel23:44 Yet that is what they did. They went in to them—to Samaria and Jerusalem
Ezekiel23:45 But just persons everywhere will judge them for what they really are—adulteresses and murderers. They will mete out to them the sentences the law demands.
Ezekiel23:46 The Lord God says: “Bring an army against them and hand them out to be crushed and despised.
Ezekiel23:47 For their enemies will stone them and kill them with swords; they will butcher their sons and daughters and burn their homes.
Ezekiel23:48 Thus will I make lewdness and idolatry to cease from the land. My judgment will be a lesson against idolatry for all to see.
Ezekiel23:49 For you will be fully repaid for all your harlotry

Ezekiel27:1 Then this message came to me from the Lord. He said:

Ezekiel27:6 They made your oars from oaks of Bashan. The walls of your cabin are of cypress from the southern coast of Cyprus.
Ezekiel27:7 Your sails are made of Egypt's finest linens; you stand beneath awnings bright with purple and scarlet dyes from eastern Cyprus.
Ezekiel27:8 'Your sailors come from Sidon and Arvad; your helmsmen are skilled men from Zemer.

Ezekiel27:20 while Dedan brings expensive saddlecloths for riding.

Ezekiel27:27 Everything is lost. Your riches and wares

Ezekiel27:34 Now you lie broken beneath the sea; all your merchandise and all your crew have perished with you.
Ezekiel27:35 All who live along the coastlands watch

Ezekiel38:11 You will have said

Ezekiel38:22 I will fight you with sword

Ezekiel39:5 You will never reach the cities—you will fall upon the open fields; for I have spoken
Ezekiel39:6 And I will rain down fire on Magog and on all your allies who live safely on the coasts
Ezekiel39:7 'Thus I will make known my holy name among my people Israel; I will not let it be mocked at anymore. And the nations
Ezekiel39:8 That day of judgment will come; everything will happen just as I have declared it.

Ezekiel39:11 'And I will make a vast graveyard for Gog and his armies in the Valley of the Travelers
Ezekiel39:12 It will take seven months for the people of Israel to bury the bodies.
Ezekiel39:13 Everyone in Israel will help

Ezekiel39:22 And from that time onward the people of Israel will know I am the Lord their God.
Ezekiel39:23 And the nations will know why Israel was sent away to exile—it was punishment for sin
Ezekiel39:24 I turned my face away and punished them in proportion to the vileness of their sins.
Ezekiel39:25 “But now

Ezekiel39:28 Then my people will know I am the Lord their God—responsible for sending them away to exile
Ezekiel39:29 And I will never hide my face from them again
Ezekiel40:1 Early in April of the twenty-fifth year of our exile—the fourteenth year after Jerusalem was captured—the hand of the Lord was upon me

Ezekiel44:7 by letting the uncircumcised into my sanctuary—those who have no heart for God—when you offer me my food
Ezekiel44:8 You have not kept the laws I gave you concerning these holy affairs

Ezekiel44:14 They are the Temple caretakers

Ezekiel44:19 When they return to the outer court
Ezekiel44:20 “They must not let their hair grow too long nor shave it off. Regular
Ezekiel44:21 No priest may drink wine before coming to the inner court.

Ezekiel44:25 “A priest must not defile himself by being in the presence of a dead person

Daniel6:2 The governors were accountable to three presidents (Daniel was one of them) so the king could administer the kingdom efficiently.
Daniel6:3 Daniel soon proved himself more capable than all the other presidents and governors
Daniel6:4 This made the other presidents and governors very jealous
Daniel6:5 So they concluded
Daniel6:6 They decided to go to the king and say

Daniel6:8 Your Majesty

Daniel6:12 They rushed back to the king and reminded him about his law. “Haven't you signed a decree
Daniel6:13 Then they told the king
Daniel6:14 Hearing this
Daniel6:15 In the evening the men came again to the king and said
Daniel6:16 So at last the king gave the order for Daniel's arrest
Daniel6:17 A stone was brought and placed over the mouth of the den; and the king sealed it with his own signet ring and with that of his government
Daniel6:18 Then the king returned to his palace and went to bed without dinner. He refused his usual entertainment and didn't sleep all night.
Daniel6:19 Very early the next morning he hurried out to the lions' den
Daniel6:20 and called out in anguish
Daniel6:21 Then he heard a voice! “Your Majesty
Daniel6:22 “My God has sent his angel
Daniel6:23 The king was beside himself with joy and ordered Daniel lifted from the den. And not a scratch was found on him because he believed in his God.
Daniel6:24 Then the king issued a command to bring the men who had accused Daniel and throw them into the den along with their children and wives
Daniel6:25 Afterward King Darius wrote this message addressed to everyone in his empire: “Greetings!
Daniel6:26 I decree that everyone shall tremble and fear before the God of Daniel in every part of my kingdom. For his God is the living
Daniel6:27 He delivers his people
Daniel6:28 So Daniel prospered in the reign of Darius and in the reign of Cyrus the Persian.

Daniel7:4 The first was like a lion

Hosea7:2 “Her people never seem to recognize that I am watching them. Their sinful deeds give them away on every side; I see them all.

Hosea7:4 They are all adulterers; as a baker's oven is constantly aflame—except after he kneads the dough and waits for it to rise again—so are these people constantly aflame with lust.

Hosea7:6 Their hearts blaze like a furnace with intrigue. Their plot smolders through the night

Hosea9:1 O Israel
Hosea9:2 Therefore your harvests will be small; your grapes will blight upon the vine.
Hosea9:3 You may no longer stay here in this land of God; you will be carried off to Egypt and Assyria and live there on scraps of food.
Hosea9:4 There
Hosea9:5 What then will you do on holy days
Hosea9:6 when you are carried off to Assyria as slaves? Who will inherit your possessions left behind? Egypt will! She will gather your dead; Memphis will bury them. And thorns and thistles will grow up among the ruins.
Hosea9:7 The time of Israel's punishment has come; the day of recompense is almost here
Hosea9:8 “I appointed the prophets to guard my people
Hosea9:9 The things my people do are as depraved as what they did in Gibeah long ago. The Lord does not forget. He will surely punish them.

Hosea9:11 The glory of Israel flies away like a bird
Hosea9:12 And if your children grow

Hosea9:17 My God will destroy the people of Israel because they will not listen or obey. They will be wandering Jews

Hosea10:2 The hearts of her people are false toward God. They are guilty and must be punished. God will break down their heathen altars and smash their idols.“
Hosea10:3 Then they will say

Hosea10:7 As for Samaria
Hosea10:8 And the idol altars of Aven at Bethel where Israel sinned will crumble. Thorns and thistles will grow up to surround them. And the people will cry to the mountains and hills to fall upon them and crush them.
Hosea10:9 “O Israel
Hosea10:10 I will come against you for your disobedience; I will gather the armies of the nations against you to punish you for your heaped-up sins.
Hosea10:11 “Ephraim is accustomed to treading out the grain—an easy job she loves. I have never put her under a heavy yoke before; I have spared her tender neck. But now I will harness her to the plow and harrow. Her days of ease are gone.
Hosea10:12 “Plant the good seeds of righteousness
Hosea10:13 “But you have cultivated wickedness and raised a thriving crop of sins. You have earned the full reward of trusting in a lie—believing that military might and great armies can make a nation safe!
Hosea10:14 “Therefore
Hosea10:15 That will be your fate

Joel1:1 This message came from the Lord to Joel
Joel1:2 Listen
Joel1:3 In years to come
Joel1:4 After the cutter-locusts finish eating your crops
Joel1:5 Wake up and weep
Joel1:6 A vast army of locusts covers the land. It is a terrible army too numerous to count
Joel1:7 They have ruined my vines and stripped the bark from the fig trees
Joel1:8 Weep with sorrow
Joel1:9 Gone are the offerings of grain and wine to bring to the Temple of the Lord; the priests are starving. Hear the crying of these ministers of God.
Joel1:10 The fields are bare of crops. Sorrow and sadness are everywhere. The grain
Joel1:11 Well may you farmers stand so shocked and stricken; well may you vinedressers weep. Weep for the wheat and the barley
Joel1:12 The grapevines are dead; the fig trees are dying; the pomegranates wither; the apples shrivel on the trees; all joy has withered with them.
Joel1:13 O priests
Joel1:14 Announce a fast; call a solemn meeting. Gather the elders and all the people into the Temple of the Lord your God
Joel1:15 Alas
Joel1:16 Our food will disappear before our eyes; all joy and gladness will be ended in the Temple of our God.
Joel1:17 The seed rots in the ground; the barns and granaries are empty; the grain has dried up in the fields.
Joel1:18 The cattle groan with hunger; the herds stand perplexed
Joel1:19 Lord
Joel1:20 Even the wild animals cry to you for help
Joel2:1 Sound the alarm in Jerusalem! Let the blast of the warning trumpet be heard upon my holy mountain! Let everyone tremble in fear
Joel2:2 It is a day of darkness and gloom
Joel2:3 Fire goes before them and follows them on every side! Ahead of them the land lies fair as Eden's Garden in all its beauty
Joel2:4 They look like tiny horses
Joel2:5 Look at them leaping along the tops of the mountain! Listen to the noise they make
Joel2:6 Fear grips the waiting people; their faces grow pale with fright.
Joel2:7 These “soldiers“ charge like infantry; they scale the walls like picked and trained commandos. Straight forward they march
Joel2:8 They never crowd each other. Each is right in place. No weapon can stop them.
Joel2:9 They swarm upon the city; they run upon the walls; they climb up into the houses
Joel2:10 The earth quakes before them and the heavens tremble. The sun and moon are obscured and the stars are hid.
Joel2:11 The Lord leads them with a shout. This is his mighty army
Joel2:12 That is why the Lord says
Joel2:13 Let your remorse tear at your hearts and not your garments.“ Return to the Lord your God
Joel2:14 Who knows? Perhaps even yet he will decide to let you alone and give you a blessing instead of his terrible curse. Perhaps he will give you so much that you can offer your grain and wine to the Lord as before!
Joel2:15 Sound the trumpet in Zion! Call a fast and gather all the people together for a solemn meeting.
Joel2:16 Bring everyone—the elders
Joel2:17 The priests
Joel2:18 Then the Lord will pity his people and be indignant for the honor of his land!
Joel2:19 He will reply
Joel2:20 I will remove these armies from the north and send them far away; I will turn them back into the parched wastelands where they will die; half shall be driven into the Dead Sea and the rest into the Mediterranean
Joel2:21 Fear not
Joel2:22 Let the flocks and herds forget their hunger; the pastures will turn green again. The trees will bear their fruit; the fig trees and grape vines will flourish once more.
Joel2:23 Rejoice
Joel2:24 The threshing floors will pile high again with wheat
Joel2:25 “And I will give you back the crops the locusts ate!—my great destroying army that I sent against you.
Joel2:26 Once again you will have all the food you want. “Praise the Lord
Joel2:27 And you will know that I am here among my people Israel
Joel2:28 “After I have poured out my rains again
Joel2:29 And I will pour out my Spirit even on your slaves
Joel2:30 and put strange symbols in the earth and sky—blood and fire and pillars of smoke.
Joel2:31 “The sun will be turned into darkness and the moon to blood before the great and terrible Day of the Lord shall come.
Joel2:32 “Everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord will be saved; even in Jerusalem some will escape

Amos4:1 Listen to me
Amos4:2 The Lord God has sworn by his holiness that the time will come when he will put hooks in your noses and lead you away like the cattle you are; they will drag the last of you away with fishhooks!
Amos4:3 You will be hauled from your beautiful homes and tossed out through the nearest breach in the wall. The Lord has said it.
Amos4:4 Go ahead and sacrifice to idols at Bethel and Gilgal. Keep disobeying—your sins are mounting up. Sacrifice each morning and bring your tithes twice a week!
Amos4:5 Go through all your proper forms and give extra offerings. How you pride yourselves and crow about it everywhere!
Amos4:6 “I sent you hunger
Amos4:7 I ruined your crops by holding back the rain three months before the harvest. I sent rain on one city but not another. While rain fell on one field
Amos4:8 People from two or three cities would make their weary journey for a drink of water to a city that had rain
Amos4:9 “I sent blight and mildew on your farms and your vineyards; the locusts ate your figs and olive trees. And still you wouldn't return to me

Amos6:7 Therefore you will be the first to be taken as slaves; suddenly your revelry will end.

Amos6:10 A man's uncle will be the only one left to bury him
Amos6:11 For the Lord commanded this: That homes both great and small should be smashed to pieces.
Amos6:12 Can horses run on rocks? Can oxen plow the sea? Stupid even to ask—but no more stupid than what you do when you make a mockery of justice and corrupt and sour all that should be good and right.
Amos6:13 And just as stupid is your rejoicing in how great you are when you are less than nothing—and priding yourselves on your own tiny power!

Obadiah1:1 In a vision the Lord God showed Obadiah the future of the land of Edom. “A report has come from the Lord
Obadiah1:2 “I will cut you down to size among the nations
Obadiah1:3 “You are proud because you live in those high
Obadiah1:4 Though you soar as high as eagles
Obadiah1:5 “Far better it would be for you if thieves had come at night to plunder you—for they would not take everything! Or if your vineyards were robbed of all their fruit—for at least the gleanings would be left!
Obadiah1:6 Every nook and cranny will be searched and robbed
Obadiah1:7 “All your allies will turn against you and help to push you out of your land. They will promise peace while plotting your destruction. Your trusted friends will set traps for you
Obadiah1:8 In that day not one wise man will be left in all of Edom!“ says the Lord. “For I will fill the wise men of Edom with stupidity.
Obadiah1:9 The mightiest soldiers of Teman will be confused
Obadiah1:10 “And why? Because of what you did to your brother Israel. Now your sins will be exposed for all to see; ashamed and defenseless
Obadiah1:11 For you deserted Israel in his time of need. You stood aloof
Obadiah1:12 “You should not have done it. You should not have gloated when they took him far away to foreign lands; you should not have rejoiced in the day of his misfortune; you should not have mocked in his time of need.
Obadiah1:13 You yourselves went into the land of Israel in the day of his calamity and looted him. You made yourselves rich at his expense.
Obadiah1:14 You stood at the crossroads and killed those trying to escape; you captured the survivors and returned them to their enemies in that terrible time of his distress.
Obadiah1:15 The Lord's vengeance will soon fall upon all Gentile nations. As you have done to Israel
Obadiah1:16 You drank my cup of punishment upon my holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 “But Jerusalem will become a refuge
Obadiah1:18 Israel will be a fire that sets the dry fields of Edom aflame. There will be no survivors
Obadiah1:19 Then my people who live in the Negeb shall occupy the hill country of Edom; those living in Judean lowlands shall possess the Philistine plains and repossess the fields of Ephraim and Samaria. And the people of Benjamin shall possess Gilead.
Obadiah1:20 The Israeli exiles shall return and occupy the Phoenician coastal strip as far north as Zarephath. Those exiled in Asia Minor shall return to their homeland and conquer the Negeb's outlying villages.
Obadiah1:21 For deliverers will come to Jerusalem and rule all Edom. And the Lord shall be King!

Zechariah1:1 Subject: messages from the Lord. These messages from the Lord were given to Zechariah (son of Berechiah and grandson of Iddo the prophet) in early November of the second year of the reign of King Darius.
Zechariah1:2 The Lord Almighty was very angry with your fathers.
Zechariah1:3 But he will turn again and favor you if only you return to him.
Zechariah1:4 Don't be like your fathers were! The earlier prophets pled in vain with them to turn from all their evil ways. “Come
Zechariah1:5 Your fathers and their prophets are now long dead
Zechariah1:6 but remember the lesson they learned
Zechariah1:7 The following February
Zechariah1:8 I saw a Man sitting on a red horse that was standing among the myrtle trees beside a river. Behind him were other horses
Zechariah1:9 An angel stood beside me
Zechariah1:10 Then the rider on the red horse—he was the Angel of the Lord—answered me
Zechariah1:11 Then the other riders reported to the Angel of the Lord
Zechariah1:12 Upon hearing this
Zechariah1:13 And the Lord answered the angel who stood beside me
Zechariah1:14 Then the angel said
Zechariah1:15 I am very angry with the heathen nations sitting around at ease
Zechariah1:16 Therefore the Lord declares: 'I have returned to Jerusalem filled with mercy; my Temple will be rebuilt
Zechariah1:17 Say it again: 'The Lord Almighty declares that the cities of Israel will again overflow with prosperity
Zechariah1:18 Then I looked and saw four animal horns!
Zechariah1:19 “What are these?“ I asked the angel. He replied
Zechariah1:20 Then the Lord showed me four blacksmiths.
Zechariah1:21 “What have these men come to do?“ I asked. The angel replied

Zechariah7:1 Another message came to me from the Lord in late November of the fourth year of the reign of King Darius.
Zechariah7:2 The Jews of the city of Bethel had sent a group of men headed by Sharezer
Zechariah7:3 and to speak with the priests and prophets about whether they must continue their traditional custom of fasting and mourning during the month of August each year

Zechariah7:5 “When you return to Bethel

Zechariah7:10 Tell them to stop oppressing widows and orphans
Zechariah7:11 Your fathers would not listen to this message. They turned stubbornly away and put their fingers in their ears to keep from hearing me.
Zechariah7:12 They hardened their hearts like flint
Zechariah7:13 I called
Zechariah7:14 I scattered them as with a whirlwind among the far-off nations. Their land became desolate; no one even traveled through it; the Pleasant Land lay bare and blighted.“

Zechariah8:2 The Lord Almighty says

Zechariah8:6 The Lord says
Zechariah8:7 You can be sure that I will rescue my people from east and west

Zechariah8:10 Before the work began there were no jobs

Zechariah8:14 If you do
Zechariah8:15 I did what I said I would when your fathers angered me and I promised to punish them
Zechariah8:16 Here is your part: Tell the truth. Be fair. Live at peace with everyone.
Zechariah8:17 Don't plot harm to others; don't swear that something is true when it isn't! How I hate all that sort of thing!“ says the Lord.

Zechariah9:2 “Doomed is Hamath
Zechariah9:3 Though Tyre has armed herself to the hilt and become so rich that silver is like dirt to her
Zechariah9:4 yet the Lord will dispossess her and hurl her fortifications into the sea; and she shall be set on fire and burned to the ground.
Zechariah9:5 “Ashkelon will see it happen and be filled with fear; Gaza will huddle in desperation
Zechariah9:6 “Foreigners will take over the city of Ashdod
Zechariah9:7 I will yank her idolatry out of her mouth and pull from her teeth her sacrifices that she eats with blood. Everyone left will worship God and be adopted into Israel as a new clan: the Philistines of Ekron will intermarry with the Jews
Zechariah9:8 And I will surround my Temple like a guard to keep invading armies from entering Israel. I am closely watching their movements
Zechariah9:9 “Rejoice greatly
Zechariah9:10 I will disarm all peoples of the earth

Zechariah9:13 Judah

Zechariah9:15 He will defend his people
Zechariah9:16 The Lord their God will save his people in that day
Zechariah9:17 How wonderful and beautiful all shall be! The abundance of grain and grapes will make the young men and girls flourish; they will be radiant with health and happiness.
Zechariah10:1 Ask the Lord for rain in the springtime
Zechariah10:2 How foolish to ask the idols for anything like that! Fortune-tellers' predictions are all a bunch of silly lies; what comfort is there in promises that don't come true? Judah and Israel have been led astray and wander like lost sheep; everyone attacks them
Zechariah10:3 “My anger burns against your 'shepherds'—your leaders—and I will punish them—these goats. For the Lord Almighty has arrived to help his flock of Judah. I will make them strong and glorious like a proud steed in battle.

Zechariah10:5 They will be mighty warriors for God

Malachi2:2 Indeed

Malachi2:5 “The purpose of these laws was to give him life and peace
Malachi2:6 He passed on to the people all the truth he got from me. He did not lie or cheat; he walked with me
Malachi2:7 “Priests' lips should flow with the knowledge of God so the people will learn God's laws. The priests are the messengers of the Lord Almighty
Malachi2:8 But not to you! For you have left God's paths. Your 'guidance' has caused many to stumble in sin. You have distorted the covenant of Levi and made it into a grotesque parody
Malachi2:9 “Therefore
Malachi2:10 We are children of the same father
Malachi2:11 In Judah

Malachi2:14 “Why has God abandoned us?“ you cry. I'll tell you why; it is because the Lord has seen your treachery in divorcing your wives who have been faithful to you through the years
Malachi2:15 You were united to your wife by the Lord. In God's wise plan
Malachi2:16 For the Lord
Malachi2:17 You have wearied the Lord with your words. “Wearied him?“ you ask in fake surprise. “How have we wearied him?“ By saying that evil is good

Malachi3:2 “But who can live when he appears? Who can endure his coming? For he is like a blazing fire refining precious metal
Malachi3:3 Like a refiner of silver he will sit and closely watch as the dross is burned away. He will purify the Levites
Malachi3:4 Then once more the Lord will enjoy the offerings brought to him by the people of Judah and Jerusalem
Malachi3:5 At that time my punishments will be quick and certain; I will move swiftly against wicked men who trick the innocent
Malachi3:6 “For I am the Lord—I do not change. That is why you are not already utterly destroyed for my mercy endures forever.
Malachi3:7 “Though you have scorned my laws from earliest time
Malachi3:8 “Will a man rob God? Surely not! And yet you have robbed me. 'What do you mean? When did we ever rob you?' “You have robbed me of the tithes and offerings due to me.
Malachi3:9 And so the awesome curse of God is cursing you
Malachi3:10 Bring all the tithes into the storehouse so that there will be food enough in my Temple; if you do
Malachi3:11 Your crops will be large
Malachi3:12 “And all nations will call you blessed
Malachi3:13 “Your attitude toward me has been proud and arrogant
Malachi3:14 “Listen; you have said
Malachi3:15 From now on
Malachi3:16 Then those who feared and loved the Lord spoke often of him to each other. And he had a Book of Remembrance drawn up in which he recorded the names of those who feared him and loved to think about him.
Malachi3:17 “They shall be mine

Matthew1:1 These are the ancestors of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac; Isaac was the father of Jacob; Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
Matthew1:3 Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah (Tamar was their mother); Perez was the father of Hezron; Hezron was the father of Aram;
Matthew1:4 Aram was the father of Amminadab; Amminadab was the father of Nahshon; Nahshon was the father of Salmon;
Matthew1:5 Salmon was the father of Boaz (Rahab was his mother); Boaz was the father of Obed (Ruth was his mother); Obed was the father of Jesse;
Matthew1:6 Jesse was the father of King David. David was the father of Solomon (his mother was the widow of Uriah);
Matthew1:7 Solomon was the father of Rehoboam; Rehoboam was the father of Abijah; Abijah was the father of Asa;
Matthew1:8 Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat; Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram; Joram was the father of Uzziah;
Matthew1:9 Uzziah was the father of Jotham; Jotham was the father of Ahaz; Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah;
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh; Manasseh was the father of Amos; Amos was the father of Josiah;
Matthew1:11 Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers (born at the time of the exile to Babylon).
Matthew1:12 After the exile: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel; Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel;
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud; Abiud was the father of Eliakim; Eliakim was the father of Azor;
Matthew1:14 Azor was the father of Zadok; Zadok was the father of Achim; Achim was the father of Eliud;
Matthew1:15 Eliud was the father of Eleazar; Eleazar was the father of Matthan; Matthan was the father of Jacob;
Matthew1:16 Jacob was the father of Joseph (who was the husband of Mary
Matthew1:17 These are fourteen of the generations from Abraham to King David; and fourteen from King David's time to the exile; and fourteen from the exile to Christ.
Matthew1:18 These are the facts concerning the birth of Jesus Christ: His mother
Matthew1:19 Then Joseph
Matthew1:20 As he lay awake considering this
Matthew1:21 And she will have a Son
Matthew1:22 This will fulfill God's message through his prophets—
Matthew1:23 Listen! The virgin shall conceive a child! She shall give birth to a Son
Matthew1:24 When Joseph awoke
Matthew1:25 but she remained a virgin until her Son was born; and Joseph named him “Jesus.“

Matthew5:1 One day as the crowds were gathering
Matthew5:2 and taught them there.
Matthew5:3 “Humble men are very fortunate!“ he told them
Matthew5:4 Those who mourn are fortunate! for they shall be comforted.
Matthew5:5 The meek and lowly are fortunate! for the whole wide world belongs to them.
Matthew5:6 “Happy are those who long to be just and good
Matthew5:7 Happy are the kind and merciful
Matthew5:8 Happy are those whose hearts are pure
Matthew5:9 Happy are those who strive for peace—they shall be called the sons of God.
Matthew5:10 Happy are those who are persecuted because they are good
Matthew5:11 “When you are reviled and persecuted and lied about because you are my followers—wonderful!
Matthew5:12 Be happy about it! Be very glad! for a tremendous reward awaits you up in heaven. And remember
Matthew5:13 “You are the world's seasoning
Matthew5:14 You are the world's light—a city on a hill
Matthew5:15 Don't hide your light!
Matthew5:16 Let it shine for all; let your good deeds glow for all to see
Matthew5:17 “Don't misunderstand why I have come—it isn't to cancel the laws of Moses and the warnings of the prophets. No
Matthew5:18 With all the earnestness I have I say: Every law in the Book will continue until its purpose is achieved.

Matthew6:25 “So my counsel is: Don't worry about things—food
Matthew6:26 Look at the birds! They don't worry about what to eat—they don't need to sow or reap or store up food—for your heavenly Father feeds them. And you are far more valuable to him than they are.
Matthew6:27 Will all your worries add a single moment to your life?
Matthew6:28 “And why worry about your clothes? Look at the field lilies! They don't worry about theirs.
Matthew6:29 Yet King Solomon in all his glory was not clothed as beautifully as they.
Matthew6:30 And if God cares so wonderfully for flowers that are here today and gone tomorrow
Matthew6:31 “So don't worry at all about having enough food and clothing. Why be like the heathen?
Matthew6:32 For they take pride in all these things and are deeply concerned about them. But your heavenly Father already knows perfectly well that you need them
Matthew6:33 and he will give them to you if you give him first place in your life and live as he wants you to.
Matthew6:34 “So don't be anxious about tomorrow. God will take care of your tomorrow too. Live one day at a time.

Matthew13:5 And some fell on rocky soil where there was little depth of earth; the plants sprang up quickly enough in the shallow soil

Matthew13:21 but he doesn't have much depth in his life

Matthew27:50 Then Jesus shouted out again

Matthew27:52 and tombs opened
Matthew27:53 After Jesus' resurrection
Matthew27:54 The soldiers at the crucifixion and their sergeant were terribly frightened by the earthquake and all that happened. They exclaimed
Matthew27:55 And many women who had come down from Galilee with Jesus to care for him were watching from a distance.
Matthew27:56 Among them were Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joseph
Matthew27:57 When evening came
Matthew27:58 went to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body. And Pilate issued an order to release it to him.
Matthew27:59 Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth
Matthew27:60 and placed it in his own new rock-hewn tomb
Matthew27:61 Both Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting nearby watching.
Matthew27:62 The next day—at the close of the first day of the Passover ceremonies—the chief priests and Pharisees went to Pilate
Matthew27:63 and told him
Matthew27:64 So we request an order from you sealing the tomb until the third day
Matthew27:65 “Use your own Temple police
Matthew27:66 So they sealed the stone and posted guards to protect it from intrusion.
Matthew28:1 Early on Sunday morning
Matthew28:2 Suddenly there was a great earthquake; for an angel of the Lord came down from heaven and rolled aside the stone and sat on it.
Matthew28:3 His face shone like lightning and his clothing was a brilliant white.
Matthew28:4 The guards shook with fear when they saw him
Matthew28:5 Then the angel spoke to the women. “Don't be frightened!“ he said. “I know you are looking for Jesus
Matthew28:6 but he isn't here! For he has come back to life again
Matthew28:7 And now

Matthew28:15 So the police accepted the bribe and said what they were told to. Their story spread widely among the Jews and is still believed by them to this very day.

Mark1:1 Here begins the wonderful story of Jesus the Messiah
Mark1:2 In the book written by the prophet Isaiah
Mark1:3 “This messenger will live out in the barren wilderness
Mark1:4 This messenger was John the Baptist. He lived in the wilderness and taught that all should be baptized as a public announcement of their decision to turn their backs on sin
Mark1:5 People from Jerusalem and from all over Judea traveled out into the Judean wastelands to see and hear John
Mark1:6 His clothes were woven from camel's hair and he wore a leather belt; locusts and wild honey were his food.
Mark1:7 Here is a sample of his preaching: “Someone is coming soon who is far greater than I am
Mark1:8 I baptize you with water but he will baptize you with God's Holy Spirit!“
Mark1:9 Then one day Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee
Mark1:10 The moment Jesus came up out of the water
Mark1:11 and a voice from heaven said

Mark1:14 Later on

Mark1:21 Jesus and his companions now arrived at the town of Capernaum and on Saturday morning went into the Jewish place of worship—the synagogue—where he preached.
Mark1:22 The congregation was surprised at his sermon because he spoke as an authority and didn't try to prove his points by quoting others—quite unlike what they were used to hearing!

Mark1:24 “Why are you bothering us
