Mark1:26 At that the evil spirit screamed and convulsed the man violently and left him.
Mark1:27 Amazement gripped the audience and they began discussing what had happened. “What sort of new religion is this?“ they asked excitedly. “Why

Mark4:1 Once again an immense crowd gathered around him on the beach as he was teaching
Mark4:2 His usual method of teaching was to tell the people stories. One of them went like this:
Mark4:3 “Listen! A farmer decided to sow some grain. As he scattered it across his field
Mark4:4 some of it fell on a path
Mark4:5 Some fell on thin soil with underlying rock. It grew up quickly enough
Mark4:6 but soon wilted beneath the hot sun and died because the roots had no nourishment in the shallow soil.
Mark4:7 Other seeds fell among thorns that shot up and crowded the young plants so that they produced no grain.
Mark4:8 But some of the seeds fell into good soil and yielded thirty times as much as he had planted—some of it even sixty or a hundred times as much!
Mark4:9 If you have ears
Mark4:10 Afterwards
Mark4:11 He replied
Mark4:12 'Though they see and hear
Mark4:13 But if you can't understand this simple illustration
Mark4:14 “The farmer I talked about is anyone who brings God's message to others
Mark4:15 The hard pathway
Mark4:16 The rocky soil represents the hearts of those who hear the message with joy
Mark4:17 but
Mark4:18 “The thorny ground represents the hearts of people who listen to the Good News and receive it
Mark4:19 but all too quickly the attractions of this world and the delights of wealth
Mark4:20 “But the good soil represents the hearts of those who truly accept God's message and produce a plentiful harvest for God—thirty
Mark4:21 Then he asked them
Mark4:22 “All that is now hidden will someday come to light.
Mark4:23 If you have ears
Mark4:24 And be sure to put into practice what you hear. The more you do this
Mark4:25 To him who has shall be given; from him who has not shall be taken away even what he has.
Mark4:26 “Here is another story illustrating what the Kingdom of God is like: “A farmer sowed his field
Mark4:27 and went away
Mark4:28 For the soil made the seeds grow. First a leaf-blade pushed through
Mark4:29 and then the farmer came at once with his sickle and harvested it.“
Mark4:30 Jesus asked
Mark4:31 It is like a tiny mustard seed! Though this is one of the smallest of seeds
Mark4:32 yet it grows to become one of the largest of plants
Mark4:33 He used many such illustrations to teach the people as much as they were ready to understand.
Mark4:34 In fact

Mark7:4 So when they come home from the market they must always sprinkle themselves in this way before touching any food. This is but one of many examples of laws and regulations they have clung to for centuries

Mark7:6 Jesus replied

Mark7:10 For instance

Mark9:38 One of his disciples

Mark9:41 If anyone so much as gives you a cup of water because you are Christ's—I say this solemnly—he won't lose his reward.
Mark9:42 But if someone causes one of these little ones who believe in me to lose faith—it would be better for that man if a huge millstone were tied around his neck and he were thrown into the sea.
Mark9:43 “If your hand does wrong
Mark9:44 Better live forever with one hand than be thrown into the unquenchable fires of hell with two!
Mark9:45 If your foot carries you toward evil
Mark9:46 Better be lame and live forever than have two feet that carry you to hell.
Mark9:47 “And if your eye is sinful
Mark9:48 where the worm never dies
Mark9:49 where all are salted with fire.
Mark9:50 “Good salt is worthless if it loses its saltiness; it can't season anything. So don't lose your flavor! Live in peace with each other.“
Mark10:1 Then he left Capernaum and went southward to the Judean borders and into the area east of the Jordan River. And as always there were the crowds; and as usual he taught them.
Mark10:2 Some Pharisees came and asked him
Mark10:3 “What did Moses say about divorce?“ Jesus asked them.
Mark10:4 “He said it was all right
Mark10:5 “And why did he say that?“ Jesus asked. “I'll tell you why—it was a concession to your hardhearted wickedness.
Mark10:6 But it certainly isn't God's way. For from the very first he made man and woman to be joined together permanently in marriage;
Mark10:7 therefore a man is to leave his father and mother
Mark10:8 and he and his wife are united so that they are no longer two
Mark10:9 And no man may separate what God has joined together.“

Luke1:1 Dear friend who loves God: Several biographies of Christ have already been written using as
Luke1:2 their source material the reports circulating among us from the early disciples and other eyewitnesses.
Luke1:3 However
Luke1:4 to reassure you of the truth of all you were taught.
Luke1:5 My story begins with a Jewish priest
Luke1:6 Zacharias and Elizabeth were godly folk
Luke1:7 But they had no children
Luke1:8 One day as Zacharias was going about his work in the Temple—for his division was on duty that week—
Luke1:9 the honor fell to him by lot to enter the inner sanctuary and burn incense before the Lord.
Luke1:10 Meanwhile
Luke1:11 Zacharias was in the sanctuary when suddenly an angel appeared
Luke1:12 Zacharias was startled and terrified.
Luke1:13 But the angel said
Luke1:14 You will both have great joy and gladness at his birth
Luke1:15 For he will be one of the Lord's great men. He must never touch wine or hard liquor—and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit
Luke1:16 And he will persuade many a Jew to turn to the Lord his God.
Luke1:17 He will be a man of rugged spirit and power like Elijah
Luke1:18 Zacharias said to the angel
Luke1:19 Then the angel said
Luke1:20 And now
Luke1:21 Meanwhile the crowds outside were waiting for Zacharias to appear and wondered why he was taking so long.
Luke1:22 When he finally came out
Luke1:23 He stayed on at the Temple for the remaining days of his Temple duties and then returned home.
Luke1:24 Soon afterwards Elizabeth his wife became pregnant and went into seclusion for five months.
Luke1:25 “How kind the Lord is
Luke1:26 The following month God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth
Luke1:27 to a virgin
Luke1:28 Gabriel appeared to her and said
Luke1:29 Confused and disturbed
Luke1:30 “Don't be frightened
Luke1:31 Very soon now
Luke1:32 He shall be very great and shall be called the Son of God. And the Lord God shall give him the throne of his ancestor David.
Luke1:33 And he shall reign over Israel forever; his Kingdom shall never end!“
Luke1:34 Mary asked the angel
Luke1:35 The angel replied
Luke1:36 Furthermore
Luke1:37 For every promise from God shall surely come true.“
Luke1:38 Mary said

Luke1:40 to the town where Zacharias lived

Luke1:42 She gave a glad cry and exclaimed to Mary
Luke1:43 What an honor this is
Luke1:44 When you came in and greeted me
Luke1:45 You believed that God would do what he said; that is why he has given you this wonderful blessing.“
Luke1:46 Mary responded
Luke1:47 How I rejoice in God my Savior!
Luke1:48 For he took notice of his lowly servant girl
Luke1:49 For he
Luke1:50 His mercy goes on from generation to generation
Luke1:51 “How powerful is his mighty arm! How he scatters the proud and haughty ones!
Luke1:52 He has torn princes from their thrones and exalted the lowly.
Luke1:53 He has satisfied the hungry hearts and sent the rich away with empty hands.
Luke1:54 And how he has helped his servant Israel! He has not forgotten his promise to be merciful.
Luke1:55 For he promised our fathers—Abraham and his children—to be merciful to them forever.“
Luke1:56 Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months and then went back to her own home.
Luke1:57 By now Elizabeth's waiting was over
Luke1:58 The word spread quickly to her neighbors and relatives of how kind the Lord had been to her

Luke1:60 But Elizabeth said

Luke1:64 Instantly Zacharias could speak again
Luke1:65 Wonder fell upon the whole neighborhood
Luke1:66 And everyone who heard about it thought long thoughts and asked

Luke1:69 He is sending us a Mighty Savior from the royal line of his servant David

Luke3:1 In the fifteenth year of the reign of Emperor Tiberius Caesar
Luke3:2 (Pilate was governor over Judea at that time; Herod

Luke3:4 In the words of Isaiah the prophet
Luke3:5 Level the mountains! Fill up the valleys! Straighten the curves! Smooth out the ruts!
Luke3:6 And then all mankind shall see the Savior sent from God.' ' '
Luke3:7 Here is a sample of John's preaching to the crowds that came for baptism: “You brood of snakes! You are trying to escape hell without truly turning to God! That is why you want to be baptized!
Luke3:8 First go and prove by the way you live that you really have repented. And don't think you are safe because you are descendants of Abraham. That isn't enough. God can produce children of Abraham from these desert stones!
Luke3:9 The axe of his judgment is poised over you
Luke3:10 The crowd replied
Luke3:11 “If you have two coats
Luke3:12 Even tax collectors—notorious for their corruption—came to be baptized and asked
Luke3:13 “By your honesty
Luke3:14 “And us
Luke3:15 Everyone was expecting the Messiah to come soon
Luke3:16 John answered the question by saying

Luke6:44 A tree is identified by the kind of fruit it produces. Figs never grow on thorns
Luke6:45 A good man produces good deeds from a good heart. And an evil man produces evil deeds from his hidden wickedness. Whatever is in the heart overflows into speech.

Luke8:4 One day he gave this illustration to a large crowd that was gathering to hear him—while many others were still on the way
Luke8:5 “A farmer went out to his field to sow grain. As he scattered the seed on the ground
Luke8:6 Other seed fell on shallow soil with rock beneath. This seed began to grow
Luke8:7 Other seed landed in thistle patches
Luke8:8 Still other fell on fertile soil; this seed grew and produced a crop one hundred times as large as he had planted.“ (As he was giving this illustration he said
Luke8:9 His apostles asked him what the story meant.
Luke8:10 He replied
Luke8:11 “This is its meaning: The seed is God's message to men.
Luke8:12 The hard path where some seed fell represents the hard hearts of those who hear the words of God
Luke8:13 The stony ground represents those who enjoy listening to sermons
Luke8:14 The seed among the thorns represents those who listen and believe God's words but whose faith afterwards is choked out by worry and riches and the responsibilities and pleasures of life. And so they are never able to help anyone else to believe the Good News.

Romans1:1 Dear friends in Rome: This letter is from Paul
Romans1:2 This Good News was promised long ago by God's prophets in the Old Testament.
Romans1:3 It is the Good News about his Son
Romans1:4 and by being raised from the dead he was proved to be the mighty Son of God
Romans1:5 And now
Romans1:6 And you
Romans1:7 May all God's mercies and peace be yours from God our Father and from Jesus Christ our Lord.
Romans1:8 Let me say first of all that wherever I go I hear you being talked about! For your faith in God is becoming known around the world. How I thank God through Jesus Christ for this good report
Romans1:9 God knows how often I pray for you. Day and night I bring you and your needs in prayer to the one I serve with all my might
Romans1:10 And one of the things I keep on praying for is the opportunity
Romans1:11 For I long to visit you so that I can impart to you the faith that will help your church grow strong in the Lord.
Romans1:12 Then
Romans1:13 I want you to know
Romans1:14 For I owe a great debt to you and to everyone else
Romans1:15 So
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of this Good News about Christ. It is God's powerful method of bringing all who believe it to heaven. This message was preached first to the Jews alone
Romans1:17 This Good News tells us that God makes us ready for heaven—makes us right in God's sight—when we put our faith and trust in Christ to save us. This is accomplished from start to finish by faith. As the Scripture says it
Romans1:18 But God shows his anger from heaven against all sinful
Romans1:19 For the truth about God is known to them instinctively; God has put this knowledge in their hearts.
Romans1:20 Since earliest times men have seen the earth and sky and all God made
Romans1:21 Yes
Romans1:22 Claiming themselves to be wise without God
Romans1:23 And then
Romans1:24 So God let them go ahead into every sort of sex sin
Romans1:25 Instead of believing what they knew was the truth about God
Romans1:26 That is why God let go of them and let them do all these evil things
Romans1:27 And the men
Romans1:28 So it was that when they gave God up and would not even acknowledge him
Romans1:29 Their lives became full of every kind of wickedness and sin
Romans1:30 They were backbiters
Romans1:31 They tried to misunderstand
Romans1:32 They were fully aware of God's death penalty for these crimes
Romans2:1 “Well
Romans2:2 And we know that God
Romans2:3 Do you think that God will judge and condemn others for doing them and overlook you when you do them
Romans2:4 Don't you realize how patient he is being with you? Or don't you care? Can't you see that he has been waiting all this time without punishing you
Romans2:5 But no
Romans2:6 He will give each one whatever his deeds deserve.
Romans2:7 He will give eternal life to those who patiently do the will of God
Romans2:8 But he will terribly punish those who fight against the truth of God and walk in evil ways—God's anger will be poured out upon them.
Romans2:9 There will be sorrow and suffering for Jews and Gentiles alike who keep on sinning.
Romans2:10 But there will be glory and honor and peace from God for all who obey him
Romans2:11 For God treats everyone the same.
Romans2:12 He will punish sin wherever it is found. He will punish the heathen when they sin
Romans2:13 God's laws are written within them; their own conscience accuses them
Romans2:14 And God will punish the Jews for sinning because they have his written laws but don't obey them.
Romans2:15 They know what is right but don't do it. After all
Romans2:16 The day will surely come when at God's command Jesus Christ will judge the secret lives of everyone
Romans2:17 You Jews think all is well between yourselves and God because he gave his laws to you; you brag that you are his special friends.
Romans2:18 Yes
Romans2:19 You are so sure of the way to God that you could point it out to a blind man. You think of yourselves as beacon lights
Romans2:20 You think that you can guide the simple and teach even children the affairs of God
Romans2:21 Yes
Romans2:22 You say it is wrong to commit adultery—do you do it? You say “Don't pray to idols“ and then make money your god instead.
Romans2:23 You are so proud of knowing God's laws
Romans2:24 No wonder the Scriptures say that the world speaks evil of God because of you.
Romans2:25 Being a Jew is worth something if you obey God's laws; but if you don't
Romans2:26 And if the heathen obey God's laws
Romans2:27 In fact
Romans2:28 For you are not real Jews just because you were born of Jewish parents or because you have gone through the Jewish initiation ceremony of circumcision.
Romans2:29 No
Romans3:1 Then what's the use of being a Jew? Are there any special benefits for them from God? Is there any value in the Jewish circumcision ceremony?
Romans3:2 Yes
Romans3:3 True
Romans3:4 Of course not! Though everyone else in the world is a liar

Romans3:9 Well
Romans3:10 As the Scriptures say
Romans3:11 No one has ever really followed God's paths or even truly wanted to.
Romans3:12 Every one has turned away; all have gone wrong. No one anywhere has kept on doing what is right; not one.
Romans3:13 Their talk is foul and filthy like the stench from an open grave. Their tongues are loaded with lies. Everything they say has in it the sting and poison of deadly snakes.
Romans3:14 Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.
Romans3:15 They are quick to kill
Romans3:16 Wherever they go they leave misery and trouble behind them
Romans3:17 and they have never known what it is to feel secure or enjoy God's blessing.
Romans3:18 They care nothing about God nor what he thinks of them.
Romans3:19 So the judgment of God lies very heavily upon the Jews
Romans3:20 Now do you see it? No one can ever be made right in God's sight by doing what the law commands. For the more we know of God's laws
Romans3:21 But now God has shown us a different way to heaven—not by “being good enough“ and trying to keep his laws
Romans3:22 Now God says he will accept and acquit us—declare us “not guilty“—if we trust Jesus Christ to take away our sins. And we all can be saved in this same way
Romans3:23 Yes
Romans3:24 yet now God declares us “not guilty“ of offending him if we trust in Jesus Christ
Romans3:25 For God sent Christ Jesus to take the punishment for our sins and to end all God's anger against us. He used Christ's blood and our faith as the means of saving us from his wrath. In this way he was being entirely fair
Romans3:26 And now in these days also he can receive sinners in this same way because Jesus took away their sins. But isn't this unfair for God to let criminals go free
Romans3:27 Then what can we boast about doing to earn our salvation? Nothing at all. Why? Because our acquittal is not based on our good deeds; it is based on what Christ has done and our faith in him.
Romans3:28 So it is that we are saved by faith in Christ and not by the good things we do.
Romans3:29 And does God save only the Jews in this way? No
Romans3:30 God treats us all the same; all
Romans3:31 Well then
Romans4:1 Abraham was
Romans4:2 If so
Romans4:3 For the Scriptures tell us Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 But didn't he earn his right to heaven by all the good things he did? No
Romans4:5 It is given to those who do not work for it. For God declares sinners to be good in his sight if they have faith in Christ to save them from God's wrath.
Romans4:6 King David spoke of this
Romans4:7 “Blessed and to be envied
Romans4:8 Yes
Romans4:9 Now then
Romans4:10 For the answer to that question
Romans4:11 It wasn't until later on
Romans4:12 And Abraham is also the spiritual father of those Jews who have been circumcised. They can see from his example that it is not this ceremony that saves them
Romans4:13 It is clear
Romans4:14 So if you still claim that God's blessings go to those who are “good enough
Romans4:15 But the fact of the matter is this: when we try to gain God's blessing and salvation by keeping his laws we always end up under his anger
Romans4:16 So God's blessings are given to us by faith
Romans4:17 That is what the Scriptures mean when they say that God made Abraham the father of many nations. God will accept all people in every nation who trust God as Abraham did. And this promise is from God himself
Romans4:18 So
Romans4:19 And because his faith was strong
Romans4:20 But Abraham never doubted. He believed God
Romans4:21 He was completely sure that God was well able to do anything he promised.
Romans4:22 And because of Abraham's faith God forgave his sins and declared him “not guilty.“
Romans4:23 Now this wonderful statement—that he was accepted and approved through his faith—wasn't just for Abraham's benefit.
Romans4:24 It was for us
Romans4:25 He died for our sins and rose again to make us right with God
Romans5:1 So now
Romans5:2 For because of our faith
Romans5:3 We can rejoice
Romans5:4 And patience develops strength of character in us and helps us trust God more each time we use it until finally our hope and faith are strong and steady.
Romans5:5 Then
Romans5:6 When we were utterly helpless
Romans5:7 Even if we were good
Romans5:8 But God showed his great love for us by sending Christ to die for us while we were still sinners.
Romans5:9 And since by his blood he did all this for us as sinners
Romans5:10 And since
Romans5:11 Now we rejoice in our wonderful new relationship with God—all because of what our Lord Jesus Christ has done in dying for our sins—making us friends of God.
Romans5:12 When Adam sinned
Romans5:13 We know that it was Adam's sin that caused this because although
Romans5:14 So when their bodies died it was not for their own sins since they themselves had never disobeyed God's special law against eating the forbidden fruit
Romans5:15 And what a difference between man's sin and God's forgiveness! For this one man
Romans5:16 Adam's one sin brought the penalty of death to many
Romans5:17 The sin of this one man
Romans5:18 Yes
Romans5:19 Adam caused many to be sinners because he disobeyed God
Romans5:20 The Ten Commandments were given so that all could see the extent of their failure to obey God's laws. But the more we see our sinfulness
Romans5:21 Before
Romans6:1 Well then
Romans6:2 Of course not! Should we keep on sinning when we don't have to?
Romans6:3 For sin's power over us was broken when we became Christians and were baptized to become a part of Jesus Christ; through his death the power of your sinful nature was shattered.
Romans6:4 Your old sin-loving nature was buried with him by baptism when he died; and when God the Father
Romans6:5 For you have become a part of him
Romans6:6 Your old evil desires were nailed to the cross with him; that part of you that loves to sin was crushed and fatally wounded
Romans6:7 for when you are deadened to sin you are freed from all its allure and its power over you.
Romans6:8 And since your old sin-loving nature “died“ with Christ
Romans6:9 Christ rose from the dead and will never die again. Death no longer has any power over him.
Romans6:10 He died once for all to end sin's power
Romans6:11 So look upon your old sin nature as dead and unresponsive to sin
Romans6:12 Do not let sin control your puny body any longer; do not give in to its sinful desires.
Romans6:13 Do not let any part of your bodies become tools of wickedness
Romans6:14 Sin need never again be your master
Romans6:15 Does this mean that now we can go ahead and sin and not worry about it? (For our salvation does not depend on keeping the law but on receiving God's grace!) Of course not!
Romans6:16 Don't you realize that you can choose your own master? You can choose sin (with death) or else obedience (with acquittal). The one to whom you offer yourself—he will take you and be your master
Romans6:17 Thank God that though you once chose to be slaves of sin
Romans6:18 And now you are free from your old master
Romans6:19 I speak this way
Romans6:20 In those days when you were slaves of sin you didn't bother much with goodness.
Romans6:21 And what was the result? Evidently not good
Romans6:22 But now you are free from the power of sin and are slaves of God
Romans6:23 For the wages of sin is death
Romans7:1 Don't you understand yet
Romans7:2 Let me illustrate: when a woman marries
Romans7:3 Then she can marry someone else if she wants to. That would be wrong while he was alive
Romans7:4 Your “husband
Romans7:5 When your old nature was still active
Romans7:6 But now you need no longer worry about the Jewish laws and customs because you “died“ while in their captivity
Romans7:7 Well then
Romans7:8 But sin used this law against evil desires by reminding me that such desires are wrong
Romans7:9 That is why I felt fine so long as I did not understand what the law really demanded. But when I learned the truth
Romans7:10 So as far as I was concerned
Romans7:11 Sin fooled me by taking the good laws of God and using them to make me guilty of death.
Romans7:12 But still
Romans7:13 But how can that be? Didn't the law cause my doom? How then can it be good? No
Romans7:14 The law is good
Romans7:15 I don't understand myself at all
Romans7:16 I know perfectly well that what I am doing is wrong
Romans7:17 But I can't help myself because I'm no longer doing it. It is sin inside me that is stronger than I am that makes me do these evil things.
Romans7:18 I know I am rotten through and through so far as my old sinful nature is concerned. No matter which way I turn I can't make myself do right. I want to but I can't.
Romans7:19 When I want to do good
Romans7:20 Now if I am doing what I don't want to
Romans7:21 It seems to be a fact of life that when I want to do what is right
Romans7:22 I love to do God's will so far as my new nature is concerned;
Romans7:23 but there is something else deep within me
Romans7:24 So you see how it is: my new life tells me to do right
Romans7:25 Thank God! It has been done by Jesus Christ our Lord. He has set me free.
Romans8:1 So there is now no condemnation awaiting those who belong to Christ Jesus.
Romans8:2 For the power of the life-giving Spirit—and this power is mine through Christ Jesus—has freed me from the vicious circle of sin and death.
Romans8:3 We aren't saved from sin's grasp by knowing the commandments of God because we can't and don't keep them
Romans8:4 So now we can obey God's laws if we follow after the Holy Spirit and no longer obey the old evil nature within us.
Romans8:5 Those who let themselves be controlled by their lower natures live only to please themselves
Romans8:6 Following after the Holy Spirit leads to life and peace
Romans8:7 because the old sinful nature within us is against God. It never did obey God's laws and it never will.
Romans8:8 That's why those who are still under the control of their old sinful selves
Romans8:9 But you are not like that. You are controlled by your new nature if you have the Spirit of God living in you. (And remember that if anyone doesn't have the Spirit of Christ living in him
Romans8:10 Yet
Romans8:11 And if the Spirit of God
Romans8:12 So
Romans8:13 For if you keep on following it you are lost and will perish
Romans8:14 For all who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.
Romans8:15 And so we should not be like cringing
Romans8:16 For his Holy Spirit speaks to us deep in our hearts and tells us that we really are God's children.
Romans8:17 And since we are his children
Romans8:18 Yet what we suffer now is nothing compared to the glory he will give us later.
Romans8:19 For all creation is waiting patiently and hopefully for that future day when God will resurrect his children.
Romans8:20 For on that day thorns and thistles
Romans8:21 and the world around us will share in the glorious freedom from sin which God's children enjoy.
Romans8:22 For we know that even the things of nature
Romans8:23 And even we Christians
Romans8:24 We are saved by trusting. And trusting means looking forward to getting something we don't yet have—for a man who already has something doesn't need to hope and trust that he will get it.
Romans8:25 But if we must keep trusting God for something that hasn't happened yet
Romans8:26 And in the same way—by our faith—the Holy Spirit helps us with our daily problems and in our praying. For we don't even know what we should pray for nor how to pray as we should
Romans8:27 And the Father who knows all hearts knows
Romans8:28 And we know that all that happens to us is working for our good if we love God and are fitting into his plans.
Romans8:29 For from the very beginning God decided that those who came to him—and all along he knew who would—should become like his Son
Romans8:30 And having chosen us
Romans8:31 What can we ever say to such wonderful things as these? If God is on our side
Romans8:32 Since he did not spare even his own Son for us but gave him up for us all
Romans8:33 Who dares accuse us whom God has chosen for his own? Will God? No! He is the one who has forgiven us and given us right standing with himself.
Romans8:34 Who then will condemn us? Will Christ? No! For he is the one who died for us and came back to life again for us and is sitting at the place of highest honor next to God
Romans8:35 Who then can ever keep Christ's love from us? When we have trouble or calamity
Romans8:36 No
Romans8:37 but despite all this
Romans8:38 For I am convinced that nothing can ever separate us from his love. Death can't
Romans8:39 or where we are—high above the sky

Romans9:3 Christ knows and the Holy Spirit knows that it is no mere pretense when I say that I would be willing to be forever damned if that would save you.

Romans9:5 Great men of God were your fathers
Romans9:6 Well then
Romans9:7 Just the fact that they come from Abraham doesn't make them truly Abraham's children. For the Scriptures say that the promises apply only to Abraham's son Isaac and Isaac's descendants
Romans9:8 This means that not all of Abraham's children are children of God

Romans9:10 And years later

Romans9:18 So you see
Romans9:19 Well then
Romans9:20 No
Romans9:21 When a man makes a jar out of clay
Romans9:22 Does not God have a perfect right to show his fury and power against those who are fit only for destruction
Romans9:23 And he has a right to take others such as ourselves
Romans9:24 whether we are Jews or Gentiles
Romans9:25 Remember what the prophecy of Hosea says? There God says that he will find other children for himself (who are not from his Jewish family) and will love them
Romans9:26 And the heathen
Romans9:27 Isaiah the prophet cried out concerning the Jews that though there would be millions of them
Romans9:28 “For the Lord will execute his sentence upon the earth
Romans9:29 And Isaiah says in another place that except for God's mercy all the Jews would be destroyed—all of them—just as everyone in the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah perished.
Romans9:30 Well then
Romans9:31 But the Jews
Romans9:32 Why not? Because they were trying to be saved by keeping the law and being good instead of by depending on faith. They have stumbled over the great stumbling stone.
Romans9:33 God warned them of this in the Scriptures when he said
Romans10:1 Dear brothers
Romans10:2 I know what enthusiasm they have for the honor of God
Romans10:3 For they don't understand that Christ has died to make them right with God. Instead they are trying to make themselves good enough to gain God's favor by keeping the Jewish laws and customs
Romans10:4 They don't understand that Christ gives to those who trust in him everything they are trying to get by keeping his laws. He ends all of that.
Romans10:5 For Moses wrote that if a person could be perfectly good and hold out against temptation all his life and never sin once
Romans10:6 But the salvation that comes through faith says
Romans10:7 “You don't need to go among the dead to bring Christ back to life again.“
Romans10:8 For salvation that comes from trusting Christ—which is what we preach—is already within easy reach of each of us; in fact
Romans10:9 For if you tell others with your own mouth that Jesus Christ is your Lord and believe in your own heart that God has raised him from the dead
Romans10:10 For it is by believing in his heart that a man becomes right with God; and with his mouth he tells others of his faith
Romans10:11 For the Scriptures tell us that no one who believes in Christ will ever be disappointed.
Romans10:12 Jew and Gentile are the same in this respect: they all have the same Lord who generously gives his riches to all those who ask him for them.
Romans10:13 Anyone who calls upon the name of the Lord will be saved.
Romans10:14 But how shall they ask him to save them unless they believe in him? And how can they believe in him if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear about him unless someone tells them?
Romans10:15 And how will anyone go and tell them unless someone sends him? That is what the Scriptures are talking about when they say
Romans10:16 But not everyone who hears the Good News has welcomed it
Romans10:17 Yet faith comes from listening to this Good News—the Good News about Christ.
Romans10:18 But what about the Jews? Have they heard God's Word? Yes
Romans10:19 And did they understand that God would give his salvation to others if they refused to take it? Yes
Romans10:20 And later on Isaiah said boldly that God would be found by people who weren't even looking for him.
Romans10:21 In the meantime
Romans11:1 I ask then
Romans11:2 No
Romans11:3 telling God how they had killed the prophets and torn down God's altars; Elijah claimed that he was the only one left in all the land who still loved God
Romans11:4 And do you remember how God replied? God said
Romans11:5 It is the same today. Not all the Jews have turned away from God; there are a few being saved as a result of God's kindness in choosing them.
Romans11:6 And if it is by God's kindness
Romans11:7 So this is the situation: Most of the Jews have not found the favor of God they are looking for. A few have—the ones God has picked out—but the eyes of the others have been blinded.
Romans11:8 This is what our Scriptures refer to when they say that God has put them to sleep
Romans11:9 King David spoke of this same thing when he said
Romans11:10 Let their eyes be dim
Romans11:11 Does this mean that God has rejected his Jewish people forever? Of course not! His purpose was to make his salvation available to the Gentiles
Romans11:12 Now if the whole world became rich as a result of God's offer of salvation
Romans11:13 As you know
Romans11:14 so that if possible I can make them want what you Gentiles have and in that way save some of them.
Romans11:15 And how wonderful it will be when they become Christians! When God turned away from them it meant that he turned to the rest of the world to offer his salvation; and now it is even more wonderful when the Jews come to Christ. It will be like dead people coming back to life.
Romans11:16 And since Abraham and the prophets are God's people
Romans11:17 But some of these branches from Abraham's tree
Romans11:18 But you must be careful not to brag about being put in to replace the branches that were broken off. Remember that you are important only because you are now a part of God's tree; you are just a branch
Romans11:19 “Well
Romans11:20 Watch out! Remember that those branches
Romans11:21 For if God did not spare the branches he put there in the first place
Romans11:22 Notice how God is both kind and severe. He is very hard on those who disobey
Romans11:23 On the other hand
Romans11:24 For if God was willing to take you who were so far away from him—being part of a wild olive tree—and graft you into his own good tree—a very unusual thing to do—don't you see that he will be far more ready to put the Jews back again
Romans11:25 I want you to know about this truth from God
Romans11:26 And then all Israel will be saved. Do you remember what the prophets said about this? “There shall come out of Zion a Deliverer
Romans11:27 At that time I will take away their sins
Romans11:28 Now many of the Jews are enemies of the Gospel. They hate it. But this has been a benefit to you
Romans11:29 For God's gifts and his call can never be withdrawn; he will never go back on his promises.
Romans11:30 Once you were rebels against God
Romans11:31 And now the Jews are the rebels
Romans11:32 For God has given them all up to sin so that he could have mercy upon all alike.
Romans11:33 Oh
Romans11:34 For who among us can know the mind of the Lord? Who knows enough to be his counselor and guide?
Romans11:35 And who could ever offer to the Lord enough to induce him to act?
Romans11:36 For everything comes from God alone. Everything lives by his power
Romans12:1 And so
Romans12:2 Don't copy the behavior and customs of this world
Romans12:3 As God's messenger I give each of you God's warning: Be honest in your estimate of yourselves
Romans12:4 Just as there are many parts to our bodies
Romans12:5 We are all parts of it
Romans12:6 God has given each of us the ability to do certain things well. So if God has given you the ability to prophesy
Romans12:7 If your gift is that of serving others
Romans12:8 If you are a preacher
Romans12:9 Don't just pretend that you love others: really love them. Hate what is wrong. Stand on the side of the good.
Romans12:10 Love each other with brotherly affection and take delight in honoring each other.
Romans12:11 Never be lazy in your work
Romans12:12 Be glad for all God is planning for you. Be patient in trouble
Romans12:13 When God's children are in need
Romans12:14 If someone mistreats you because you are a Christian
Romans12:15 When others are happy
Romans12:16 Work happily together. Don't try to act big. Don't try to get into the good graces of important people
Romans12:17 Never pay back evil for evil. Do things in such a way that everyone can see you are honest clear through.
Romans12:18 Don't quarrel with anyone. Be at peace with everyone
Romans12:19 Dear friends
Romans12:20 Instead
Romans12:21 Don't let evil get the upper hand
Romans13:1 Obey the government
Romans13:2 So those who refuse to obey the laws of the land are refusing to obey God
Romans13:3 For the policeman does not frighten people who are doing right; but those doing evil will always fear him. So if you don't want to be afraid
Romans13:4 The policeman is sent by God to help you. But if you are doing something wrong
Romans13:5 Obey the laws
Romans13:6 Pay your taxes too
Romans13:7 Pay everyone whatever he ought to have: pay your taxes and import duties gladly
Romans13:8 Pay all your debts except the debt of love for others—never finish paying that! For if you love them
Romans13:9 If you love your neighbor as much as you love yourself you will not want to harm or cheat him
Romans13:10 Love does no wrong to anyone. That's why it fully satisfies all of God's requirements. It is the only law you need.
Romans13:11 Another reason for right living is this: you know how late it is; time is running out. Wake up
Romans13:12 The night is far gone
Romans13:13 Be decent and true in everything you do so that all can approve your behavior. Don't spend your time in wild parties and getting drunk or in adultery and lust or fighting or jealousy.
Romans13:14 But ask the Lord Jesus Christ to help you live as you should
Romans14:1 Give a warm welcome to any brother who wants to join you
Romans14:2 For instance
Romans14:3 Those who think it is all right to eat such meat must not look down on those who won't. And if you are one of those who won't
Romans14:4 They are God's servants
Romans14:5 Some think that Christians should observe the Jewish holidays as special days to worship God
Romans14:6 If you have special days for worshiping the Lord
Romans14:7 We are not our own bosses to live or die as we ourselves might choose.
Romans14:8 Living or dying we follow the Lord. Either way we are his.
Romans14:9 Christ died and rose again for this very purpose
Romans14:10 You have no right to criticize your brother or look down on him. Remember
Romans14:11 For it is written
Romans14:12 Yes
Romans14:13 So don't criticize each other anymore. Try instead to live in such a way that you will never make your brother stumble by letting him see you doing something he thinks is wrong.
Romans14:14 As for myself
Romans14:15 And if your brother is bothered by what you eat
Romans14:16 Don't do anything that will cause criticism against yourself even though you know that what you do is right.
Romans14:17 For
Romans14:18 If you let Christ be Lord in these affairs
Romans14:19 In this way aim for harmony in the church
Romans14:20 Don't undo the work of God for a chunk of meat. Remember
Romans14:21 The right thing to do is to quit eating meat or drinking wine or doing anything else that offends your brother or makes him sin.
Romans14:22 You may know that there is nothing wrong with what you do
Romans14:23 But anyone who believes that something he wants to do is wrong shouldn't do it. He sins if he does

Romans15:2 for we must bear the “burden“ of being considerate of the doubts and fears of others—of those who feel these things are wrong. Let's please the other fellow
Romans15:3 Christ didn't please himself. As the Psalmist said
Romans15:4 These things that were written in the Scriptures so long ago are to teach us patience and to encourage us so that we will look forward expectantly to the time when God will conquer sin and death.
Romans15:5 May God who gives patience
Romans15:6 And then all of us can praise the Lord together with one voice
Romans15:7 So warmly welcome each other into the church
Romans15:8 Remember that Jesus Christ came to show that God is true to his promises and to help the Jews.
Romans15:9 And remember that he came also that the Gentiles might be saved and give glory to God for his mercies to them. That is what the psalmist meant when he wrote: “I will praise you among the Gentiles and sing to your name.“
Romans15:10 And in another place
Romans15:11 And yet again
Romans15:12 And the prophet Isaiah said
Romans15:13 So I pray for you Gentiles that God who gives you hope will keep you happy and full of peace as you believe in him. I pray that God will help you overflow with hope in him through the Holy Spirit's power within you.
Romans15:14 I know that you are wise and good
Romans15:15 But even so I have been bold enough to emphasize some of these points
Romans15:16 a special messenger from Jesus Christ to you Gentiles
Romans15:17 So it is right for me to be a little proud of all Christ Jesus has done through me.
Romans15:18 I dare not judge how effectively he has used others
Romans15:19 I have won them by my message and by the good way I have lived before them and by the miracles done through me as signs from God—all by the Holy Spirit's power. In this way I have preached the full Gospel of Christ all the way from Jerusalem clear over into Illyricum.
Romans15:20 But all the while my ambition has been to go still farther
Romans15:21 I have been following the plan spoken of in the Scriptures where Isaiah says that those who have never heard the name of Christ before will see and understand.
Romans15:22 In fact
Romans15:23 But now at last I am through with my work here
Romans15:24 For I am planning to take a trip to Spain
Romans15:25 But before I come
Romans15:26 For you see
Romans15:27 They were very glad to do this
Romans15:28 As soon as I have delivered this money and completed this good deed of theirs
Romans15:29 And I am sure that when I come the Lord will give me a great blessing for you.
Romans15:30 Will you be my prayer partners? For the Lord Jesus Christ's sake and because of your love for me—given to you by the Holy Spirit—pray much with me for my work.
Romans15:31 Pray that I will be protected in Jerusalem from those who are not Christians. Pray also that the Christians there will be willing to accept the money I am bringing them.
Romans15:32 Then I will be able to come to you with a happy heart by the will of God
Romans15:33 And now may our God
Romans16:1 Phoebe
Romans16:2 Receive her as your sister in the Lord
Romans16:3 Tell Priscilla and Aquila hello. They have been my fellow workers in the affairs of Christ Jesus.
Romans16:4 In fact
Romans16:5 Please give my greetings to all those who meet to worship in their home. Greet my good friend Epaenetus. He was the very first person to become a Christian in Asia.
Romans16:6 Remember me to Mary
Romans16:7 Then there are Andronicus and Junias
Romans16:8 Say hello to Ampliatus
Romans16:9 and Urbanus
Romans16:10 Then there is Apelles
Romans16:11 Remember me to Herodion my relative. Remember me to the Christian slaves over at Narcissus House.
Romans16:12 Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa
Romans16:13 Greet Rufus for me
Romans16:14 And please give my greetings to Asyncritus
Romans16:15 Give my love to Philologus
Romans16:16 Shake hands warmly with each other. All the churches here send you their greetings.
Romans16:17 And now there is one more thing to say before I end this letter. Stay away from those who cause divisions and are upsetting people's faith
Romans16:18 Such teachers are not working for our Lord Jesus but only want gain for themselves. They are good speakers
Romans16:19 But everyone knows that you stand loyal and true. This makes me very happy. I want you always to remain very clear about what is right and to stay innocent of any wrong.
Romans16:20 The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The blessings from our Lord Jesus Christ be upon you.
Romans16:21 Timothy my fellow worker
Romans16:22 I
Romans16:23 Gaius says to say hello to you for him. I am his guest
Romans16:24 Good-bye. May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
Romans16:25 I commit you to God
Romans16:26 But now as the prophets foretold and as God commands
Romans16:27 To God
1Corinthians1:1 From: Paul
1Corinthians1:2 To: The Christians in Corinth
1Corinthians1:3 May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you all of his blessings
1Corinthians1:4 I can never stop thanking God for all the wonderful gifts he has given you
1Corinthians1:5 he has enriched your whole life. He has helped you speak out for him and has given you a full understanding of the truth;
1Corinthians1:6 what I told you Christ could do for you has happened!
1Corinthians1:7 Now you have every grace and blessing; every spiritual gift and power for doing his will are yours during this time of waiting for the return of our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Corinthians1:8 And he guarantees right up to the end that you will be counted free from all sin and guilt on that day when he returns.
1Corinthians1:9 God will surely do this for you
1Corinthians1:10 But
1Corinthians1:11 For some of those who live at Chloe's house have told me of your arguments and quarrels
1Corinthians1:12 Some of you are saying
1Corinthians1:13 And so
1Corinthians1:14 I am so thankful now that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
1Corinthians1:15 For now no one can think that I have been trying to start something new
1Corinthians1:16 Oh
1Corinthians1:17 For Christ didn't send me to baptize
1Corinthians1:18 I know very well how foolish it sounds to those who are lost
1Corinthians1:19 For God says
1Corinthians1:20 So what about these wise men
1Corinthians1:21 For God in his wisdom saw to it that the world would never find God through human brilliance
1Corinthians1:22 It seems foolish to the Jews because they want a sign from heaven as proof that what is preached is true; and it is foolish to the Gentiles because they believe only what agrees with their philosophy and seems wise to them.
1Corinthians1:23 So when we preach about Christ dying to save them
1Corinthians1:24 But God has opened the eyes of those called to salvation
1Corinthians1:25 This so-called “foolish“ plan of God is far wiser than the wisest plan of the wisest man
1Corinthians1:26 Notice among yourselves
1Corinthians1:27 Instead
1Corinthians1:28 He has chosen a plan despised by the world
1Corinthians1:29 so that no one anywhere can ever brag in the presence of God.
1Corinthians1:30 For it is from God alone that you have your life through Christ Jesus. He showed us God's plan of salvation; he was the one who made us acceptable to God; he made us pure and holy and gave himself to purchase our salvation.
1Corinthians1:31 As it says in the Scriptures
1Corinthians2:1 Dear brothers
1Corinthians2:2 For I decided that I would speak only of Jesus Christ and his death on the cross.
1Corinthians2:3 I came to you in weakness—timid and trembling.
1Corinthians2:4 And my preaching was very plain
1Corinthians2:5 I did this because I wanted your faith to stand firmly upon God
1Corinthians2:6 Yet when I am among mature Christians I do speak with words of great wisdom
1Corinthians2:7 Our words are wise because they are from God
1Corinthians2:8 But the great men of the world have not understood it; if they had
1Corinthians2:9 That is what is meant by the Scriptures which say that no mere man has ever seen
1Corinthians2:10 But we know about these things because God has sent his Spirit to tell us
1Corinthians2:11 No one can really know what anyone else is thinking or what he is really like except that person himself. And no one can know God's thoughts except God's own Spirit.
1Corinthians2:12 And God has actually given us his Spirit (not the world's spirit) to tell us about the wonderful free gifts of grace and blessing that God has given us.
1Corinthians2:13 In telling you about these gifts we have even used the very words given to us by the Holy Spirit
1Corinthians2:14 But the man who isn't a Christian can't understand and can't accept these thoughts from God
1Corinthians2:15 But the spiritual man has insight into everything
1Corinthians2:16 How could he? For certainly he has never been one to know the Lord's thoughts
1Corinthians3:1 Dear brothers
1Corinthians3:2 I have had to feed you with milk and not with solid food because you couldn't digest anything stronger. And even now you still have to be fed on milk.
1Corinthians3:3 For you are still only baby Christians
1Corinthians3:4 There you are
1Corinthians3:5 Who am I
1Corinthians3:6 My work was to plant the seed in your hearts
1Corinthians3:7 The person who does the planting or watering isn't very important
1Corinthians3:8 Apollos and I are working as a team
1Corinthians3:9 We are only God's coworkers. You are God's garden
1Corinthians3:10 God
1Corinthians3:11 And no one can ever lay any other real foundation than that one we already have—Jesus Christ.
1Corinthians3:12 But there are various kinds of materials that can be used to build on that foundation. Some use gold and silver and jewels; and some build with sticks and hay or even straw!
1Corinthians3:13 There is going to come a time of testing at Christ's Judgment Day to see what kind of material each builder has used. Everyone's work will be put through the fire so that all can see whether or not it keeps its value
1Corinthians3:14 Then every workman who has built on the foundation with the right materials
1Corinthians3:15 But if the house he has built burns up
1Corinthians3:16 Don't you realize that all of you together are the house of God
1Corinthians3:17 If anyone defiles and spoils God's home
1Corinthians3:18 Stop fooling yourselves. If you count yourself above average in intelligence
1Corinthians3:19 For the wisdom of this world is foolishness to God. As it says in the book of Job
1Corinthians3:20 And again
1Corinthians3:21 So don't be proud of following the wise men of this world. For God has already given you everything you need.
1Corinthians3:22 He has given you Paul and Apollos and Peter as your helpers. He has given you the whole world to use
1Corinthians3:23 and you belong to Christ
1Corinthians4:1 So Apollos and I should be looked upon as Christ's servants who distribute God's blessings by explaining God's secrets.
1Corinthians4:2 Now the most important thing about a servant is that he does just what his master tells him to.
1Corinthians4:3 What about me? Have I been a good servant? Well
1Corinthians4:4 My conscience is clear
1Corinthians4:5 So be careful not to jump to conclusions before the Lord returns as to whether someone is a good servant or not. When the Lord comes
1Corinthians4:6 I have used Apollos and myself as examples to illustrate what I have been saying: that you must not have favorites. You must not be proud of one of God's teachers more than another.
1Corinthians4:7 What are you so puffed up about? What do you have that God hasn't given you? And if all you have is from God
1Corinthians4:8 You seem to think you already have all the spiritual food you need. You are full and spiritually contented
1Corinthians4:9 Sometimes I think God has put us apostles at the very end of the line
1Corinthians4:10 Religion has made us foolish
1Corinthians4:11 To this very hour we have gone hungry and thirsty
1Corinthians4:12 We have worked wearily with our hands to earn our living. We have blessed those who cursed us. We have been patient with those who injured us.
1Corinthians4:13 We have replied quietly when evil things have been said about us. Yet right up to the present moment we are like dirt underfoot
1Corinthians4:14 I am not writing about these things to make you ashamed
1Corinthians4:15 For although you may have ten thousand others to teach you about Christ
1Corinthians4:16 So I beg you to follow my example and do as I do.
1Corinthians4:17 That is the very reason why I am sending Timothy—to help you do this. For he is one of those I won to Christ
1Corinthians4:18 I know that some of you will have become proud
1Corinthians4:19 But I will come
1Corinthians4:20 The Kingdom of God is not just talking; it is living by God's power.
1Corinthians4:21 Which do you choose? Shall I come with punishment and scolding
1Corinthians5:1 Everyone is talking about the terrible thing that has happened there among you
1Corinthians5:2 And are you still so conceited
1Corinthians5:3 Although I am not there with you
1Corinthians5:4 You are to call a meeting of the church—and the power of the Lord Jesus will be with you as you meet
1Corinthians5:5 and cast out this man from the fellowship of the church and into Satan's hands
1Corinthians5:6 What a terrible thing it is that you are boasting about your purity and yet you let this sort of thing go on. Don't you realize that if even one person is allowed to go on sinning
1Corinthians5:7 Remove this evil cancer—this wicked person—from among you
1Corinthians5:8 So let us feast upon him and grow strong in the Christian life
1Corinthians5:9 When I wrote to you before I said not to mix with evil people.
1Corinthians5:10 But when I said that I wasn't talking about unbelievers who live in sexual sin or are greedy cheats and thieves and idol worshipers. For you can't live in this world without being with people like that.
1Corinthians5:11 What I meant was that you are not to keep company with anyone who claims to be a brother Christian but indulges in sexual sins
1Corinthians5:12 It isn't our job to judge outsiders. But it certainly is our job to judge and deal strongly with those who are members of the church and who are sinning in these ways.
1Corinthians5:13 God alone is the Judge of those on the outside. But you yourselves must deal with this man and put him out of your church.
1Corinthians6:1 How is it that when you have something against another Christian
1Corinthians6:2 Don't you know that someday we Christians are going to judge and govern the world? So why can't you decide even these little things among yourselves?
1Corinthians6:3 Don't you realize that we Christians will judge and reward the very angels in heaven? So you should be able to decide your problems down here on earth easily enough.
1Corinthians6:4 Why then go to outside judges who are not even Christians?
1Corinthians6:5 I am trying to make you ashamed. Isn't there anyone in all the church who is wise enough to decide these arguments?
1Corinthians6:6 But
1Corinthians6:7 To have such lawsuits at all is a real defeat for you as Christians. Why not just accept mistreatment and leave it at that? It would be far more honoring to the Lord to let yourselves be cheated.
1Corinthians6:8 But
1Corinthians6:9 Don't you know that those doing such things have no share in the Kingdom of God? Don't fool yourselves. Those who live immoral lives
1Corinthians6:10 Neither will thieves or greedy people
1Corinthians6:11 There was a time when some of you were just like that but now your sins are washed away
1Corinthians6:12 I can do anything I want to if Christ has not said no
1Corinthians6:13 For instance
1Corinthians6:14 And God is going to raise our bodies from the dead by his power just as he raised up the Lord Jesus Christ.
1Corinthians6:15 Don't you realize that your bodies are actually parts and members of Christ? So should I take part of Christ and join him to a prostitute? Never!
1Corinthians6:16 And don't you know that if a man joins himself to a prostitute she becomes a part of him and he becomes a part of her? For God tells us in the Scripture that in his sight the two become one person.
1Corinthians6:17 But if you give yourself to the Lord
1Corinthians6:18 That is why I say to run from sex sin. No other sin affects the body as this one does. When you sin this sin it is against your own body.
1Corinthians6:19 Haven't you yet learned that your body is the home of the Holy Spirit God gave you
1Corinthians6:20 For God has bought you with a great price. So use every part of your body to give glory back to God because he owns it.
1Corinthians7:1 Now about those questions you asked in your last letter: my answer is that if you do not marry
1Corinthians7:2 But usually it is best to be married
1Corinthians7:3 The man should give his wife all that is her right as a married woman
1Corinthians7:4 for a girl who marries no longer has full right to her own body
1Corinthians7:5 So do not refuse these rights to each other. The only exception to this rule would be the agreement of both husband and wife to refrain from the rights of marriage for a limited time
1Corinthians7:6 I'm not saying you must marry
1Corinthians7:7 I wish everyone could get along without marrying
1Corinthians7:8 So I say to those who aren't married and to widows—better to stay unmarried if you can
1Corinthians7:9 But if you can't control yourselves
1Corinthians7:10 Now
1Corinthians7:11 But if she is separated from him
1Corinthians7:12 Here I want to add some suggestions of my own. These are not direct commands from the Lord
1Corinthians7:13 And if a Christian woman has a husband who isn't a Christian
1Corinthians7:14 For perhaps the husband who isn't a Christian may become a Christian with the help of his Christian wife. And the wife who isn't a Christian may become a Christian with the help of her Christian husband. Otherwise
1Corinthians7:15 But if the husband or wife who isn't a Christian is eager to leave
1Corinthians7:16 For
1Corinthians7:17 But be sure in deciding these matters that you are living as God intended
1Corinthians7:18 For instance
1Corinthians7:19 For it doesn't make any difference at all whether a Christian has gone through this ceremony or not. But it makes a lot of difference whether he is pleasing God and keeping God's commandments. That is the important thing.
1Corinthians7:20 Usually a person should keep on with the work he was doing when God called him.
1Corinthians7:21 Are you a slave? Don't let that worry you—but of course
1Corinthians7:22 If the Lord calls you
1Corinthians7:23 You have been bought and paid for by Christ
1Corinthians7:24 So
1Corinthians7:25 Now I will try to answer your other question. What about girls who are not yet married? Should they be permitted to do so? In answer to this question
1Corinthians7:26 Here is the problem: We Christians are facing great dangers to our lives at present. In times like these I think it is best for a person to remain unmarried.
1Corinthians7:27 Of course
1Corinthians7:28 But if you men decide to go ahead anyway and get married now
1Corinthians7:29 The important thing to remember is that our remaining time is very short
1Corinthians7:30 happiness or sadness or wealth should not keep anyone from doing God's work.
1Corinthians7:31 Those in frequent contact with the exciting things the world offers should make good use of their opportunities without stopping to enjoy them; for the world in its present form will soon be gone.
1Corinthians7:32 In all you do
1Corinthians7:33 But a married man can't do that so well; he has to think about his earthly responsibilities and how to please his wife.
1Corinthians7:34 His interests are divided. It is the same with a girl who marries. She faces the same problem. A girl who is not married is anxious to please the Lord in all she is and does. But a married woman must consider other things such as housekeeping and the likes and dislikes of her husband.
1Corinthians7:35 I am saying this to help you
1Corinthians7:36 But if anyone feels he ought to marry because he has trouble controlling his passions
1Corinthians7:37 But if a man has the willpower not to marry and decides that he doesn't need to and won't
1Corinthians7:38 So the person who marries does well
1Corinthians7:39 The wife is part of her husband as long as he lives; if her husband dies
1Corinthians7:40 But in my opinion she will be happier if she doesn't marry again; and I think I am giving you counsel from God's Spirit when I say this.
1Corinthians8:1 Next is your question about eating food that has been sacrificed to idols. On this question everyone feels that only his answer is the right one! But although being a “know-it-all“ makes us feel important
1Corinthians8:2 If anyone thinks he knows all the answers
1Corinthians8:3 But the person who truly loves God is the one who is open to God's knowledge.
1Corinthians8:4 So now
1Corinthians8:5 According to some people
1Corinthians8:6 But we know that there is only one God
1Corinthians8:7 However
1Corinthians8:8 Just remember that God doesn't care whether we eat it or not. We are no worse off if we don't eat it
1Corinthians8:9 But be careful not to use your freedom to eat it
1Corinthians8:10 You see
1Corinthians8:11 So because you “know it is all right to do it
1Corinthians8:12 And it is a sin against Christ to sin against your brother by encouraging him to do something he thinks is wrong.
1Corinthians8:13 So if eating meat offered to idols is going to make my brother sin
1Corinthians9:1 I am an apostle
1Corinthians9:2 If in the opinion of others
1Corinthians9:3 This is my answer to those who question my rights.
1Corinthians9:4 Or don't I have any rights at all? Can't I claim the same privilege the other apostles have of being a guest in your homes?
1Corinthians9:5 If I had a wife
1Corinthians9:6 And must Barnabas and I alone keep working for our living while you supply these others?
1Corinthians9:7 What soldier in the army has to pay his own expenses? And have you ever heard of a farmer who harvests his crop and doesn't have the right to eat some of it? What shepherd takes care of a flock of sheep and goats and isn't allowed to drink some of the milk?
1Corinthians9:8 And I'm not merely quoting the opinions of men as to what is right. I'm telling you what God's law says.
1Corinthians9:9 For in the law God gave to Moses he said that you must not put a muzzle on an ox to keep it from eating when it is treading out the wheat. Do you suppose God was thinking only about oxen when he said this?
1Corinthians9:10 Wasn't he also thinking about us? Of course he was. He said this to show us that Christian workers should be paid by those they help. Those who do the plowing and threshing should expect some share of the harvest.
1Corinthians9:11 We have planted good spiritual seed in your souls. Is it too much to ask
1Corinthians9:12 You give them to others who preach to you
1Corinthians9:13 Don't you realize that God told those working in his temple to take for their own needs some of the food brought there as gifts to him? And those who work at the altar of God get a share of the food that is brought by those offering it to the Lord.
1Corinthians9:14 In the same way the Lord has given orders that those who preach the Gospel should be supported by those who accept it.
1Corinthians9:15 Yet I have never asked you for one penny. And I am not writing this to hint that I would like to start now. In fact
1Corinthians9:16 For just preaching the Gospel isn't any special credit to me—I couldn't keep from preaching it if I wanted to. I would be utterly miserable. Woe unto me if I don't.
1Corinthians9:17 If I were volunteering my services of my own free will
1Corinthians9:18 Under this circumstance
1Corinthians9:19 And this has a real advantage: I am not bound to obey anyone just because he pays my salary; yet I have freely and happily become a servant of any and all so that I can win them to Christ.
1Corinthians9:20 When I am with the Jews I seem as one of them so that they will listen to the Gospel and I can win them to Christ. When I am with Gentiles who follow Jewish customs and ceremonies I don't argue
1Corinthians9:21 When with the heathen I agree with them as much as I can
1Corinthians9:22 When I am with those whose consciences bother them easily
1Corinthians9:23 I do this to get the Gospel to them and also for the blessing I myself receive when I see them come to Christ.
1Corinthians9:24 In a race everyone runs
1Corinthians9:25 To win the contest you must deny yourselves many things that would keep you from doing your best. An athlete goes to all this trouble just to win a blue ribbon or a silver cup
1Corinthians9:26 So I run straight to the goal with purpose in every step. I fight to win. I'm not just shadow-boxing or playing around.
1Corinthians9:27 Like an athlete I punish my body
1Corinthians10:1 For we must never forget
1Corinthians10:2 This might be called their “baptism“—baptized both in sea and cloud!—as followers of Moses—their commitment to him as their leader.
1Corinthians10:3 And by a miracle God sent them food to eat and water to drink there in the desert;
1Corinthians10:4 they drank the water that Christ gave them. He was there with them as a mighty Rock of spiritual refreshment.
1Corinthians10:5 Yet after all this most of them did not obey God
1Corinthians10:6 From this lesson we are warned that we must not desire evil things as they did
1Corinthians10:7 nor worship idols as they did. (The Scriptures tell us
1Corinthians10:8 Another lesson for us is what happened when some of them sinned with other men's wives
1Corinthians10:9 And don't try the Lord's patience—they did and died from snake bites.
1Corinthians10:10 And don't murmur against God and his dealings with you as some of them did
1Corinthians10:11 All these things happened to them as examples—as object lessons to us—to warn us against doing the same things; they were written down so that we could read about them and learn from them in these last days as the world nears its end.
1Corinthians10:12 So be careful. If you are thinking
1Corinthians10:13 But remember this—the wrong desires that come into your life aren't anything new and different. Many others have faced exactly the same problems before you. And no temptation is irresistible. You can trust God to keep the temptation from becoming so strong that you can't stand up against it
1Corinthians10:14 So
1Corinthians10:15 You are intelligent people. Look now and see for yourselves whether what I am about to say is true.
1Corinthians10:16 When we ask the Lord's blessing upon our drinking from the cup of wine at the Lord's Table
1Corinthians10:17 No matter how many of us there are
1Corinthians10:18 And the Jewish people
1Corinthians10:19 What am I trying to say? Am I saying that the idols to whom the heathen bring sacrifices are really alive and are real gods
1Corinthians10:20 What I am saying is that those who offer food to these idols are united together in sacrificing to demons
1Corinthians10:21 You cannot drink from the cup at the Lord's Table and at Satan's table
1Corinthians10:22 What? Are you tempting the Lord to be angry with you? Are you stronger than he is?
1Corinthians10:23 You are certainly free to eat food offered to idols if you want to; it's not against God's laws to eat such meat
1Corinthians10:24 Don't think only of yourself. Try to think of the other fellow
1Corinthians10:25 Here's what you should do. Take any meat you want that is sold at the market. Don't ask whether or not it was offered to idols
1Corinthians10:26 For the earth and every good thing in it belongs to the Lord and is yours to enjoy.
1Corinthians10:27 If someone who isn't a Christian asks you out to dinner
1Corinthians10:28 But if someone warns you that this meat has been offered to idols
1Corinthians10:29 In this case his feeling about it is the important thing
1Corinthians10:30 If I can thank God for the food and enjoy it
1Corinthians10:31 Well
1Corinthians10:32 So don't be a stumbling block to anyone
1Corinthians10:33 That is the plan I follow

1Corinthians11:2 I am so glad

1Corinthians11:4 That is why
1Corinthians11:5 And that is why a woman who publicly prays or prophesies without a covering on her head dishonors her husband for her covering is a sign of her subjection to him.
1Corinthians11:6 Yes
1Corinthians11:7 But a man should not wear anything on his head when worshiping
1Corinthians11:8 The first man didn't come from woman
1Corinthians11:9 And Adam
1Corinthians11:10 So a woman should wear a covering on her head as a sign that she is under man's authority
1Corinthians11:11 But remember that in God's plan men and women need each other.
1Corinthians11:12 For although the first woman came out of man
1Corinthians11:13 What do you yourselves really think about this? Is it right for a woman to pray in public without covering her head?
1Corinthians11:14 Doesn't even instinct itself teach us that women's heads should be covered?
1Corinthians11:15 For women are proud of their long hair
1Corinthians11:16 But if anyone wants to argue about this
1Corinthians11:17 Next on my list of items to write you about is something else I cannot agree with. For it sounds as if more harm than good is done when you meet together for your communion services.
1Corinthians11:18 Everyone keeps telling me about the arguing that goes on in these meetings
1Corinthians11:19 But I suppose you feel this is necessary so that you who are always right will become known and recognized!
1Corinthians11:20 When you come together to eat
1Corinthians11:21 but your own. For I am told that everyone hastily gobbles all the food he can without waiting to share with the others
1Corinthians11:22 What? Is this really true? Can't you do your eating and drinking at home to avoid disgracing the church and shaming those who are poor and can bring no food? What am I supposed to say about these things? Do you want me to praise you? Well
1Corinthians11:23 For this is what the Lord himself has said about his Table
1Corinthians11:24 and when he had given thanks to God for it
1Corinthians11:25 In the same way
1Corinthians11:26 For every time you eat this bread and drink this cup you are retelling the message of the Lord's death
1Corinthians11:27 So if anyone eats this bread and drinks from this cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner
1Corinthians11:28 That is why a man should examine himself carefully before eating the bread and drinking from the cup.
1Corinthians11:29 For if he eats the bread and drinks from the cup unworthily
1Corinthians11:30 That is why many of you are weak and sick
1Corinthians11:31 But if you carefully examine yourselves before eating you will not need to be judged and punished.
1Corinthians11:32 Yet
1Corinthians11:33 So
1Corinthians11:34 if anyone is really hungry he should eat at home so that he won't bring punishment upon himself when you meet together. I'll talk to you about the other matters after I arrive.
1Corinthians12:1 And now
1Corinthians12:2 You will remember that before you became Christians you went around from one idol to another
1Corinthians12:3 But now you are meeting people who claim to speak messages from the Spirit of God. How can you know whether they are really inspired by God or whether they are fakes? Here is the test: no one speaking by the power of the Spirit of God can curse Jesus
1Corinthians12:4 Now God gives us many kinds of special abilities
1Corinthians12:5 There are different kinds of service to God
1Corinthians12:6 There are many ways in which God works in our lives
1Corinthians12:7 The Holy Spirit displays God's power through each of us as a means of helping the entire church.
1Corinthians12:8 To one person the Spirit gives the ability to give wise advice; someone else may be especially good at studying and teaching
1Corinthians12:9 He gives special faith to another
1Corinthians12:10 He gives power for doing miracles to some
1Corinthians12:11 It is the same and only Holy Spirit who gives all these gifts and powers
1Corinthians12:12 Our bodies have many parts
1Corinthians12:13 Each of us is a part of the one body of Christ. Some of us are Jews
1Corinthians12:14 Yes
1Corinthians12:15 If the foot says
1Corinthians12:16 And what would you think if you heard an ear say
1Corinthians12:17 Suppose the whole body were an eye—then how would you hear? Or if your whole body were just one big ear
1Corinthians12:18 But that isn't the way God has made us. He has made many parts for our bodies and has put each part just where he wants it.
1Corinthians12:19 What a strange thing a body would be if it had only one part!
1Corinthians12:20 So he has made many parts
1Corinthians12:21 The eye can never say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 And some of the parts that seem weakest and least important are really the most necessary.
1Corinthians12:23 Yes
1Corinthians12:24 while of course the parts that may be seen do not require this special care. So God has put the body together in such a way that extra honor and care are given to those parts that might otherwise seem less important.
1Corinthians12:25 This makes for happiness among the parts
1Corinthians12:26 If one part suffers
1Corinthians12:27 Now here is what I am trying to say: All of you together are the one body of Christ
1Corinthians12:28 Here is a list of some of the parts he has placed in his Church
1Corinthians12:29 Is everyone an apostle? Of course not. Is everyone a preacher? No. Are all teachers? Does everyone have the power to do miracles?
1Corinthians12:30 Can everyone heal the sick? Of course not. Does God give all of us the ability to speak in languages we've never learned? Can just anyone understand and translate what those are saying who have that gift of foreign speech?
1Corinthians12:31 No
1Corinthians13:1 If I had the gift of being able to speak in other languages without learning them and could speak in every language there is in all of heaven and earth
1Corinthians13:2 If I had the gift of prophecy and knew all about what is going to happen in the future
1Corinthians13:3 If I gave everything I have to poor people
1Corinthians13:4 Love is very patient and kind
1Corinthians13:5 never haughty or selfish or rude. Love does not demand its own way. It is not irritable or touchy. It does not hold grudges and will hardly even notice when others do it wrong.
1Corinthians13:6 It is never glad about injustice
1Corinthians13:7 If you love someone
1Corinthians13:8 All the special gifts and powers from God will someday come to an end
1Corinthians13:9 Now we know so little
1Corinthians13:10 But when we have been made perfect and complete

1Corinthians15:1 Now let me remind you
1Corinthians15:2 and it is this Good News that saves you if you still firmly believe it
1Corinthians15:3 I passed on to you right from the first what had been told to me
1Corinthians15:4 and that he was buried
1Corinthians15:5 He was seen by Peter and later by the rest of “the Twelve.“
1Corinthians15:6 After that he was seen by more than five hundred Christian brothers at one time
1Corinthians15:7 Then James saw him
1Corinthians15:8 Last of all I saw him too
1Corinthians15:9 For I am the least worthy of all the apostles
1Corinthians15:10 But whatever I am now it is all because God poured out such kindness and grace upon me—and not without results: for I have worked harder than all the other apostles
1Corinthians15:11 It makes no difference who worked the hardest
1Corinthians15:12 But tell me this! Since you believe what we preach
1Corinthians15:13 For if there is no resurrection of the dead
1Corinthians15:14 And if he is still dead
1Corinthians15:15 and we apostles are all liars because we have said that God raised Christ from the grave
1Corinthians15:16 If they don't
1Corinthians15:17 and you are very foolish to keep on trusting God to save you
1Corinthians15:18 in that case
1Corinthians15:19 And if being a Christian is of value to us only now in this life
1Corinthians15:20 But the fact is that Christ did actually rise from the dead and has become the first of millions who will come back to life again someday.
1Corinthians15:21 Death came into the world because of what one man (Adam) did
1Corinthians15:22 Everyone dies because all of us are related to Adam
1Corinthians15:23 Each
1Corinthians15:24 After that the end will come when he will turn the Kingdom over to God the Father
1Corinthians15:25 For Christ will be King until he has defeated all his enemies
1Corinthians15:26 including the last enemy—death. This too must be defeated and ended.
1Corinthians15:27 For the rule and authority over all things has been given to Christ by his Father; except
1Corinthians15:28 When Christ has finally won the battle against all his enemies
1Corinthians15:29 If the dead will not come back to life again
1Corinthians15:30 And why should we ourselves be continually risking our lives
1Corinthians15:31 For it is a fact that I face death daily; that is as true as my pride in your growth in the Lord.
1Corinthians15:32 And what value was there in fighting wild beasts—those men of Ephesus—if it was only for what I gain in this life down here? If we will never live again after we die
1Corinthians15:33 Don't be fooled by those who say such things. If you listen to them you will start acting like them.
1Corinthians15:34 Get some sense and quit your sinning. For to your shame I say it; some of you are not even Christians at all and have never really known God.
1Corinthians15:35 But someone may ask
1Corinthians15:36 What a foolish question! You will find the answer in your own garden! When you put a seed into the ground it doesn't grow into a plant unless it “dies“ first.
1Corinthians15:37 And when the green shoot comes up out of the seed
1Corinthians15:38 then God gives it a beautiful new body—just the kind he wants it to have; a different kind of plant grows from each kind of seed.
1Corinthians15:39 And just as there are different kinds of seeds and plants
1Corinthians15:40 The angels in heaven have bodies far different from ours
1Corinthians15:41 The sun has one kind of glory while the moon and stars have another kind. And the stars differ from each other in their beauty and brightness.
1Corinthians15:42 In the same way
1Corinthians15:43 The bodies we have now embarrass us
1Corinthians15:44 They are just human bodies at death
1Corinthians15:45 The Scriptures tell us that the first man
1Corinthians15:46 First
1Corinthians15:47 Adam was made from the dust of the earth
1Corinthians15:48 Every human being has a body just like Adam's
1Corinthians15:49 Just as each of us now has a body like Adam's
1Corinthians15:50 I tell you this
1Corinthians15:51 But I am telling you this strange and wonderful secret: we shall not all die
1Corinthians15:52 It will all happen in a moment
1Corinthians15:53 For our earthly bodies
1Corinthians15:54 When this happens
1Corinthians15:55 O death
1Corinthians15:56 For sin—the sting that causes death—will all be gone; and the law
1Corinthians15:57 How we thank God for all of this! It is he who makes us victorious through Jesus Christ our Lord!
1Corinthians15:58 So
