2Corinthians1:3 What a wonderful God we have—he is the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ
2Corinthians1:4 and the one who so wonderfully comforts and strengthens us in our hardships and trials. And why does he do this? So that when others are troubled
2Corinthians1:5 You can be sure that the more we undergo sufferings for Christ
2Corinthians1:6 We are in deep trouble for bringing you God's comfort and salvation. But in our trouble God has comforted us—and this
2Corinthians1:7 to show you from our personal experience how God will tenderly comfort you when you undergo these same sufferings. He will give you the strength to endure.
2Corinthians1:8 I think you ought to know
2Corinthians1:9 We felt we were doomed to die and saw how powerless we were to help ourselves; but that was good
2Corinthians1:10 And he did help us and saved us from a terrible death; yes
2Corinthians1:11 But you must help us too by praying for us. For much thanks and praise will go to God from you who see his wonderful answers to your prayers for our safety!
2Corinthians1:12 We are so glad that we can say with utter honesty that in all our dealings we have been pure and sincere
2Corinthians1:13 My letters have been straightforward and sincere; nothing is written between the lines! And even though you don't know me very well (I hope someday you will)
2Corinthians1:14 I want you to try to accept me and be proud of me as you already are to some extent; just as I shall be of you on that day when our Lord Jesus comes back again.

2Corinthians1:20 He carries out and fulfills all of God's promises
2Corinthians1:21 It is this God who has made you and me into faithful Christians and commissioned us apostles to preach the Good News.
2Corinthians1:22 He has put his brand upon us—his mark of ownership—and given us his Holy Spirit in our hearts as guarantee that we belong to him and as the first installment of all that he is going to give us.
2Corinthians1:23 I call upon this God to witness against me if I am not telling the absolute truth: the reason I haven't come to visit you yet is that I don't want to sadden you with a severe rebuke.

Galatians1:1 From: Paul the missionary and all the other Christians here.
Galatians1:2 To: The churches of Galatia. I was not called to be a missionary by any group or agency. My call is from Jesus Christ himself and from God the Father who raised him from the dead.

Galatians1:4 He died for our sins just as God our Father planned
Galatians1:5 All glory to God through all the ages of eternity. Amen.

Ephesians6:7 with gladness all the time
Ephesians6:8 Remember

Ephesians6:11 Put on all of God's armor so that you will be able to stand safe against all strategies and tricks of Satan.
Ephesians6:12 For we are not fighting against people made of flesh and blood
Ephesians6:13 So use every piece of God's armor to resist the enemy whenever he attacks
Ephesians6:14 But to do this
Ephesians6:15 Wear shoes that are able to speed you on as you preach the Good News of peace with God.
Ephesians6:16 In every battle you will need faith as your shield to stop the fiery arrows aimed at you by Satan.
Ephesians6:17 And you will need the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit—which is the Word of God.

Colossians1:1 From: Paul
Colossians1:2 To: The faithful Christian brothers—God's people—in the city of Colosse. May God our Father shower you with blessings and fill you with his great peace.
Colossians1:3 Whenever we pray for you
Colossians1:4 for we have heard how much you trust the Lord
Colossians1:5 And you are looking forward to the joys of heaven
Colossians1:6 The same Good News that came to you is going out all over the world and changing lives everywhere
Colossians1:7 Epaphras
Colossians1:8 And he is the one who has told us about the great love for others that the Holy Spirit has given you.
Colossians1:9 So ever since we first heard about you we have kept on praying and asking God to help you understand what he wants you to do; asking him to make you wise about spiritual things;
Colossians1:10 and asking that the way you live will always please the Lord and honor him
Colossians1:11 We are praying
Colossians1:12 and always thankful to the Father who has made us fit to share all the wonderful things that belong to those who live in the Kingdom of light.
Colossians1:13 For he has rescued us out of the darkness and gloom of Satan's kingdom and brought us into the Kingdom of his dear Son
Colossians1:14 who bought our freedom with his blood and forgave us all our sins.
Colossians1:15 Christ is the exact likeness of the unseen God. He existed before God made anything at all
Colossians1:16 Christ himself is the Creator who made everything in heaven and earth
Colossians1:17 He was before all else began and it is his power that holds everything together.
Colossians1:18 He is the Head of the body made up of his people—that is
Colossians1:19 for God wanted all of himself to be in his Son.
Colossians1:20 It was through what his Son did that God cleared a path for everything to come to him—all things in heaven and on earth—for Christ's death on the cross has made peace with God for all by his blood.
Colossians1:21 This includes you who were once so far away from God. You were his enemies and hated him and were separated from him by your evil thoughts and actions
Colossians1:22 He has done this through the death on the cross of his own human body
Colossians1:23 the only condition is that you fully believe the Truth
Colossians1:24 But part of my work is to suffer for you; and I am glad
Colossians1:25 God has sent me to help his Church and to tell his secret plan to you Gentiles.
Colossians1:26 He has kept this secret for centuries and generations past
Colossians1:27 and the riches and glory of his plan are for you Gentiles
Colossians1:28 So everywhere we go we talk about Christ to all who will listen
Colossians1:29 This is my work

Colossians3:14 Most of all

1Timothy1:1 From: Paul
1Timothy1:2 To: Timothy. Timothy
1Timothy1:3 As I said when I left for Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 Put an end to their myths and fables
1Timothy1:5 What I am eager for is that all the Christians there will be filled with love that comes from pure hearts
1Timothy1:6 But these teachers have missed this whole idea and spend their time arguing and talking foolishness.
1Timothy1:7 They want to become famous as teachers of the laws of Moses when they haven't the slightest idea what those laws really show us.
1Timothy1:8 Those laws are good when used as God intended.
1Timothy1:9 But they were not made for us
1Timothy1:10 Yes
1Timothy1:11 the glorious Good News of our blessed God
1Timothy1:12 How thankful I am to Christ Jesus our Lord for choosing me as one of his messengers
1Timothy1:13 even though I used to scoff at the name of Christ. I hunted down his people
1Timothy1:14 Oh
1Timothy1:15 How true it is
1Timothy1:16 But God had mercy on me so that Christ Jesus could use me as an example to show everyone how patient he is with even the worst sinners
1Timothy1:17 Glory and honor to God forever and ever. He is the King of the ages
1Timothy1:18 Now
1Timothy1:19 Cling tightly to your faith in Christ and always keep your conscience clear
1Timothy1:20 Hymenaeus and Alexander are two examples of this. I had to give them over to Satan to punish them until they could learn not to bring shame to the name of Christ.
1Timothy2:1 Here are my directions: Pray much for others; plead for God's mercy upon them; give thanks for all he is going to do for them.
1Timothy2:2 Pray in this way for kings and all others who are in authority over us
1Timothy2:3 This is good and pleases God our Savior
1Timothy2:4 for he longs for all to be saved and to understand this truth:
1Timothy2:5 That God is on one side and all the people on the other side
1Timothy2:6 by giving his life for all mankind. This is the message that at the proper time God gave to the world.
1Timothy2:7 And I have been chosen—this is the absolute truth—as God's minister and missionary to teach this truth to the Gentiles and to show them God's plan of salvation through faith.
1Timothy2:8 So I want men everywhere to pray with holy hands lifted up to God
1Timothy2:9 And the women should be the same way
1Timothy2:10 Christian women should be noticed for being kind and good
1Timothy2:11 Women should listen and learn quietly and humbly.
1Timothy2:12 I never let women teach men or lord it over them. Let them be silent in your church meetings.
1Timothy2:13 Why? Because God made Adam first
1Timothy2:14 And it was not Adam who was fooled by Satan
1Timothy2:15 So God sent pain and suffering to women when their children are born
1Timothy3:1 It is a true saying that if a man wants to be a pastor he has a good ambition.
1Timothy3:2 For a pastor must be a good man whose life cannot be spoken against. He must have only one wife
1Timothy3:3 He must not be a drinker or quarrelsome
1Timothy3:4 He must have a well-behaved family
1Timothy3:5 For if a man can't make his own little family behave
1Timothy3:6 The pastor must not be a new Christian because he might be proud of being chosen so soon
1Timothy3:7 Also
1Timothy3:8 The deacons must be the same sort of good
1Timothy3:9 They must be earnest
1Timothy3:10 Before they are asked to be deacons
1Timothy3:11 Their wives must be thoughtful
1Timothy3:12 Deacons should have only one wife
1Timothy3:13 Those who do well as deacons will be well rewarded both by respect from others and also by developing their own confidence and bold trust in the Lord.
1Timothy3:14 I am writing these things to you now
1Timothy3:15 so that if I don't come for awhile
1Timothy3:16 It is quite true that the way to live a godly life is not an easy matter. But the answer lies in Christ
1Timothy4:1 But the Holy Spirit tells us clearly that in the last times some in the church will turn away from Christ and become eager followers of teachers with devil-inspired ideas.
1Timothy4:2 These teachers will tell lies with straight faces and do it so often that their consciences won't even bother them.
1Timothy4:3 They will say it is wrong to be married and wrong to eat meat
1Timothy4:4 For everything God made is good
1Timothy4:5 and if we ask God to bless it
1Timothy4:6 If you explain this to the others you will be doing your duty as a worthy pastor who is fed by faith and by the true teaching you have followed.
1Timothy4:7 Don't waste time arguing over foolish ideas and silly myths and legends. Spend your time and energy in the exercise of keeping spiritually fit.
1Timothy4:8 Bodily exercise is all right
1Timothy4:9 This is the truth and everyone should accept it.
1Timothy4:10 We work hard and suffer much in order that people will believe it
1Timothy4:11 Teach these things and make sure everyone learns them well.
1Timothy4:12 Don't let anyone think little of you because you are young. Be their ideal; let them follow the way you teach and live; be a pattern for them in your love
1Timothy4:13 Until I get there
1Timothy4:14 Be sure to use the abilities God has given you through his prophets when the elders of the church laid their hands upon your head.
1Timothy4:15 Put these abilities to work; throw yourself into your tasks so that everyone may notice your improvement and progress.
1Timothy4:16 Keep a close watch on all you do and think. Stay true to what is right and God will bless you and use you to help others.
1Timothy5:1 Never speak sharply to an older man
1Timothy5:2 Treat the older women as mothers
1Timothy5:3 The church should take loving care of women whose husbands have died if they don't have anyone else to help them.
1Timothy5:4 But if they have children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 The church should care for widows who are poor and alone in the world if they are looking to God for his help and spending much time in prayer;
1Timothy5:6 but not if they are spending their time running around gossiping
1Timothy5:7 This should be your church rule so that the Christians will know and do what is right.
1Timothy5:8 But anyone who won't care for his own relatives when they need help
1Timothy5:9 A widow who wants to become one of the special church workers should be at least sixty years old and have been married only once.
1Timothy5:10 She must be well thought of by everyone because of the good she has done. Has she brought up her children well? Has she been kind to strangers as well as to other Christians? Has she helped those who are sick and hurt? Is she always ready to show kindness?
1Timothy5:11 The younger widows should not become members of this special group because after awhile they are likely to disregard their vow to Christ and marry again.
1Timothy5:12 And so they will stand condemned because they broke their first promise.
1Timothy5:13 Besides
1Timothy5:14 So I think it is better for these younger widows to marry again and have children and take care of their own homes; then no one will be able to say anything against them.
1Timothy5:15 For I am afraid that some of them have already turned away from the church and been led astray by Satan.
1Timothy5:16 Let me remind you again that a widow's relatives must take care of her and not leave this to the church to do. Then the church can spend its money for the care of widows who are all alone and have nowhere else to turn.
1Timothy5:17 Pastors who do their work well should be paid well and should be highly appreciated
1Timothy5:18 For the Scriptures say
1Timothy5:19 Don't listen to complaints against the pastor unless there are two or three witnesses to accuse him.
1Timothy5:20 If he has really sinned
1Timothy5:21 I solemnly command you in the presence of God and the Lord Jesus Christ and of the holy angels to do this whether the pastor is a special friend of yours or not. All must be treated exactly the same.
1Timothy5:22 Never be in a hurry about choosing a pastor; you may overlook his sins
1Timothy5:23 (By the way
1Timothy5:24 Remember that some men
1Timothy5:25 In the same way
1Timothy6:1 Christian slaves should work hard for their owners and respect them; never let it be said that Christ's people are poor workers. Don't let the name of God or his teaching be laughed at because of this.
1Timothy6:2 If their owner is a Christian
1Timothy6:3 Some may deny these things
1Timothy6:4 Anyone who says anything different is both proud and stupid. He is quibbling over the meaning of Christ's words and stirring up arguments ending in jealousy and anger
1Timothy6:5 These arguers—their minds warped by sin—don't know how to tell the truth; to them the Good News is just a means of making money. Keep away from them.
1Timothy6:6 Do you want to be truly rich? You already are if you are happy and good.
1Timothy6:7 After all
1Timothy6:8 So we should be well satisfied without money if we have enough food and clothing.
1Timothy6:9 But people who long to be rich soon begin to do all kinds of wrong things to get money
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is the first step toward all kinds of sin. Some people have even turned away from God because of their love for it
1Timothy6:11 O Timothy
1Timothy6:12 Fight on for God. Hold tightly to the eternal life that God has given you and that you have confessed with such a ringing confession before many witnesses.
1Timothy6:13 I command you before God
1Timothy6:14 that you fulfill all he has told you to do so that no one can find fault with you from now until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
1Timothy6:15 For in due season Christ will be revealed from heaven by the blessed and only Almighty God
1Timothy6:16 who alone can never die
1Timothy6:17 Tell those who are rich not to be proud and not to trust in their money
1Timothy6:18 Tell them to use their money to do good. They should be rich in good works and should give happily to those in need
1Timothy6:19 By doing this they will be storing up real treasure for themselves in heaven—it is the only safe investment for eternity! And they will be living a fruitful Christian life down here as well.
1Timothy6:20 Oh
1Timothy6:21 Some of these people have missed the most important thing in life—they don't know God. May God's mercy be upon you. Sincerely
2Timothy1:1 From: Paul
2Timothy1:2 To: Timothy
2Timothy1:3 How I thank God for you
2Timothy1:4 How I long to see you again. How happy I would be
2Timothy1:5 I know how much you trust the Lord
2Timothy1:6 This being so
2Timothy1:7 For the Holy Spirit
2Timothy1:8 If you will stir up this inner power
2Timothy1:9 It is he who saved us and chose us for his holy work not because we deserved it but because that was his plan long before the world began—to show his love and kindness to us through Christ.
2Timothy1:10 And now he has made all of this plain to us by the coming of our Savior Jesus Christ
2Timothy1:11 And God has chosen me to be his missionary
2Timothy1:12 That is why I am suffering here in jail
2Timothy1:13 Hold tightly to the pattern of truth I taught you
2Timothy1:14 Guard well the splendid
2Timothy1:15 As you know
2Timothy1:16 May the Lord bless Onesiphorus and all his family because he visited me and encouraged me often. His visits revived me like a breath of fresh air
2Timothy1:17 In fact
2Timothy1:18 May the Lord give him a special blessing at the day of Christ's return. And you know better than I can tell you how much he helped me at Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 O Timothy
2Timothy2:2 For you must teach others those things you and many others have heard me speak about. Teach these great truths to trustworthy men who will
2Timothy2:3 Take your share of suffering as a good soldier of Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:4 and as Christ's soldier
2Timothy2:5 Follow the Lord's rules for doing his work
2Timothy2:6 Work hard like a farmer who gets paid well if he raises a large crop.
2Timothy2:7 Think over these three illustrations
2Timothy2:8 Don't ever forget the wonderful fact that Jesus Christ was a man
2Timothy2:9 It is because I have preached these great truths that I am in trouble here and have been put in jail like a criminal. But the Word of God is not chained
2Timothy2:10 I am more than willing to suffer if that will bring salvation and eternal glory in Christ Jesus to those God has chosen.
2Timothy2:11 I am comforted by this truth
2Timothy2:12 And if we think that our present service for him is hard
2Timothy2:13 Even when we are too weak to have any faith left
2Timothy2:14 Remind your people of these great facts
2Timothy2:15 Work hard so God can say to you
2Timothy2:16 Steer clear of foolish discussions that lead people into the sin of anger with each other.
2Timothy2:17 Things will be said that will burn and hurt for a long time to come. Hymenaeus and Philetus
2Timothy2:18 They have left the path of truth
2Timothy2:19 But God's truth stands firm like a great rock
2Timothy2:20 In a wealthy home there are dishes made of gold and silver as well as some made from wood and clay. The expensive dishes are used for guests
2Timothy2:21 If you stay away from sin you will be like one of these dishes made of purest gold—the very best in the house—so that Christ himself can use you for his highest purposes.
2Timothy2:22 Run from anything that gives you the evil thoughts that young men often have
2Timothy2:23 Again I say
2Timothy2:24 God's people must not be quarrelsome; they must be gentle
2Timothy2:25 Be humble when you are trying to teach those who are mixed up concerning the truth. For if you talk meekly and courteously to them
2Timothy2:26 Then they will come to their senses and escape from Satan's trap of slavery to sin
2Timothy3:1 You may as well know this too
2Timothy3:2 For people will love only themselves and their money; they will be proud and boastful
2Timothy3:3 They will be hardheaded and never give in to others; they will be constant liars and troublemakers and will think nothing of immorality. They will be rough and cruel
2Timothy3:4 They will betray their friends; they will be hotheaded
2Timothy3:5 They will go to church
2Timothy3:6 They are the kind who craftily sneak into other people's homes and make friendships with silly
2Timothy3:7 Women of that kind are forever following new teachers
2Timothy3:8 And these teachers fight truth just as Jannes and Jambres fought against Moses. They have dirty minds
2Timothy3:9 But they won't get away with all this forever. Someday their deceit will be well known to everyone
2Timothy3:10 But you know from watching me that I am not that kind of person. You know what I believe and the way I live and what I want. You know my faith in Christ and how I have suffered. You know my love for you
2Timothy3:11 You know how many troubles I have had as a result of my preaching the Good News. You know about all that was done to me while I was visiting in Antioch
2Timothy3:12 Yes
2Timothy3:13 In fact
2Timothy3:14 But you must keep on believing the things you have been taught. You know they are true
2Timothy3:15 You know how
2Timothy3:16 The whole Bible was given to us by inspiration from God and is useful to teach us what is true and to make us realize what is wrong in our lives; it straightens us out and helps us do what is right.
2Timothy3:17 It is God's way of making us well prepared at every point
2Timothy4:1 And so I solemnly urge you before God and before Christ Jesus—who will someday judge the living and the dead when he appears to set up his Kingdom—
2Timothy4:2 to preach the Word of God urgently at all times
2Timothy4:3 For there is going to come a time when people won't listen to the truth but will go around looking for teachers who will tell them just what they want to hear.
2Timothy4:4 They won't listen to what the Bible says but will blithely follow their own misguided ideas.
2Timothy4:5 Stand steady
2Timothy4:6 I say this because I won't be around to help you very much longer. My time has almost run out. Very soon now I will be on my way to heaven.
2Timothy4:7 I have fought long and hard for my Lord
2Timothy4:8 In heaven a crown is waiting for me
2Timothy4:9 Please come as soon as you can
2Timothy4:10 for Demas has left me. He loved the good things of this life and went to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia
2Timothy4:11 Only Luke is with me. Bring Mark with you when you come
2Timothy4:12 (Tychicus is gone too
2Timothy4:13 When you come
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the coppersmith has done me much harm. The Lord will punish him
2Timothy4:15 but be careful of him
2Timothy4:16 The first time I was brought before the judge
2Timothy4:17 But the Lord stood with me and gave me the opportunity to boldly preach a whole sermon for all the world to hear. And he saved me from being thrown to the lions.
2Timothy4:18 Yes
2Timothy4:19 Please say hello for me to Priscilla and Aquila and those living at the home of Onesiphorus.
2Timothy4:20 Erastus stayed at Corinth
2Timothy4:21 Do try to be here before winter. Eubulus sends you greetings
2Timothy4:22 May the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Farewell
Titus1:1 From: Paul
Titus1:2 the kind of truth that changes lives—so that they can have eternal life
Titus1:3 And now in his own good time he has revealed this Good News and permits me to tell it to everyone. By command of God our Savior
Titus1:4 To: Titus
Titus1:5 I left you there on the island of Crete so that you could do whatever was needed to help strengthen each of its churches
Titus1:6 The men you choose must be well thought of for their good lives; they must have only one wife and their children must love the Lord and not have a reputation for being wild or disobedient to their parents.
Titus1:7 These pastors must be men of blameless lives because they are God's ministers. They must not be proud or impatient; they must not be drunkards or fighters or greedy for money.
Titus1:8 They must enjoy having guests in their homes and must love all that is good. They must be sensible men
Titus1:9 Their belief in the truth that they have been taught must be strong and steadfast so that they will be able to teach it to others and show those who disagree with them where they are wrong.
Titus1:10 For there are many who refuse to obey; this is especially true among those who say that all Christians must obey the Jewish laws. But this is foolish talk; it blinds people to the truth
Titus1:11 and it must be stopped. Already whole families have been turned away from the grace of God. Such teachers are only after your money.
Titus1:12 One of their own men
Titus1:13 And this is true. So speak to the Christians there as sternly as necessary to make them strong in the faith
Titus1:14 and to stop them from listening to Jewish folk tales and the demands of men who have turned their backs on the truth.
Titus1:15 A person who is pure of heart sees goodness and purity in everything; but a person whose own heart is evil and untrusting finds evil in everything
Titus1:16 Such persons claim they know God
Titus2:1 But as for you
Titus2:2 Teach the older men to be serious and unruffled; they must be sensible
Titus2:3 Teach the older women to be quiet and respectful in everything they do. They must not go around speaking evil of others and must not be heavy drinkers
Titus2:4 These older women must train the younger women to live quietly
Titus2:5 and to be sensible and clean minded
Titus2:6 In the same way
Titus2:7 And here you yourself must be an example to them of good deeds of every kind. Let everything you do reflect your love of the truth and the fact that you are in dead earnest about it.
Titus2:8 Your conversation should be so sensible and logical that anyone who wants to argue will be ashamed of himself because there won't be anything to criticize in anything you say!
Titus2:9 Urge slaves to obey their masters and to try their best to satisfy them. They must not talk back
Titus2:10 nor steal
Titus2:11 For the free gift of eternal salvation is now being offered to everyone;
Titus2:12 and along with this gift comes the realization that God wants us to turn from godless living and sinful pleasures and to live good
Titus2:13 looking forward to that wonderful time we've been expecting
Titus2:14 He died under God's judgment against our sins so that he could rescue us from constant falling into sin and make us his very own people
Titus2:15 You must teach these things and encourage your people to do them
Titus3:1 Remind your people to obey the government and its officers
Titus3:2 They must not speak evil of anyone
Titus3:3 Once we
Titus3:4 But when the time came for the kindness and love of God our Savior to appear
Titus3:5 then he saved us—not because we were good enough to be saved but because of his kindness and pity—by washing away our sins and giving us the new joy of the indwelling Holy Spirit
Titus3:6 whom he poured out upon us with wonderful fullness—and all because of what Jesus Christ our Savior did
Titus3:7 so that he could declare us good in God's eyes—all because of his great kindness; and now we can share in the wealth of the eternal life he gives us
Titus3:8 These things I have told you are all true. Insist on them so that Christians will be careful to do good deeds all the time
Titus3:9 Don't get involved in arguing over unanswerable questions and controversial theological ideas; keep out of arguments and quarrels about obedience to Jewish laws
Titus3:10 If anyone is causing divisions among you
Titus3:11 for such a person has a wrong sense of values. He is sinning
Titus3:12 I am planning to send either Artemas or Tychicus to you. As soon as one of them arrives
Titus3:13 Do everything you can to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos with their trip; see that they are given everything they need.
Titus3:14 For our people must learn to help all who need their assistance
Titus3:15 Everybody here sends greetings. Please say hello to all of the Christian friends there. May God's blessings be with you all. Sincerely
Philemon1:1 From: Paul
Philemon1:2 and to the church that meets in your home
Philemon1:3 May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you his blessings and his peace.
Philemon1:4 I always thank God when I am praying for you
Philemon1:5 because I keep hearing of your love and trust in the Lord Jesus and in his people.
Philemon1:6 And I pray that as you share your faith with others it will grip their lives too
Philemon1:7 I myself have gained much joy and comfort from your love
Philemon1:8 Now I want to ask a favor of you. I could demand it of you in the name of Christ because it is the right thing for you to do
Philemon1:9 but I love you and prefer just to ask you—I
Philemon1:10 My plea is that you show kindness to my child Onesimus
Philemon1:11 Onesimus (whose name means “Useful“) hasn't been of much use to you in the past
Philemon1:12 I am sending him back to you
Philemon1:13 I really wanted to keep him here with me while I am in these chains for preaching the Good News
Philemon1:14 but I didn't want to do it without your consent. I didn't want you to be kind because you had to but because you wanted to.
Philemon1:15 Perhaps you could think of it this way: that he ran away from you for a little while so that now he can be yours forever
Philemon1:16 no longer only a slave
Philemon1:17 If I am really your friend
Philemon1:18 If he has harmed you in any way or stolen anything from you
Philemon1:19 I will pay it back (I
Philemon1:20 Yes
Philemon1:21 I've written you this letter because I am positive that you will do what I ask and even more!
Philemon1:22 Please keep a guest room ready for me
Philemon1:23 Epaphras my fellow prisoner
Philemon1:24 So do Mark
Philemon1:25 The blessings of our Lord Jesus Christ be upon your spirit. Paul
Hebrews1:1 Long ago God spoke in many different ways to our fathers through the prophets in visions
Hebrews1:2 But now in these days he has spoken to us through his Son to whom he has given everything and through whom he made the world and everything there is.
Hebrews1:3 God's Son shines out with God's glory
Hebrews1:4 Thus he became far greater than the angels
Hebrews1:5 For God never said to any angel
Hebrews1:6 And still another time—when his firstborn Son came to earth—God said
Hebrews1:7 God speaks of his angels as messengers swift as the wind and as servants made of flaming fire;
Hebrews1:8 but of his Son he says
Hebrews1:9 You love right and hate wrong; so God
Hebrews1:10 God also called him “Lord“ when he said
Hebrews1:11 They will disappear into nothingness
Hebrews1:12 and some day you will fold them up and replace them. But you yourself will never change
Hebrews1:13 And did God ever say to an angel
Hebrews1:14 No
Hebrews2:1 So we must listen very carefully to the truths we have heard
Hebrews2:2 For since the messages from angels have always proved true and people have always been punished for disobeying them
Hebrews2:3 what makes us think that we can escape if we are indifferent to this great salvation announced by the Lord Jesus himself and passed on to us by those who heard him speak?
Hebrews2:4 God always has shown us that these messages are true by signs and wonders and various miracles and by giving certain special abilities from the Holy Spirit to those who believe; yes
Hebrews2:5 And the future world we are talking about will not be controlled by angels.
Hebrews2:6 No
Hebrews2:7 For though you made him lower than the angels for a little while
Hebrews2:8 And you have put him in complete charge of everything there is. Nothing is left out.“ We have not yet seen all of this take place
Hebrews2:9 but we do see Jesus—who for awhile was a little lower than the angels—crowned now by God with glory and honor because he suffered death for us. Yes
Hebrews2:10 And it was right and proper that God
Hebrews2:11 We who have been made holy by Jesus
Hebrews2:12 For he says in the book of Psalms
Hebrews2:13 At another time he said
Hebrews2:14 Since we
Hebrews2:15 Only in that way could he deliver those who through fear of death have been living all their lives as slaves to constant dread.
Hebrews2:16 We all know he did not come as an angel but as a human being—yes
Hebrews2:17 And it was necessary for Jesus to be like us
Hebrews2:18 For since he himself has now been through suffering and temptation

Hebrews3:2 For Jesus was faithful to God who appointed him High Priest
Hebrews3:3 But Jesus has far more glory than Moses
Hebrews3:4 And many people can build houses
Hebrews3:5 Well
Hebrews3:6 But Christ
Hebrews3:7 And since Christ is so much superior
Hebrews3:8 and not let our hearts become set against him

Hebrews3:10 “But
Hebrews3:11 Then God
Hebrews3:12 Beware then of your own hearts
Hebrews3:13 Speak to each other about these things every day while there is still time so that none of you will become hardened against God
Hebrews3:14 For if we are faithful to the end
Hebrews3:15 But now is the time. Never forget the warning
Hebrews3:16 And who were those people I speak of
Hebrews3:17 And who was it who made God angry for all those forty years? These same people who sinned and as a result died in the wilderness.
Hebrews3:18 And to whom was God speaking when he swore with an oath that they could never go into the land he had promised his people? He was speaking to all those who disobeyed him.
Hebrews3:19 And why couldn't they go in? Because they didn't trust him.
Hebrews4:1 Although God's promise still stands—his promise that all may enter his place of rest—we ought to tremble with fear because some of you may be on the verge of failing to get there after all.
Hebrews4:2 For this wonderful news—the message that God wants to save us—has been given to us just as it was to those who lived in the time of Moses. But it didn't do them any good because they didn't believe it. They didn't mix it with faith.
Hebrews4:3 For only we who believe God can enter into his place of rest. He has said
Hebrews4:4 We know he is ready and waiting because it is written that God rested on the seventh day of creation
Hebrews4:5 Even so they didn't get in
Hebrews4:6 Yet the promise remains and some get in—but not those who had the first chance
Hebrews4:7 But he has set another time for coming in
Hebrews4:8 This new place of rest he is talking about does not mean the land of Israel that Joshua led them into. If that were what God meant
Hebrews4:9 So there is a full complete rest still waiting for the people of God.
Hebrews4:10 Christ has already entered there. He is resting from his work
Hebrews4:11 Let us do our best to go into that place of rest
Hebrews4:12 For whatever God says to us is full of living power: it is sharper than the sharpest dagger
Hebrews4:13 He knows about everyone
Hebrews4:14 But Jesus the Son of God is our great High Priest who has gone to heaven itself to help us; therefore let us never stop trusting him.
Hebrews4:15 This High Priest of ours understands our weaknesses since he had the same temptations we do
Hebrews4:16 So let us come boldly to the very throne of God and stay there to receive his mercy and to find grace to help us in our times of need.
Hebrews5:1 The Jewish high priest is merely a man like anyone else
Hebrews5:2 And because he is a man
Hebrews5:3 for he
Hebrews5:4 Another thing to remember is that no one can be a high priest just because he wants to be. He has to be called by God for this work in the same way God chose Aaron.
Hebrews5:5 That is why Christ did not elect himself to the honor of being High Priest; no
Hebrews5:6 And another time God said to him

Hebrews9:1 Now in that first agreement between God and his people there were rules for worship and there was a sacred tent down here on earth.
Hebrews9:2 Inside this place of worship there were two rooms. The first one contained the golden candlestick and a table with special loaves of holy bread upon it; this part was called the Holy Place.
Hebrews9:3 Then there was a curtain
Hebrews9:4 In that room there were a golden incense-altar and the golden chest

Hebrews9:9 This has an important lesson for us today. For under the old system
Hebrews9:10 For the old system dealt only with certain rituals—what foods to eat and drink

Hebrews9:15 Christ came with this new agreement so that all who are invited may come and have forever all the wonders God has promised them. For Christ died to rescue them from the penalty of the sins they had committed while still under that old system.

Hebrews11:2 Men of God in days of old were famous for their faith.
Hebrews11:3 By faith—by believing God—we know that the world and the stars—in fact
Hebrews11:4 It was by faith that Abel obeyed God and brought an offering that pleased God more than Cain's offering did. God accepted Abel and proved it by accepting his gift; and though Abel is long dead
Hebrews11:5 Enoch trusted God too
Hebrews11:6 You can never please God without faith

Hebrews11:8 Abraham trusted God
Hebrews11:9 And even when he reached God's promised land

Hebrews11:18 yes

Hebrews11:28 And it was because he believed God would save his people that he commanded them to kill a lamb as God had told them to and sprinkle the blood on the doorposts of their homes so that God's terrible Angel of Death could not touch the oldest child in those homes as he did among the Egyptians.
Hebrews11:29 The people of Israel trusted God and went right through the Red Sea as though they were on dry ground. But when the Egyptians chasing them tried it
Hebrews11:30 It was faith that brought the walls of Jericho tumbling down after the people of Israel had walked around them seven days as God had commanded them.
